        {x |
          вҲғ A,
            вҲғ (_ : A вҠҶ Finset.Icc 1 N) (_ :
              вҲғ Оҙ, вҲ‘ n вҲҲ A, вҶ‘вҶ‘(Оҙ n) / вҶ‘n = 0 вҲ§ вҲҖ A' вҠӮ A, A'.Nonempty вҶ’ вҲ‘ n вҲҲ A', вҶ‘вҶ‘(Оҙ n) / вҶ‘n вү  0), вҶ‘A.card = x}
        (c N)) вҶ’
    c =O[Filter.atTop] sorry
problems.docstring:
Let $c(N)$ be the size of the largest $A\subseteq\{1, \dots, N\}$ such that there is a function
$\delta : A \to \{-1, 1\}$ such that
$$
  \sum_{n\in A} \frac{\delta n}{n} = 0
$$
and
$$
  \sum_{n\in A'}\frac{\delta n}{n} \neq 0
$$
for all non-empty $A'\subsetneq A$. Find the simplest $g(N)$ such that $c(N) = O(g(N)).

problems.statement: Filter.Tendsto (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos624.H n) - Real.logb 2 вҶ‘n) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Let $X$ be a finite set of size $n$ and $H(n)$ be such that there is a function
$f:\{A : A\subseteq X\}\to X$ so that for every $Y\subseteq X$ with $\lvert Y\rvert \geq H(n)$
we have $\left\{ f(A) : A\subseteq Y\right\}=X$.
Prove that $H(n)-\log_2 n \to \infty$.

problems.statement: {(t, Оұ) | 0 < t вҲ§ 0 < Оұ вҲ§ Erdos349.IsGoodPair t Оұ} = sorry
problems.docstring:
For what values of $t,\alpha \in (0,\infty)$ is the sequence $\lfloor t\alpha^n\rfloor$ complete
(that is, all sufficiently large integers are the sum of distinct integers of the form $\lfloor t\alpha^n\rfloor$)?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (t Оұ : в„қ), 0 < t вҶ’ Оұ вҲҲ Set.Ioo 1 ((1 + вҲҡ5) / 2) вҶ’ Erdos349.IsGoodPair t Оұ
problems.docstring:
It seems likely that the sequence is complete for all
for all $t>0$ and all $1 < \alpha < \frac{1+\sqrt{5}}{2}$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” {n | Even вҢҠ(3 / 2) ^ nвҢӢ}.Infinite
problems.docstring: Is it true that the terms of the sequence $\lfloor (3/2)^n\rfloor$ are even infinitely often?

problems.statement: True вҶ” {n | Odd вҢҠ(3 / 2) ^ nвҢӢ}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
Is it true that the terms of the sequence $\lfloor (3/2)^n\rfloor$ are odd infinitely
often and even infinitely often?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (k : в„•),
  вҲғ t вҲҲ Set.Ioo 0 1,
    вҲғ О№,
      вҶ‘k вүӨ Set.univ.encard вҲ§
        вҲғ I,
          (вҲҖ (i : О№), 2 вүӨ (I i).encard вҲ§ (I i).Nonempty вҲ§ IsConnected (I i)) вҲ§
            Pairwise (Function.onFun Disjoint I) вҲ§ вӢғ i, I i вҠҶ {Оұ | Оұ > 0 вҲ§ Erdos349.IsGoodPair t Оұ}
problems.docstring:
For any $k$ there exists some $t_k\in (0,1)$ such that the set of $\alpha$
such that the sequence $\lfloor t_k\alpha^n\rfloor$ is complete consists of at least $k$
disjoint line segments.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ S,
    Maximal
      (fun T =>
        (вҲҖ seg вҲҲ T,
            dist seg.1 seg.2 = 1 вҲ§
              seg.1.ofLp 0 вҲҲ Set.Icc 0 1 вҲ§
                seg.1.ofLp 1 вҲҲ Set.Icc 0 1 вҲ§ seg.2.ofLp 0 вҲҲ Set.Icc 0 1 вҲ§ seg.2.ofLp 1 вҲҲ Set.Icc 0 1) вҲ§
          (вҶ‘T).Pairwise Erdos1071.SegmentsDisjoint)
      S
problems.docstring:
Can a finite set of disjoint unit segments in a unit square be maximal?
Solved affirmatively by [Da85], who gave an explicit construction.

This was formalized in Lean by Alexeev using Aristotle and ChatGPT.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ R S,
    S.Countable вҲ§
      S.Infinite вҲ§
        Maximal
          (fun T => (вҲҖ seg вҲҲ T, dist seg.1 seg.2 = 1 вҲ§ seg.1 вҲҲ R вҲ§ seg.2 вҲҲ R) вҲ§ T.Pairwise Erdos1071.SegmentsDisjoint) S
problems.docstring: Is there a region $R$ with a maximal set of disjoint unit line segments that is countably infinite?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ c, Nat.PillaiPrimes.HasDensity c {p | Nat.Prime p}
problems.docstring:
Similarly, if $P$ is the set of all primes $p$ such that there exists an $m$ with
$p\not\equiv 1\pmod{m}$ such that $m! + 1 \equiv 0\pmod{p}$, then does
$$
  \lim\frac{|P\cap[1, x]|}{\pi(x)}
$$
exist?

problems.statement: 23 вҲҲ Nat.PillaiPrimes
problems.docstring:
Pillai [Pi30] raised the question of whether there exist any primes in $P$. This was answered
by Chowla, who noted that, for example, $14! + 1 \equiv 18! + 1 \equiv 0 \pmod{23}$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ c, Nat.EHSNumbers.HasDensity c
problems.docstring:
Let $S$ be the set of all $m\geq 1$ such that there exists a prime $p\not\equiv 1\pmod{m}$ such
that $m! + 1 \equiv 0\pmod{p}$. Does
$$
  \lim\frac{|S\cap[1, x]|}{x}
$$
exist?

problems.statement: Nat.nth Nat.EHSNumbers '' Set.Icc 0 6 = {8, 9, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17}
problems.docstring: The sequence $S$ begins $8, 9, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, ...$

problems.statement: Nat.PillaiPrimes.HasDensity sorry {p | Nat.Prime p}
problems.docstring:
Similarly, if $P$ is the set of all primes $p$ such that there exists an $m$ with
$p\not\equiv 1\pmod{m}$ such that $m! + 1 \equiv 0\pmod{p}$, then what is
$$
  \lim\frac{|P\cap[1, x]|}{\pi(x)}?
$$

problems.statement: Nat.PillaiPrimes.Infinite
problems.docstring: ErdЕ‘s, Hardy, and Subbarao proved that $P$ is infinite.

problems.statement: Nat.EHSNumbers.Infinite
problems.docstring: ErdЕ‘s, Hardy, and Subbarao proved that $S$ is infinite.

problems.statement: 2 вҲү Nat.PillaiPrimes
Nat.EHSNumbers.HasDensity sorry
problems.docstring:
Let $S$ be the set of all $m\geq 1$ such that there exists a prime $p\not\equiv 1\pmod{m}$ such
that $m! + 1 \equiv 0\pmod{p}$. What is
$$
  \lim\frac{|S\cap[1, x]|}{x}?
$$

problems.statement: 23 вҲҲ Nat.PillaiPrimes
Nat.nth Nat.PillaiPrimes '' Set.Icc 0 5 = {23, 29, 59, 61, 67, 71}
problems.docstring: The sequence $P$ begins $23, 29, 59, 61, 67, 71, ...$

problems.statement: Nat.EHSNumbers.HasDensity (1 / 2)
problems.docstring:
Regarding the first question, Hardy and Subbarao computed all EHS numbers up to $2^{10}$, and
write "...if this trend conditions we expect [the limit] to be around 0.5, if it exists."

problems.statement:
False вҶ”
  вҲҖ (V : Type) (G : SimpleGraph V),
    G.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph 1 вҶ’ вҲғ H, H.coe.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph 1 вҲ§ H.coe.InfinitelyConnected
problems.docstring:
Does every graph with chromatic number $\aleph_1$ contain an infinitely connected subgraph with
chromatic number $\aleph_1$?

KomjГЎth [Ko13] proved that it is consistent that the answer is no. This was improved by
Soukup [So15], who constructed a counterexample using no extra set-theoretical assumptions. A
simpler elementary example was given by Bowler and Pitz [BoPi24].

This was formalized in Lean by Alexeev using Aristotle and Aleph Prover.

problems.statement:
False вҶ”
  вҲҖ (V : Type) (G : SimpleGraph V),
    G.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph 1 вҶ’
      вҲғ H, H.coe.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph 1 вҲ§ Erdos1067.InfinitelyEdgeConnected H.coe
problems.docstring:
Thomassen [Th17] constructed a counterexample to the version which asks for infinite
edge-connectivity (that is, to disconnect the graph requires deleting infinitely many edges).

problems.statement:
(fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos208.erdos208.s (n + 1)) - вҶ‘(Erdos208.erdos208.s n)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n =>
  Real.log вҶ‘(Erdos208.erdos208.s n)
problems.docstring:
In [Er79] ErdЕ‘s says perhaps $s_{n+1} - s_n \ll \log s_n$, but he is 'very doubtful'.

[Er79] ErdЕ‘s, Paul, __Some unconventional problems in number theory__. Math. Mag. (1979), 67-70.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ Оө > 0,
    (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos208.erdos208.s (n + 1)) - вҶ‘(Erdos208.erdos208.s n)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n =>
      вҶ‘(Erdos208.erdos208.s n) ^ Оө
problems.docstring:
Let $s_1 < s_2 < \dots$ be the sequence of squarefree numbers. Is it true that
for any $\epsilon > 0$ and large $n$, $s_{n+1} - s_n \ll_\epsilon s_n^\epsilon$?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ c,
    c =o[Filter.atTop] 1 вҲ§
      вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
        вҶ‘(Erdos208.erdos208.s (n + 1)) - вҶ‘(Erdos208.erdos208.s n) вүӨ
          (1 + c n) * (Real.pi ^ 2 / 6) * Real.log вҶ‘(Erdos208.erdos208.s n) /
            Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘(Erdos208.erdos208.s n))
problems.docstring:
Let $s_1 < s_2 < \dots$ be the sequence of squarefree numbers. Is it true that
$s_{n + 1} - s_n \le (1 + o(1)) \cdot (\pi^2 / 6) \cdot \log (s_n) / \log (\log (s_n))$?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖб¶  (x : в„•) (y : в„•) in Filter.atTop, (x + y).primeCounting вүӨ x.primeCounting + y.primeCounting
вҲҖ Оө вҲҲ Set.Ioo 0 1, вҲғ r d, (Erdos851.TwoPowAddSet r).HasDensity d вҲ§ 1 - Оө вүӨ d
problems.docstring:
Let $\epsilon > 0$. Is there some $r \ll_\epsilon 1$ such that the density of integers of the
form $2^k+n$, where $k \geq 0$ and $n$ has at most $r$ prime divisors, is at least $1-\epsilon$?

problems.statement: 0 < (Erdos851.TwoPowAddSet 1).lowerDensity
problems.docstring:
The set of integers of the form `2^k+p` (where `p` is prime) has positive lower density.

Formalisation note: here we also allow `p = 1` since this simplifies the code and is equivalent
to the original statement.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (A : Set в„•),
    (A + A).HasPosDensity вҶ’ вҲғ AвӮҒ AвӮӮ, A = AвӮҒ вҲӘ AвӮӮ вҲ§ Disjoint AвӮҒ AвӮӮ вҲ§ (AвӮҒ + AвӮҒ).HasPosDensity вҲ§ (AвӮӮ + AвӮӮ).HasPosDensity
problems.docstring:
Let $A\subseteq \mathbb{N}$ be such that $A+A$ has positive density.
Can one always decompose $A=A_1\sqcup A_2$ such that $A_1+A_1$ and $A_2+A_2$
both have positive density?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ A,
    (A вҲӘ {0}).IsAddBasisOfOrder 2 вҲ§
      вҲҖ (AвӮҒ AвӮӮ : Set в„•), A = AвӮҒ вҲӘ AвӮӮ вҶ’ Disjoint AвӮҒ AвӮӮ вҶ’ В¬(IsSyndetic (AвӮҒ + AвӮҒ) вҲ§ IsSyndetic (AвӮӮ + AвӮӮ))
problems.docstring:
Is there a basis $A$ of order $2$ such that if $A=A_1\sqcup A_2$ then $A_1+A_1$ and $A_2+A_2$
cannot both have bounded gaps?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ в„Ө),
    (вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, IsLeast {x | a n < x вҲ§ x вҲү {x | вҲғ i вүӨ n, вҲғ j вүӨ n, a i + a j = x}} (a (n + 1))) вҶ’
      have d := fun i => a (i + 1) - a i;
      вҲғ p > 0, вҲҖб¶  (m : в„•) in Filter.atTop, d (m + p) = d m
problems.docstring:
Let $A=\{a_1 < \cdots < a_k\}$ be a finite set of integers and extend it to an infinite
sequence $\overline{A}=\{a_1 < a_2 < \cdots \}$ by defining $a_{n+1}$ for $n \geq k$ to be
the least integer exceeding $a_n$ which is not of the form $a_i + a_j$ with $i,j \leq n$.
Is it true that the sequence of differences $a_{m+1}-a_m$ is eventually periodic?

This problem is discussed under Problem 7 on Green's open problems list.

problems.statement: вҲғ A, Erdos33.AdditiveBasisCondition A вҲ§ 1 < Filter.limsup (fun N => вҶ‘(A.interIcc 1 N).ncard / вҲҡвҶ‘N) Filter.atTop
problems.docstring: Erdos observed that this value is finite and > 1.

problems.statement: вЁ… A, Filter.limsup (fun N => вҶ‘((вҶ‘A).interIcc 1 N).ncard / вҶ‘вҲҡвҶ‘N) Filter.atTop вүӨ вҶ‘(2 * Real.goldenRatio ^ (5 / 2))
problems.docstring:
The smallest possible value of `limвҖүsup n вҶ’ вҲһ |A вҲ© {1, вҖҰ, N}| / N^(1/2)`
is at most `2ПҶ^(5/2) вүҲ 6.66`, with `ПҶ` equal to the golden ratio. Proven by
Wouter van Doorn.

problems.statement: вЁ… A, Filter.limsup (fun N => вҶ‘((вҶ‘A).interIcc 1 N).ncard / вҶ‘вҲҡвҶ‘N) Filter.atTop = sorry
problems.docstring:
Let `A вҠҶ в„•` be a set such that every integer can be written as `n^2 + a`
for some `a` in `A` and `n вүҘ 0`. What is the smallest possible value of
`limвҖүsup n вҶ’ вҲһ |A вҲ© {1, вҖҰ, N}| / N^(1/2)`?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ A B, IsCompl A B вҲ§ (вҲғ f, В¬HasMonotoneAP (вҮ‘f) 3) вҲ§ вҲғ g, В¬HasMonotoneAP (вҮ‘g) 3
problems.docstring:
Can $\mathbb{N}$ be partitioned into two sets, each of which can be permuted to avoid monotone
3-term arithmetic progressions?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  {I | вҲҖ (j : Fin 2), (I j).1 вүӨ (I j).2 вҲ§ вҲғ n, вҲ‘ j, вҲ‘ nвұј вҲҲ (Set.Icc (I j).1 (I j).2).toFinset, (вҶ‘вҶ‘nвұј)вҒ»В№ = вҶ‘вҶ‘n}.Finite
problems.docstring:
Is it true that there are only finitely many pairs of intervals $I_1$, $I_2$ such that
$$
\sum_{n_1 \in I_1} \frac{1}{n_1} + \sum_{n_2 \in I_2} \frac{1}{n_2} \in \mathbb{N}?
$$

problems.statement: True вҶ” {(I, nвӮӮ) | I.1 вүӨ I.2 вҲ§ вҲғ n, вҲ‘ nвӮҒ вҲҲ (Set.Icc I.1 I.2).toFinset, (вҶ‘вҶ‘nвӮҒ)вҒ»В№ + (вҶ‘вҶ‘nвӮӮ)вҒ»В№ = вҶ‘вҶ‘n}.Finite
problems.docstring: This is still open even if $|I_2| = 1$.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ k > 2,
    {I | вҲҖ (j : Fin k), (I j).1 вүӨ (I j).2 вҲ§ вҲғ n, вҲ‘ j, вҲ‘ nвұј вҲҲ (Set.Icc (I j).1 (I j).2).toFinset, (вҶ‘вҶ‘nвұј)вҒ»В№ = вҶ‘вҶ‘n}.Finite
problems.docstring: Is it true for any $k > 2$ that only finitely many $k$ intervals satisfy this condition?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (k : в„•),
    {I | вҲҖ (j : Fin k), (I j).1 вүӨ (I j).2 вҲ§ вҲғ n, вҲ‘ j, вҲ‘ nвұј вҲҲ (Set.Icc (I j).1 (I j).2).toFinset, (вҶ‘вҶ‘nвұј)вҒ»В№ = вҶ‘вҶ‘n}.Finite
problems.docstring: It is perhaps true with two intervals replaced by any $k$ intervals.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (A : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
  StrictMono A вҶ’
    Erdos1102.HasPropertyQ (Set.range A) вҶ’ Filter.limsup (fun j => вҶ‘j / вҶ‘(A j)) Filter.atTop вүӨ 6 / Real.pi ^ 2
problems.docstring: Every sequence with property Q has upper density at most `6 / ПҖ^2`.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (f : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
  Filter.Tendsto f Filter.atTop Filter.atTop вҶ’
    (вҲҖ (n : в„•), f n вү  0) вҶ’ вҲғ A, StrictMono A вҲ§ Erdos1102.HasPropertyP (Set.range A) вҲ§ вҲҖ (j : в„•), вҶ‘(A j) / вҶ‘j вүӨ вҶ‘(f j)
problems.docstring:
Conversely, for any function `f : в„• вҶ’ в„•` that goes to infinity,
there exists a strictly increasing sequence `A = {aвӮҒ < aвӮӮ < вҖҰ}`
with property P such that `(a_j / j) вүӨ f(j)` for all `j`.

problems.statement:
вҲғ A,
  StrictMono A вҲ§
    (вҲҖ (j : в„•), Squarefree (A j)) вҲ§
      Erdos1102.HasPropertyQ (Set.range A) вҲ§ Filter.Tendsto (fun j => вҶ‘j / вҶ‘(A j)) Filter.atTop (nhds (6 / Real.pi ^ 2))
problems.docstring:
There exists an infinite sequence $A = {aвӮҒ < aвӮӮ < вҖҰ} вҠӮ \mathsf{SF}$ where
$\mathsf{SF} := \mathbb{N} \setminus \bigcup_{p} p^{2}\mathbb{N}$, i.e. the set of
squarefree numbers. The set `A` has property `Q` and natural density `6 / ПҖ^2`.
Equivalently, `(j / a_j) вҶ’ 6/ПҖ^2` as `j вҶ’ вҲһ`.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (A : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
  StrictMono A вҶ’ Erdos1102.HasPropertyP (Set.range A) вҶ’ Filter.Tendsto (fun j => вҶ‘(A j) / вҶ‘j) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
If `A = {aвӮҒ < aвӮӮ < вҖҰ}` has property P,
then `A` has natural density `0`.
Equivalently, `(a_j / j) вҶ’ вҲһ` as `j вҶ’ вҲһ`.

problems.statement: вҲғ x C, Function.Injective x вҲ§ вҲҖ (n : в„•), Prime (x n) вҲ§ Zsqrtd.norm (x (n + 1) - x n) < C
problems.docstring:
Is there an infinite sequence of distinct Gaussian primes $x_1,x_2,\ldots$
such that $\lvert x_{n+1}-x_n\rvert \ll 1$?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n k : в„•), 0 вүӨ n вҶ’ 2 вүӨ k вҶ’ В¬Erdos930.IsPower (вҲҸ m вҲҲ Finset.Icc (n + 1) (n + k), m)
problems.docstring:
Erdos and Selfridge [ErSe75] proved that the product of
consecutive integers is never a power (establishing the case $r=1$).

Theorem 1 from [ErSe75].

It is implied from `erdos_930.variants.consecutive_strong`.

[ErSe75] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Selfridge, J. L., The product of consecutive integers is never a power. Illinois J. Math. (1975), 292-301.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ r > 0,
    вҲғ k,
      вҲҖ (IвӮҒ IвӮӮ : Fin r вҶ’ в„•),
        (вҲҖ (i : Fin r), 0 < IвӮҒ i вҲ§ IвӮҒ i + k вүӨ IвӮӮ i + 1) вҶ’
          (вҲҖ (i j : Fin r), i < j вҶ’ IвӮӮ i < IвӮҒ j) вҶ’ В¬Erdos930.IsPower (вҲҸ i, вҲҸ m вҲҲ Finset.Icc (IвӮҒ i) (IвӮӮ i), m)
problems.docstring:
Is it true that, for every $r$, there is a $k$ such that
if $I_1,\ldots,I_r$ are disjoint intervals of consecutive integers,
all of length at least $k$, then
$$
  \prod_{1\leq i\leq r}\prod_{m\in I_i}m
$$
is not a perfect power?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (k l n : в„•),
  3 вүӨ k вҶ’
    2 вүӨ l вҶ’
      Erdos930.nextPrime k вүӨ n + k вҶ’
        вҲғ p, k вүӨ p вҲ§ Nat.Prime p вҲ§ В¬l вҲЈ (вҲҸ m вҲҲ Finset.Icc (n + 1) (n + k), m).factorization p
problems.docstring:
Let $k$, $l$, $n$ be integers such that $k \ge 3$, $l \ge 2$ and $n + k \ge p^{(k)}$,
where $p^{(k)}$ is the least prime satisfying $p^{(k)} \ge k$.
Then there is a prime $p \ge k$ for which $l$ does not divide
the multiplicity of the prime factor $p$ in $(n + 1) \ldots (n + k)$.

Theorem 2 from [ErSe75].

[ErSe75] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Selfridge, J. L., The product of consecutive integers is never a power. Illinois J. Math. (1975), 292-301.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ f, Continuous f вҲ§ вҲҖ (c : NNReal), {n | вҶ‘(primeGap n) / Real.log вҶ‘n < вҶ‘c}.HasDensity (f c)
problems.docstring:
Is it true that for all `c вүҘ 0`, the density `f c` of integers for which
`(p (n + 1) - p n) / log n < c` exists and is a continuous function of `c`?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ V G, G.chromaticNumber = вҠӨ вҲ§ вҲҖ (n : в„•), вҶ‘(Erdos74.SimpleGraph.maxSubgraphEdgeDistToBipartite G n) вүӨ вҲҡвҶ‘n
problems.docstring:
Is there a graph of infinite chromatic number such that every finite subgraph on $n$
vertices can be made bipartite by deleting at most $\sqrt{n}$ edges?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (f : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
    Filter.Tendsto f Filter.atTop Filter.atTop вҶ’
      вҲғ V G, G.chromaticNumber = вҠӨ вҲ§ вҲҖ (n : в„•), Erdos74.SimpleGraph.maxSubgraphEdgeDistToBipartite G n вүӨ f n
problems.docstring:
Let $f(n)\to \infty$ possibly very slowly.
Is there a graph of infinite chromatic number such that every finite subgraph on $n$
vertices can be made bipartite by deleting at most $f(n)$ edges?

problems.statement: вҲҖ {V : Type u} {G : SimpleGraph V} (A : G.Subgraph), (Erdos74.SimpleGraph.edgeDistancesToBipartite A).Nonempty
problems.docstring:
The set of edge distances to a bipartite graph is always non-empty because deleting all edges
from a graph makes it bipartite.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {V : Type u} (G : SimpleGraph V) (n : в„•), BddAbove (Erdos74.SimpleGraph.subgraphEdgeDistsToBipartite G n)
problems.docstring:
The set of minimum edge distances to bipartite for subgraphs of size `n` is bounded above.
A graph on `n` vertices has at most `n choose 2` edges, and deleting all of them
makes the graph bipartite, providing a straightforward upper bound.

problems.statement: вҲғ C > 0, {n | вҲҖ k вүҘ 1, вҶ‘(ArithmeticFunction.cardDistinctFactors (n + k)) вүӨ C * вҶ‘k}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
Are there infinitely many $n$ such that $\omega(n + k) \ll k$ for all $k \geq 1$?
Here $\omega(n)$ is the number of distinct prime divisors of $n$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ t вүҘ 2, Irrational (вҲ‘' (n : в„•+), 1 / (вҶ‘t ^ вҶ‘n - 1))
problems.docstring: ErdЕ‘s [Er48] proved that this is true if $t\geq 2$ is an integer.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ t > 1, Irrational (вҲ‘' (n : в„•+), 1 / (вҶ‘t ^ вҶ‘n - 1))
problems.docstring:
Let $t>1$ be a rational number. Is
$\sum_{n=1}^\infty\frac{1}{t^n-1}=\sum_{n=1}^\infty \frac{\tau(n)}{t^n}$ irrational, where
$\tau(n)$ counts the divisors of $n$?

A conjecture of Chowla.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (t : в„ҡ), вҲ‘' (n : в„•+), 1 / (вҶ‘t ^ вҶ‘n - 1) = вҲ‘' (n : в„•+), вҶ‘(вҶ‘n).divisors.card / вҶ‘t ^ вҶ‘n
problems.docstring: The Lambert series identity where $x = 1/t$ for the divisor function.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ Оө > 0, вҲғ K, вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, (Erdos886.Erdos886Divisors n Оө 1).card вүӨ K
problems.docstring:
Let $\epsilon>0$. Is it true that, for all large $n$, the number of divisors of $n$ in
$(n^{1/2},n^{1/2}+n^{1/2-\epsilon})$ is $O_\epsilon(1)$?

ErdЕ‘s attributes this conjecture to Ruzsa.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ C > 0,
  вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
    вҶ‘{d вҲҲ n.divisors | вҶ‘n ^ (1 / 2) вүӨ вҶ‘d вҲ§ вҶ‘d вүӨ вҶ‘n ^ (1 / 2) + C * вҶ‘n ^ (1 / 4)}.card вүӨ 1 + C ^ 2
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and Rosenfeld [ErRo97] proved that, for any constant $C>0$, all large $n$ have at most
$1+C^2$ many divisors in $[n^{1/2}, n^{1/2}+Cn^{1/4}]$.

problems.statement: {n | 4 вүӨ (Erdos886.Erdos886Divisors n (1 / 4) 16).card}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and Rosenfeld [ErRo97] proved that there are infinitely many $n$ such that there are
four divisors of $n$ in $(n^{1/2},n^{1/2}+16n^{1/4})$.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (p : в„•) (a : Fin p вҶ’ ZMod p),
  (вҲғ r, вҲҖ (S : Finset (Fin p)), S вү  вҲ… вҶ’ вҲ‘ i вҲҲ S, a i = 0 вҶ’ S.card = r) вҶ’ (Set.range a).ncard вүӨ 2
problems.docstring: Gao, Hamidoune, and Wang [GHW10] solved this for all moduli `p` (not necessarily prime).

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (p : в„•),
    Fact (Nat.Prime p) вҶ’
      вҲҖ (a : Fin p вҶ’ ZMod p),
        (вҲғ r, вҲҖ (S : Finset (Fin p)), S вү  вҲ… вҶ’ вҲ‘ i вҲҲ S, a i = 0 вҶ’ S.card = r) вҶ’ (Set.range a).ncard вүӨ 2
problems.docstring:
Let $a_1, \dots, a_p$ be (not necessarily distinct) residues modulo a prime $p$, such that there
exists some $r$ so that if $S \subseteq [p]$ is non-empty and
$$\sum_{i \in S} a_i \equiv 0 \pmod{p}$$
then $|S| = r$.

Must there be at most two distinct residues amongst the $a_i$?

This was formalized in Lean by Alexeev using Aristotle and ChatGPT.

problems.statement:
вҲҖб¶  (p : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
  Nat.Prime p вҶ’
    вҲҖ (a : Fin p вҶ’ ZMod p),
      (вҲғ r, вҲҖ (S : Finset (Fin p)), S вү  вҲ… вҶ’ вҲ‘ i вҲҲ S, a i = 0 вҶ’ S.card = r) вҶ’ (Set.range a).ncard вүӨ 2
problems.docstring: This was proved by ErdЕ‘s and SzemerГ©di [ErSz76] for p sufficiently large.

problems.statement: Erdos672.Erdos672With 4 2
problems.docstring: According to https://www.erdosproblems.com/672, Euler proved this.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (k l : в„•), l > 1 вҶ’ k вүҘ 4 вҶ’ Erdos672.Erdos672With k l
problems.docstring:
Can the product of an arithmetic progression of positive integers $n, n + d, ..., n + (k - 1)d$
of length вүҘ 4, with $(n, d) = 1$, be a perfect power?

problems.statement: Erdos672.Erdos672With 5 2 вҲ§ Erdos672.Erdos672With 3 3 вҲ§ Erdos672.Erdos672With 3 4 вҲ§ Erdos672.Erdos672With 3 5
problems.docstring:
According to https://www.erdosproblems.com/672, OblГЎth proved this.

[Ob51] Oblath, Richard, Eine Bemerkung Гјber Produkte aufeinander folgender Zahlen.
J. Indian Math. Soc. (N.S.) (1951), 135-139.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ r вүҘ 3,
  вҲҖ {V : Type} [inst : Fintype V] [DecidableEq V],
    Fintype.card V = r ^ 2 + 1 вҶ’
      вҲҖ (coloring : Sym2 V вҶ’ Fin r), вҲғ S k, S.card = r + 1 вҲ§ вҲҖ u вҲҲ S, вҲҖ v вҲҲ S, u вү  v вҶ’ coloring s(u, v) вү  k
problems.docstring:
Let $r\geq 3$. If the edges of $K_{r^2+1}$ are $r$-coloured then there exist $r+1$ vertices with at
least one colour missing on the edges of the induced $K_{r+1}$.

In other words, there is no balanced colouring.

A conjecture of ErdЕ‘s and GyГЎrfГЎs [ErGy99].

problems.statement:
вҲҖ r вүҘ 3,
  вҲҖ {V : Type} [inst : Fintype V] [DecidableEq V],
    Fintype.card V = 3 ^ 2 + 1 вҶ’
      вҲҖ (coloring : Sym2 V вҶ’ Fin 3), вҲғ S k, S.card = 3 + 1 вҲ§ вҲҖ u вҲҲ S, вҲҖ v вҲҲ S, u вү  v вҶ’ coloring s(u, v) вү  k
problems.docstring: ErdЕ‘s and GyГЎrfГЎs [ErGy99] proved the conjecture for $r=3$.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ r вүҘ 3,
  вҲҖ {V : Type} [inst : Fintype V] [DecidableEq V],
    Fintype.card V = 4 ^ 2 + 1 вҶ’
      вҲҖ (coloring : Sym2 V вҶ’ Fin 4), вҲғ S k, S.card = 4 + 1 вҲ§ вҲҖ u вҲҲ S, вҲҖ v вҲҲ S, u вү  v вҶ’ coloring s(u, v) вү  k
problems.docstring: ErdЕ‘s and GyГЎrfГЎs [ErGy99] proved the conjecture for $r=4$.

problems.statement:
{r |
    вҲғ V x x_1,
      Fintype.card V = r ^ 2 вҲ§
        вҲғ coloring,
          вҲҖ (S : Finset V), S.card = r + 1 вҶ’ вҲҖ (k : Fin r), вҲғ u вҲҲ S, вҲғ v вҲҲ S, u вү  v вҲ§ coloring s(u, v) = k}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and GyГЎrfГЎs [ErGy99] showed this property fails for infinitely many $r$ if we replace $r^2+1$
by $r^2$.

problems.statement: Filter.limsup (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos379.S n)) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ
problems.docstring:
Let $S(n)$ denote the largest integer such that, for all $1 вүӨ k < n$, the binomial coefficient
$\binom{n}{k}$ is divisible by $p^S(n)$ for some prime $p$ (depending on $k$).Then
$\limsup S(n) = \infty$.

This was formalized in Lean by Tao.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (рқ“’ : в„Ө вҶ’ в„Ө),
    (Set.range рқ“’).Finite вҶ’ вҲғ a b c, [a, b, c, 0].Nodup вҲ§ 1 / вҶ‘a = 1 / вҶ‘b + 1 / вҶ‘c вҲ§ (рқ“’ '' {a, b, c}).Subsingleton
problems.docstring:
Is it true that in any finite colouring of the integers there exists a monochromatic solution
to $\frac 1 a = \frac 1 b + \frac 1 c$ with distinct $a, b, c$?

This is true, as proved by Brown and RГ¶dl [BrRo91].

This was formalized in Lean by Yuan using Seed-Prover.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ f,
    Transcendental (Polynomial в„Ӯ) f вҲ§
      Differentiable в„Ӯ f вҲ§ вҲҖ (n : в„• вҶ’ в„•), StrictMono n вҶ’ Dense {z | вҲғ k, iteratedDeriv (n k) f z = 0}
problems.docstring:
Does there exists an entire non-zero transcendental function `f : в„Ӯ вҶ’ в„Ӯ` such that for any
sequence `nвӮҖ < nвӮҒ < ...`, `{ z | вҲғ k, iteratedDeriv (n k) f z = 0 }` is dense.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (A : Set в„қ), Erdos143.WellSeparatedSet A вҶ’ Filter.liminf (fun x => вҶ‘(A вҲ© Set.Icc 1 x).ncard / x) Filter.atTop = 0
problems.docstring:
Does this imply that
$$
\liminf \frac{|A \cap [1,x]|}{x} = 0?
$$

problems.statement: вҲҖ (A : Set в„қ), Erdos143.WellSeparatedSet A вҶ’ Summable fun x => 1 / (вҶ‘x * Real.log вҶ‘x)
problems.docstring:
Or
$$
\sum_{x \in A} \frac{1}{x \log x} < \infty,
$$

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ {Оұ : Type u_1} [inst : Fintype Оұ] [inst_1 : DecidableEq Оұ] (H : SimpleGraph Оұ),
    вҲғ c > 0, Erdos61.IsErdosHajnalLowerBound H fun n => вҶ‘n ^ c
problems.docstring:
The ErdЕ‘sвҖ“Hajnal Conjecture states that there is a constant $c(H) > 0$ for each
$H$ such that we can take $f(n) = n^{c(H)}$ in the above formulation.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {Оұ : Type u_1} [inst : Fintype Оұ] [inst_1 : DecidableEq Оұ] (H : SimpleGraph Оұ),
  вҲғ c > 0, Erdos61.IsErdosHajnalLowerBound H fun n => Real.exp (c * вҲҡ(Real.log вҶ‘n * Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘n)))
problems.docstring:
BuciДҮ, Nguyen, Scott, and Seymour [BNSS23] improved this to
$f(n) = \exp(c_H \sqrt{\log n \log \log n})$ for some constant $c_H > 0$ dependending on $H$.

[BNSS23] BuciДҮ, M. and Nguyen, T. and Scott, A. and Seymour, P., A loglog step towards Erdos-Hajnal

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {Оұ : Type u_1} [inst : Fintype Оұ] [inst_1 : DecidableEq Оұ] (H : SimpleGraph Оұ),
  вҲғ c > 0, Erdos61.IsErdosHajnalLowerBound H fun n => Real.exp (c * вҲҡ(Real.log вҶ‘n))
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and Hajnal [ErHa89] proved that we can take $f(n) = \exp(c_H \sqrt{\log n})$
for some constant $c_H > 0$ dependending on $H$.

[ErHa89] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Hajnal, A., Ramsey-type theorems. Discrete Appl. Math. (1989), 37-52.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (Пү : Ordinal.{u}),
    вҲғ V G,
      Cardinal.mk V = Cardinal.aleph (Пү + 1) вҲ§
        G.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph 1 вҲ§
          вҲҖ (W : Set V),
            Cardinal.mk вҶ‘W = Cardinal.aleph Пү вҶ’ (SimpleGraph.induce W G).chromaticCardinal вүӨ Cardinal.aleph0
problems.docstring:
Is there a graph with $\aleph_{\omega+1}$ vertices and chromatic number $\aleph_1$ such that
every subgraph on $\aleph_\omega$ vertices has chromatic number $\leq\aleph_0$?

problems.statement:
В¬вҲғ V G,
    Cardinal.mk V = Cardinal.aleph 2 вҲ§
      G.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph 2 вҲ§
        вҲҖ (H : G.Subgraph), Cardinal.mk вҶ‘H.verts = Cardinal.aleph 1 вҶ’ H.coe.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph0
problems.docstring:
In [ErHa69] the questions are stated with $= \aleph_0$ rather than $\leq\aleph_0$. This is
a likely typo since it can be shown that no such graph exists in this case.

This is the first question with all subgraphs.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (k : в„•),
  0 < k вҶ’
    вҲғ V G,
      Cardinal.mk V = Cardinal.aleph вҶ‘k вҲ§
        G.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph 1 вҲ§
          вҲҖ (W : Set V),
            Cardinal.mk вҶ‘W < Cardinal.aleph вҶ‘k вҶ’ (SimpleGraph.induce W G).chromaticCardinal вүӨ Cardinal.aleph0
problems.docstring:
A question of Erd\H{o}s and Hajnal [ErHa68b], who proved that for every finite $k$
there is a graph with chromatic number $\aleph_1$ and $\aleph_k$ vertices where each subgraph on
less than $\aleph_k$ vertices has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_0$.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (Пү : Ordinal.{u}),
  В¬вҲғ V G,
      Cardinal.mk V = Cardinal.aleph (Пү + 1) вҲ§
        G.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph 1 вҲ§
          вҲҖ (W : Set V),
            Cardinal.mk вҶ‘W = Cardinal.aleph Пү вҶ’ (SimpleGraph.induce W G).chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph0
problems.docstring:
In [ErHa69] the questions are stated with $= \aleph_0$ rather than $\leq\aleph_0$. This is
a likely typo since it can be shown that no such graph exists in this case.

This is the second question with induced subgraphs.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ V G,
    Cardinal.mk V = Cardinal.aleph 2 вҲ§
      G.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph 2 вҲ§
        вҲҖ (H : G.Subgraph), Cardinal.mk вҶ‘H.verts = Cardinal.aleph 1 вҶ’ H.coe.chromaticCardinal вүӨ Cardinal.aleph0
problems.docstring:
Is there a graph with $\aleph_2$ vertices and chromatic number $\aleph_2$ such that every
subgraph on $\aleph_1$ vertices has chromatic number $\leq\aleph_0$?

problems.statement:
В¬вҲғ V G,
    Cardinal.mk V = Cardinal.aleph 2 вҲ§
      G.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph 2 вҲ§
        вҲҖ (W : Set V), Cardinal.mk вҶ‘W = Cardinal.aleph 1 вҶ’ (SimpleGraph.induce W G).chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph0
problems.docstring:
In [ErHa69] the questions are stated with $= \aleph_0$ rather than $\leq\aleph_0$. This is
a likely typo since it can be shown that no such graph exists in this case.

This is the first question with induced subgraphs.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ V G,
    Cardinal.mk V = Cardinal.aleph 2 вҲ§
      G.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph 2 вҲ§
        вҲҖ (W : Set V), Cardinal.mk вҶ‘W = Cardinal.aleph 1 вҶ’ (SimpleGraph.induce W G).chromaticCardinal вүӨ Cardinal.aleph0
problems.docstring:
Is there a graph with $\aleph_2$ vertices and chromatic number $\aleph_2$ such that every
subgraph on $\aleph_1$ vertices has chromatic number $\leq\aleph_0$?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (Пү : Ordinal.{u}),
  В¬вҲғ V G,
      Cardinal.mk V = Cardinal.aleph (Пү + 1) вҲ§
        G.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph 1 вҲ§
          вҲҖ (H : G.Subgraph), Cardinal.mk вҶ‘H.verts = Cardinal.aleph Пү вҶ’ H.coe.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph0
problems.docstring:
In [ErHa69] the questions are stated with $= \aleph_0$ rather than $\leq\aleph_0$. This is
a likely typo since it can be shown that no such graph exists in this case.

This is the second question with all subgraphs.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (Пү : Ordinal.{u}),
    вҲғ V G,
      Cardinal.mk V = Cardinal.aleph (Пү + 1) вҲ§
        G.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph 1 вҲ§
          вҲҖ (H : G.Subgraph), Cardinal.mk вҶ‘H.verts = Cardinal.aleph Пү вҶ’ H.coe.chromaticCardinal вүӨ Cardinal.aleph0
problems.docstring:
Is there a graph with $\aleph_{\omega+1}$ vertices and chromatic number $\aleph_1$ such that
every subgraph on $\aleph_\omega$ vertices has chromatic number $\leq\aleph_0$?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (f : в„• вҶ’ в„қ),
    (вҲҖ a > 0, вҲҖ b > 0, a.Coprime b вҶ’ f (a * b) = f a + f b) вҶ’
      Filter.limsup
            (fun x =>
              match x with
              | (p, k) => вҶ‘(f (p ^ k)) / вҶ‘(Real.log (вҶ‘p ^ k)))
            (Filter.atTop вҠ“ Filter.principal {(p, k) | Nat.Prime p}) =
          вҠӨ вҶ’
        ((вҲҖ (k p : в„•), Nat.Prime p вҶ’ f (p ^ k) = f p) вҲЁ вҲҖ (k p : в„•), Nat.Prime p вҶ’ f (p ^ k) = вҶ‘k * f p) вҶ’
          Filter.limsup (fun n => вҶ‘(f (n + 1)) / вҶ‘(f n)) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ
problems.docstring:
Let $f(n)$ be an additive function (so that $f(ab)=f(a)+f(b)$
if $(a,b)=1$) such that $\limsup_{p,k} f(p^k) \log(p^k) = вҲһ$ and $f(p^k) = f(p)$
or $f(p^k) = kf(p)$.
Is it true that $\limsup_n f(n+1)/f(n) = вҲһ$?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (f : в„• вҶ’ в„қ),
    (вҲҖ a > 0, вҲҖ b > 0, a.Coprime b вҶ’ f (a * b) = f a + f b) вҶ’
      Filter.limsup
            (fun x =>
              match x with
              | (p, k) => вҶ‘(f (p ^ k)) / вҶ‘(Real.log (вҶ‘p ^ k)))
            (Filter.atTop вҠ“ Filter.principal {(p, k) | Nat.Prime p}) =
          вҠӨ вҶ’
        Filter.limsup (fun n => (вҶ‘(f (n + 1)) - вҶ‘(f n)) / вҶ‘(Real.log вҶ‘n)) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ
problems.docstring:
Let $f(n)$ be an additive function (so that $f(ab)=f(a)+f(b)$
if $(a,b)=1$ such that $\limsup_{p,k} f(p^k) \log(p^k) = вҲһ$.
Is it true that $\limsup_n (f(n+1)вҲ’f(n))/ \log n = вҲһ$?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (f : в„• вҶ’ в„қ),
    (вҲҖ a > 0, вҲҖ b > 0, a.Coprime b вҶ’ f (a * b) = f a + f b) вҶ’
      Filter.limsup
            (fun x =>
              match x with
              | (p, k) => вҶ‘(f (p ^ k)) / вҶ‘(Real.log (вҶ‘p ^ k)))
            (Filter.atTop вҠ“ Filter.principal {(p, k) | Nat.Prime p}) =
          вҠӨ вҶ’
        ((вҲҖ (k p : в„•), Nat.Prime p вҶ’ f (p ^ k) = f p) вҲЁ вҲҖ (k p : в„•), Nat.Prime p вҶ’ f (p ^ k) = вҶ‘k * f p) вҶ’
          Filter.limsup (fun n => (вҶ‘(f (n + 1)) - вҶ‘(f n)) / вҶ‘(Real.log вҶ‘n)) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ
problems.docstring:
Let $f(n)$ be an additive function (so that $f(ab)=f(a)+f(b)$
if $(a,b)=1$) such that $\limsup_{p,k} f(p^k) \log(p^k) = вҲһ$ and $f(p^k) = f(p)$
or $f(p^k) = kf(p)$.
Is it true that $\limsup_n (f(n+1)вҲ’f(n))/ \log n = вҲһ$?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (f : в„• вҶ’ в„қ),
    (вҲҖ a > 0, вҲҖ b > 0, a.Coprime b вҶ’ f (a * b) = f a + f b) вҶ’
      Filter.limsup
            (fun x =>
              match x with
              | (p, k) => вҶ‘(f (p ^ k)) / вҶ‘(Real.log (вҶ‘p ^ k)))
            (Filter.atTop вҠ“ Filter.principal {(p, k) | Nat.Prime p}) =
          вҠӨ вҶ’
        Filter.limsup (fun n => вҶ‘(f (n + 1)) / вҶ‘(f n)) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ
problems.docstring:
Let $f(n)$ be an additive function (so that $f(ab)=f(a)+f(b)$
if $(a,b)=1$) such that $\limsup_{p,k} f(p^k) \log(p^k) = вҲһ$.
Is it true that $\limsup_n f(n+1)/ f(n) = вҲһ$?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (f : в„• вҶ’ в„қ),
  (вҲҖ a > 0, вҲҖ b > 0, a.Coprime b вҶ’ f (a * b) = f a + f b) вҶ’
    вҲҖ (C : в„қ), (вҲҖ (n : в„•), |f (n + 1) - f n| вүӨ C) вҶ’ вҲғ c O, O =O[Filter.atTop] 1 вҲ§ вҲҖ (n : в„•), f n вүӨ c * Real.log вҶ‘n + O n
problems.docstring:
Wirsing [Wi70] proved that if $|f(n+1)вҲ’f(n)| вүӨ C$ then $f(n) = c \log n + O(1)$ for some constant
$c$.

[Wi70] Wirsing, E., _A characterization of $\log n$ as an additive arithmetic function_.
Symposia Math. (1970), 45-47.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ f,
    вҲғ (_ : Filter.Tendsto f Filter.atTop Filter.atTop),
      вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҲғ m, m.Composite вҲ§ n + f n < m вҲ§ m < n + m.minFac
problems.docstring:
Is there a function $f$ with $f(n)\to\infty$ as $n\to\infty$ such that,
for all large $n$, there is a composite number $m$ such that
$$
n + f(n) < m < n + p(m)
$$
Here $p(m)$ is the least prime factor of $m$.

problems.statement:
вҲғ cвӮҒ > 0,
  вҲғ cвӮӮ > 0,
    вҲғ d,
      (вҲҖ t > 0, (Erdos859.DivisorSumSet t).HasDensity (d t)) вҲ§
        Asymptotics.IsEquivalent Filter.atTop (fun t => d t) fun t => cвӮҒ / Real.log вҶ‘t ^ cвӮӮ
Erdos859.DivisorSumSet 0 = Set.univ

вҲғ cвӮғ > 0,
  вҲғ cвӮ„ > 0,
    вҲғ tвӮҖ,
      вҲҖб¶  (t : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
        вҲғ dвӮң, (Erdos859.DivisorSumSet t).HasDensity dвӮң вҲ§ 1 / Real.log вҶ‘t ^ cвӮғ < dвӮң вҲ§ dвӮң < 1 / Real.log вҶ‘t ^ cвӮ„
problems.docstring:
A weaker version of the problem proved by Erdos:
The density `dвӮң` of `DivisorSumSet (t : в„•)` is bounded from below by `1 / log (t) ^ cвӮғ` and
from above by `1 / log (t) ^ cвӮ„` for some positive constants `cвӮғ` and `cвӮ„`.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (t : в„•), (Erdos859.DivisorSumSet t).HasPosDensity
True вҶ” вҲҖ C > 0, Erdos4.Erdos4For C
problems.docstring:
Is it true that, for any $C > 0$, there infinitely many $n$ such that:
$$
  p_{n + 1} - p_n > C \frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log\log n) ^ 2}\log n
$$

problems.statement: вҲғ C > 0, Erdos4.Erdos4For C
True вҶ” вҲҖ m > 0, вҲҖ n > 0, вҲғ i j, Erdos414.h^[i] m = Erdos414.h^[j] n
problems.docstring:
Let $h_1(n) = h(n)$ and $h_k(n) = h(h_{k-1}(n))$. Is it true, for any $m,n$, there exist
$i$ and $j$ such that $h_i(m) = h_j(n)$?

problems.statement: True вҶ” {n | вҲғ m, n.factorial + 1 = m ^ 2} = {4, 5, 7}
problems.docstring:
**Brocard's Problem**
Does $n! + 1 = m^2$ have integer solutions other than $n = 4, 5, 7$?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (ОІ : Ordinal.{u}),
  ОІ.card вүӨ Cardinal.aleph0 вҶ’ OrdinalCardinalRamsey (Ordinal.omega0 ^ ОІ) (Ordinal.omega0 ^ ОІ) 3 вҶ” sorry ОІ
problems.docstring:
Determine which countable ordinals $ОІ$ have the property that, if $Оұ = \omega^ОІ$, then in any
red/blue colouring of the edges of $K_Оұ$ there is either a red $K_Оұ$ or a blue $K_3$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ Оұ > 1, Irrational Оұ вҶ’ (Erdos972.primeSet Оұ).Infinite
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘s problem 972.**
Let $\alpha > 1$ be irrational. Are there infinitely many primes $p$
such that $\lfloor p\alpha \rfloor$ is also prime?

problems.statement:
((fun n => вҶ‘n / вҲҡ(Real.log вҶ‘n)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos89.minimalDistinctDistances n)) вҶ’
  (fun n => вҶ‘n / Real.log вҶ‘n) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos89.minimalDistinctDistances n)
problems.docstring:
This theorem provides a sanity check, showing that the main conjecture (`erdos_89`) is strictly
stronger than the solved Guth and Katz result. It proves that, trivially, if the lower bound
$\frac{n}{\sqrt{\log n}}$ holds, then the weaker lower bound $\frac{n}{\log n}$ must also hold.

problems.statement: (fun n => вҶ‘n / Real.log вҶ‘n) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos89.minimalDistinctDistances n)
problems.docstring:
Guth and Katz [GuKa15] proved that there are always $\gg \frac{n}{\log n}$ many distinct distances.

[GuKa15] Guth, Larry and Katz, Nets Hawk, On the ErdЕ‘s distinct distances problem in the plane. Ann. of Math. (2) (2015), 155-190.

problems.statement: (fun n => вҶ‘n / вҲҡ(Real.log вҶ‘n)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos89.minimalDistinctDistances n)
problems.docstring:
Does every set of $n$ distinct points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ determine $\gg \frac{n}{\sqrt{\log n}}$
many distinct distances?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), IsPrimePow n вҶ’ вҲғ P L x x_1 x_2 pp, Configuration.ProjectivePlane.order P L = n
problems.docstring: These always exist if $n$ is a prime power.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {P L : Type} [inst : Membership P L] [Fintype P] [Fintype L] (n : в„•) (pp : Configuration.ProjectivePlane P L),
  Configuration.ProjectivePlane.order P L = n вҶ’ n вүЎ 1 [MOD 4] вҲЁ n вүЎ 2 [MOD 4] вҶ’ вҲғ a b, n = a ^ 2 + b ^ 2
problems.docstring:
Bruck and Ryser have proved that if $n \equiv 1 (\mod 4)$ or $n \equiv 2 (\mod 4)$ then $n$ must be
the sum of two squares.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ P L x x_1 x_2 pp, Configuration.ProjectivePlane.order P L = 12
problems.docstring: It is open whether there exists a projective plane of order 12.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ {P L : Type} (x : Membership P L) (x_1 : Fintype P) (x_2 : Fintype L) (pp : Configuration.ProjectivePlane P L),
    IsPrimePow (Configuration.ProjectivePlane.order P L)
problems.docstring: If there is a finite projective plane of order $n$ then must $n$ be a prime power?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {P L : Type} [inst : Membership P L] [Fintype P] [Fintype L] (pp : Configuration.ProjectivePlane P L),
  Configuration.ProjectivePlane.order P L вүӨ 11 вҶ’ IsPrimePow (Configuration.ProjectivePlane.order P L)
problems.docstring: This conjecture has been proved for $n \leq 11$.

problems.statement: {(n, k) | 0 < k вҲ§ 2 * k вүӨ n вҲ§ (n.choose k).minFac > max (n / k) k}.Finite
problems.docstring:
For all $n\ge 2k$ the least prime factor of $\binom{n}{k}$ is $\le\max(n/k,k)$, with only
finitely many exceptions.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ в„Ө) (s : в„қ),
    StrictMono a вҶ’
      Filter.Tendsto (fun n => вҶ‘(a n) / вҶ‘n) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop вҶ’
        HasSum (fun n => вҶ‘(a n) / 2 ^ a n) s вҶ’ Irrational s

вҲҖ (n : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
  StrictMono n вҶ’
    (вҲҖ t вүҘ 1, Filter.limsup (fun k => вҶ‘(вҶ‘(n k) / вҶ‘k.succ ^ t)) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ) вҶ’
      Transcendental в„ҡ (вҲ‘' (k : в„•), 1 / 2 ^ n k)
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s proved the answer is yes under the stronger condition that
$\limsup \frac{n_k}{k^t} = \infty$ for all $t\geq 1$.

[ErGr80] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Graham, R.,
_Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number theory_.
Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (n : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
    StrictMono n вҶ’
      Filter.limsup (fun k => вҶ‘(n k) / вҶ‘k.succ) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ вҶ’ Transcendental в„ҡ (вҲ‘' (k : в„•), 1 / 2 ^ n k)
problems.docstring:
Let $n_1 < n_2 < \cdots$ be a sequence of integers such that
$$
  \limsup \frac{n_k}{k} = \infty.
$$
Is
$$
  \sum_{k=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{2^{n_k}}
$$
transcendental?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (k : в„•), вҲғ n, n.descFactorial (k + 1) вҲЈ n.centralBinom
problems.docstring:
Is it true that for every $k$ there exists $n$ such that
$$\prod_{0\leq i\leq k}(n-i) \mid \binom{2n}{n}?$$

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ Оө > 0, (fun N => вҶ‘(Erdos30.h N) - вҲҡвҶ‘N) =O[Filter.atTop] fun N => вҶ‘N ^ Оө
problems.docstring: Is it true that, for every $\varepsilon > 0$, $h(N) = \sqrt N + O_{\varespilon}(N^\varespilon)

problems.statement:
вҲғ A,
  IsAddStronglyCompleteNatSeq A вҲ§
    (вҲҖ B вҠҶ Set.range A, B.Infinite вҶ’ В¬IsAddComplete (Set.range A \ B)) вҲ§
      Filter.liminf (fun n => вҶ‘(A (n + 1)) / 2) Filter.atTop = 1 вҲ§
        Filter.limsup (fun n => вҶ‘(A (n + 1)) / вҶ‘(A n)) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and Graham [ErGr80] also say that it is not hard to construct very irregular sequences
satisfying the aforementioned properties.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {B : Set в„•}, B вҠҶ Set.range Erdos346.f вҶ’ B.Infinite вҶ’ В¬IsAddComplete (Set.range Erdos346.f \ B)
problems.docstring:
The sequence `f` is not complete whenever infinitely many terms are removed from it, and this
is proved in [Gr64d].

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ {A : в„• вҶ’ в„•},
    IsLacunary A вҶ’
      IsAddStronglyCompleteNatSeq A вҶ’
        (вҲҖ B вҠҶ Set.range A, B.Infinite вҶ’ В¬IsAddComplete (Set.range A \ B)) вҶ’
          Filter.Tendsto (fun n => вҶ‘(A (n + 1)) / вҶ‘(A n)) Filter.atTop (nhds ((1 + вҲҡ5) / 2))
problems.docstring:
Is it true that for every lacunary, strongly complete sequence `A` that is not complete whenever
infinitely many terms are removed from it, `lim A (n + 1) / A n = (1 + вҲҡ5) / 2`?

problems.statement: IsLacunary Erdos346.f
problems.docstring: The sequence `f` is lacunary.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {A : в„• вҶ’ в„•},
  (вҲҖ (n : в„•), (1 + вҲҡ5) / 2 * вҶ‘(A n) < вҶ‘(A (n + 1))) вҶ’
    вҲҖ {B : Set в„•}, B вҠҶ Set.range A вҶ’ B.Infinite вҶ’ В¬IsAddComplete (Set.range A \ B)
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and Graham [ErGr80] remark that it is easy to see that if `A (n + 1) / A n > (1 + вҲҡ5) / 2`
then the second property is automatically satisfied.

problems.statement: IsAddStronglyCompleteNatSeq Erdos346.f
problems.docstring: The sequence `f` is strongly complete, and this is proved in [Gr64d].

problems.statement: (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos1085.f 2 n)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘n ^ (4 / 3)
problems.docstring: Spencer, SzemerГ©di, and Trotter showed $f_2(n) = O(n^{4/3})$.

problems.statement: (fun n => вҶ‘n ^ (4 / 3) * Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘n)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos1085.f 3 n)
problems.docstring: ErdЕ‘s showed $f_3(n) = О©(n^{4/3}\log\log n)$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {d : в„•}, 4 вүӨ d вҶ’ вҲғ C, вҲҖ (n : в„•), вҶ‘(d / 2 - 1) / (2 * вҶ‘(d / 2)) * вҶ‘n ^ 2 - C вүӨ вҶ‘(Erdos1085.f d n)
problems.docstring:
Lenz showed that, for $d \ge 4$, $f_d(n) \ge \frac{p - 1}{2p} n^2 - O(1)$ where
$p = \lfloor\frac d2\rfloor$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos1085.f 3 n)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘n ^ (4 / 3) * Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘n)
problems.docstring: Is the $n^{4/3}\log\log n$ lower bound in 3D also an upper bound?.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {d : в„•},
  4 вүӨ d вҶ’
    вҲғ g,
      Filter.Tendsto g Filter.atTop (nhds 0) вҲ§
        вҲҖ (n : в„•), вҶ‘(Erdos1085.f d n) вүӨ (вҶ‘(d / 2 - 1) / (2 * вҶ‘(d / 2)) + g n) * вҶ‘n ^ 2
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s showed that, for $d \ge 4$, $f_d(n) \le \left(\frac{p - 1}{2p} + o(1)\right) n^2$ where
$p = \lfloor\frac d2\rfloor$.

problems.statement: вҲғ c > 0, вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҶ‘n ^ (1 + c / Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘n)) < вҶ‘(Erdos1085.f 2 n)
problems.docstring: ErdЕ‘s showed $f_2(n) > n^{1+c/\log\log n}$ for some $c > 0$.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {d : в„•},
  5 вүӨ d вҶ’
    Odd d вҶ’
      вҲғ cвӮҒ > 0,
        вҲғ cвӮӮ,
          вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
            вҶ‘(d / 2 - 1) / (2 * вҶ‘(d / 2)) * вҶ‘n ^ 2 + cвӮҒ * вҶ‘n ^ (4 / 3) вүӨ вҶ‘(Erdos1085.f d n) вҲ§
              вҶ‘(Erdos1085.f d n) вүӨ вҶ‘(d / 2 - 1) / вҶ‘(d / 2) * вҶ‘n ^ 2 + cвӮӮ * вҶ‘n ^ (4 / 3)
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and Pach showed that, for $d \ge 5$ odd, there exist constants $c_1(d), c_2(d) > 0$
such that $\frac{p - 1}{2p} n^2 - c_1 n^{4/3} вүӨ f_d(n) \le \frac{p - 1}{2p} n^2 + c_2 n^{4/3}$ where
$p = \lfloor\frac d2\rfloor$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ n вүҘ 1, вҲғ k вүҘ 1, вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.range k, (n + i) вҲЈ вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.range k, (n + k + i)
problems.docstring:
Is it true that for every $n \geq 1$ there is a $k$ such that
$$
  n(n + 1) \cdots (n + k - 1) \mid (n + k) \cdots (n + 2k - 1)?
$$

problems.statement: IsLeast {k | 1 вүӨ k вҲ§ вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.range k, (4 + i) вҲЈ вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.range k, (4 + k + i)} 207
problems.docstring:
Bhavik Mehta has computed the minimal such $k$ for $1 \leq n \leq 18$.
For example, the minimal $k$ for $n = 4$ is $207$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” {a | a вҲҲ Erdos1108.FactorialSums вҲ§ Erdos1108.IsPowerful a}.Finite
problems.docstring: Does the set $A = \left\{ \sum_{n\in S}n! : S\subset \mathbb{N}\text{ finite}\right\}$ of all finite sums of distinct factorials contain only finitely many powerful numbers?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ k вүҘ 2, {a | a вҲҲ Erdos1108.FactorialSums вҲ§ вҲғ m, m ^ k = a}.Finite
problems.docstring: For each $k \geq 2$, does the set $A = \left\{ \sum_{n\in S}n! : S\subset \mathbb{N}\text{ finite}\right\}$ of all finite sums of distinct factorials contain only finitely many $k$-th powers?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ Оұ > 0, вҲҖ ОІ > 0, Irrational (Оұ / ОІ) вҶ’ IsAddCompleteNatSeq' (Erdos354.FloorMultiples.interleave Оұ ОІ 2)
problems.docstring:
Let $\alpha,\beta\in \mathbb{R}_{>0}$ such that $\alpha/\beta$ is irrational. Is
\[\{ \lfloor \alpha\rfloor,\lfloor \gamma\alpha\rfloor,\lfloor \gamma^2\alpha\rfloor,\ldots\}\cup
\{ \lfloor \beta\rfloor,\lfloor \gamma\beta\rfloor,\lfloor \gamma^2\beta\rfloor,\ldots\}\] complete?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ Оі вҲҲ Set.Ioo 1 2,
    вҲҖ Оұ > 0, вҲҖ ОІ > 0, Irrational (Оұ / ОІ) вҶ’ IsAddCompleteNatSeq' (Erdos354.FloorMultiples.interleave Оұ ОІ 2)
problems.docstring:
Let $\alpha,\beta\in \mathbb{R}_{>0}$ such that $\alpha/\beta$ is irrational. Is
\[\{ \lfloor \alpha\rfloor,\lfloor \gamma\alpha\rfloor,\lfloor \gamma^2\alpha\rfloor,\ldots\}\cup
\{ \lfloor \beta\rfloor,\lfloor \gamma\beta\rfloor,\lfloor \gamma^2\beta\rfloor,\ldots\}\] complete?

problems.statement: Erdos340.greedySidon 1 = 2
Erdos340.greedySidon 2 = 4
Erdos340.greedySidon 4 = 13
вҶ‘(Erdos340.greedySidon.goвңқ {1, 2} вӢҜ 3) = 4
вҶ‘(Erdos340.greedySidon.goвңқ {1} вӢҜ 2) = 2
Erdos340.greedySidon 3 = 8
Erdos340.greedySidon 5 = 21
вҲҖ Оө > 0, (fun n => вҶ‘(Set.range Erdos340.greedySidon вҲ© Set.Icc 1 n).ncard) =Оҳ[Filter.atTop] sorry
problems.docstring:
Let $A = \{1, 2, 4, 8, 13, 21, 31, 45, 66, 81, 97, \ldots\}$ be the greedy Sidon sequence:
we begin with $1$ and iteratively include the next smallest integer that preserves the
Sidon property (i.e. there are no non-trivial solutions to $a + b = c + d$). What is the
order of growth of $A$? Is it true that $|A \cap \{1, \ldots, N\}| \gg N^{1/2 - \varepsilon}$
for all $\varepsilon > 0$ and large $N$?

problems.statement: вҲҖ Оө > 0, (fun n => вҲҡвҶ‘n / вҶ‘n ^ Оө) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Set.range Erdos340.greedySidon вҲ© Set.Icc 1 n).ncard
problems.docstring:
Let $A = \{1, 2, 4, 8, 13, 21, 31, 45, 66, 81, 97, \ldots\}$ be the greedy Sidon sequence:
we begin with $1$ and iteratively include the next smallest integer that preserves the
Sidon property (i.e. there are no non-trivial solutions to $a + b = c + d$). What is the
order of growth of $A$? Is it true that $|A \cap \{1, \ldots, N\}| \gg N^{1/2 - \varepsilon}$
for all $\varepsilon > 0$ and large $N$?

problems.statement: вҲҖ Оө > 0, (fun n => вҶ‘n ^ (1 / 3)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Set.range Erdos340.greedySidon вҲ© Set.Icc 1 n).ncard
problems.docstring: It is trivial that this sequence grows at least like $\gg N^{1/3}$.

problems.statement: Erdos340.greedySidon 0 = 1
(Set.range Erdos340.greedySidon - Set.range Erdos340.greedySidon).HasPosDensity
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and Graham [ErGr80] also asked about the difference set $A - A$ and whether this has
positive density.

[ErGr80] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Graham, R., Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number
theory. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.cofinite, n вҲҲ Set.range Erdos340.greedySidon - Set.range Erdos340.greedySidon
problems.docstring: It may be true that all or almost all integers are in $A - A$.

problems.statement: Erdos340.greedySidon 10 = 97
True вҶ” вҲғ S, S.HasDensity 0 вҲ§ вҲҖ n вҲҲ Sб¶ң, n вҲҲ Set.range Erdos340.greedySidon - Set.range Erdos340.greedySidon
problems.docstring: It may be true that all or almost all integers are in $A - A$.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ A n,
    A.Infinite вҲ§
      (вҲҖ (a : вҶ‘A), IsLeast (Nat.totient вҒ»В№' {вҶ‘a}) (n a)) вҲ§
        Filter.Tendsto (fun a => вҶ‘(n a) / вҶ‘вҶ‘a) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Is there an infinite set $A \subset \mathbb{N}$ such that for every $a \in A$,
there is an integer n such that $\phi(n)=a$, and
yet if $n_a$ is the smallest such integer, then $\frac{n_a}{a} вҶ’ \infty$ as $a вҶ’ вҲһ$?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
    вҲҖ (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))),
      A.card = n вҶ’
        Erdos99.HasMinDist1 A вҶ’
          IsMinOn (fun B => Metric.diam вҶ‘B) {B | B.card = n вҲ§ Erdos99.HasMinDist1 B} A вҶ’
            вҲғ p вҲҲ A, вҲғ q вҲҲ A, вҲғ r вҲҲ A, Erdos99.FormsEquilateralTriangle p q r
problems.docstring:
For sufficiently large n, is it the case that any set of n points with minimum distance $1$
that minimizes diameter must contain an equilateral triangle of side length 1?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, Erdos85.f n вүӨ Erdos85.f (n + 1)
problems.docstring: Is it true that, for all large $n$, $f(n + 1) \ge f(n)$?

problems.statement: (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos920.f 3 n)) =Оҳ[Filter.atTop] fun n => (вҶ‘n / Real.log вҶ‘n) ^ (1 / 2)
problems.docstring: It is known that $f_3(n)\asymp (n/\log n)^{1/2}$ (see [erdosproblems.com/1104]).

problems.statement:
вҲҖ k вүҘ 3,
  (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos920.f k n)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n =>
    (вҶ‘n * Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘n) / Real.log вҶ‘n) ^ (1 - 1 / (вҶ‘k - 1))
problems.docstring:
Graver and Yackel [GrYa68] proved that
$f_k(n) \ll \left(n\frac{\log\log n}{\log n}\right)^{1-\frac{1}{k-1}}.$

problems.statement: (fun n => вҶ‘n ^ (2 / 3) / Real.log вҶ‘n ^ (4 / 3)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos920.f 4 n)
problems.docstring:
The lower bound $R(4,m) \gg m^3/(\log m)^4$ of Mattheus and Verstraete [MaVe23]
(see [erdosproblems.com/166]) implies $f_4(n) \gg \frac{n^{2/3}}{(\log n)^{4/3}}$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ k вүҘ 3, вҲғ c > 0, (fun n => вҶ‘n ^ (1 - 2 / (вҶ‘k + 1)) / Real.log вҶ‘n ^ c) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos920.f k n)
problems.docstring:
A positive answer to this question would follow from [erdosproblems.com/986]. The known bounds for
that problem imply $f_k(n) \gg \frac{n^{1-\frac{2}{k+1}}}{(\log n)^{c_k}}.$

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ k вүҘ 4, вҲғ c > 0, (fun n => вҶ‘n ^ (1 - 1 / (вҶ‘k - 1)) / Real.log вҶ‘n ^ c) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos920.f k n)
problems.docstring:
Is it true that, for $k\geq 4$, $f_k(n) \gg \frac{n^{1-\frac{1}{k-1}}}{(\log n)^{c_k}}$ for some
constant $c_k>0$?

problems.statement: True вҶ” Erdos567.K33.IsRamseySizeLinear
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘s Problem 567 (K33)**

Is $K_{3,3}$ Ramsey size linear?

problems.statement: True вҶ” Erdos567.H5.IsRamseySizeLinear
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘s Problem 567 (H5)**

Is $H_5$ ($C_5$ with two vertex-disjoint chords) Ramsey size linear?

problems.statement: True вҶ” Erdos567.Q3.IsRamseySizeLinear
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘s Problem 567 (Q3)**

Is $Q_3$ (the 3-dimensional hypercube) Ramsey size linear?

problems.statement: True вҶ” {n | n.centralBinom.Coprime 105}.Infinite
problems.docstring: Are there infinitely many $n$ such that ${2n\choose n}$ is coprime to $105$?

problems.statement: вҲҖ {p q : в„•}, Nat.Prime p вҶ’ Odd p вҶ’ Nat.Prime q вҶ’ Odd q вҶ’ {n | n.centralBinom.Coprime (p * q)}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s, Graham, Ruzsa, and Straus [EGRS75] have shown that, for any two odd primes $p$ and $q$,
there are infinite many $n$ such that ${2n\choose n}$ is coprime to $pq$.

problems.statement:
вҲғ c > 0,
  вҲҖ (n k : в„•),
    вҶ‘k < c * (Real.log вҶ‘n / Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘n)) ^ 3 вҶ’
      (вҲҖ i < k, В¬Nat.Prime (n + i + 1)) вҶ’ вҲғ p, Function.Injective p вҲ§ вҲҖ (i : Fin k), Nat.Prime (p i) вҲ§ p i вҲЈ n + вҶ‘i + 1
problems.docstring:
There exists a constant `c > 0` such that for all `n`, if
`k < c * (log n / (log (log n))) ^ 3 вҶ’ (вҲҖ i < k, В¬ (n + i + 1).Prime)`, then
there are distinct primes `pвӮҒ, ... pвӮ–` such that `pбөў вҲЈ n + i` for all `1 вүӨ i вүӨ k`. This is proved
in [RST75]. There is no need to only consider sufficiently large `n` because one can always take
`c` small enough so that `k < c * (log n / (log (log n))) ^ 3` implies that `k = 0` until `n` is
large.

problems.statement:
Erdos375.Erdos375Prop вҶ’
  вҲғ c > 0,
    вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
      вҶ‘(Nat.nth Nat.Prime (n + 1)) - вҶ‘(Nat.nth Nat.Prime n) < вҶ‘(Nat.nth Nat.Prime n) ^ (1 / 2 - c)
problems.docstring:
If `Erdos375Prop` is true, then `(n + 1).nth Prime - n.nth Prime < (n.nth Prime) ^ (1 / 2 - c)`
for some `c > 0`.

problems.statement: Erdos375.Erdos375Prop вҶ’ вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҲғ p вҲҲ Set.Ioo (n ^ 2) ((n + 1) ^ 2), Nat.Prime p
problems.docstring: In particular, if `Erdos375Prop` is true, then Legendre's conjecture is asymptotically true.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ n вүҘ 1,
  вҲҖ k вүӨ 2,
    (вҲҖ i < k, В¬Nat.Prime (n + i + 1)) вҶ’ вҲғ p, Function.Injective p вҲ§ вҲҖ (i : Fin k), Nat.Prime (p i) вҲ§ p i вҲЈ n + вҶ‘i + 1
problems.docstring:
It is easy to see that for any `n вүҘ 1` and `k вүӨ 2`, if `n + 1, ..., n + k` are all composite,
then there are distinct primes `pвӮҒ, ... pвӮ–` such that `pбөў вҲЈ n + i` for all `1 вүӨ i вүӨ k`.

problems.statement: True вҶ” Erdos375.Erdos375Prop
problems.docstring: Is `Erdos375Prop` true?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (A : Set в„•),
  (Filter.Tendsto (fun x => (вҶ‘(A вҲ© Set.Icc 1 (2 * x)).ncard - вҶ‘(A вҲ© Set.Icc 1 x).ncard) / Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘x))
        Filter.atTop Filter.atTop вҲ§
      вҲҖ (Оё : в„қ), 0 < Оё вҶ’ Оё < 1 вҶ’ В¬Summable fun n => distToNearestInt (Оё * вҶ‘вҶ‘n) ^ 2) вҶ’
    вҲҖб¶  (m : в„•) in Filter.atTop, Erdos254.IsSumOfDistinct A m
problems.docstring:
Cassels [Ca60] proved this under the alternative hypotheses $\lim \frac{\lvert A\cap [1,2x]\rvert
-\lvert A\cap [1,x]\rvert}{\log\log x}=\infty$ and $\sum_{n\in A} \{ \theta n\}^2=\infty$ for every
$\theta\in (0,1)$.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (A : Set в„•),
  (Filter.Tendsto (fun x => (A вҲ© Set.Icc 1 (2 * x)).ncard - (A вҲ© Set.Icc 1 x).ncard) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop вҲ§
      вҲҖ (Оё : в„қ), 0 < Оё вҶ’ Оё < 1 вҶ’ В¬Summable fun n => distToNearestInt (Оё * вҶ‘вҶ‘n)) вҶ’
    вҲҖб¶  (m : в„•) in Filter.atTop, Erdos254.IsSumOfDistinct A m
problems.docstring:
Let $A\subseteq \mathbb{N}$ be such that $\lvert A\cap [1,2x]\rvert -\lvert A\cap [1,x]\rvert \to
\infty\textrm{ as }x\to \infty$ and $\sum_{n\in A} \{ \theta n\}=\infty$ for every $\theta\in
(0,1)$, where $\{x\}$ is the distance of $x$ from the nearest integer. Then every sufficiently large
integer is the sum of distinct elements of $A$.

problems.statement: 0 < {n | (Erdos358.intervalRepresentations (Nat.nth Nat.Prime) n).Nonempty}.upperDensity
problems.docstring:
When $A =\{a_1 < \cdots\}$ corresponds to the set of primes, it is conjectured that the set of
numbers $n$ that have representations \[n=\sum_{u\leq i\leq v}a_i\] has positive upper density.

problems.statement: Filter.limsup (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos358.f (Nat.nth Nat.Prime) n)) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ
problems.docstring:
When $A =\{a_1 < \cdots\}$ corresponds to the set of primes, it is conjectured that the
$\limsup$ of the number of representations \[n=\sum_{u\leq i\leq v}a_i\] is infinite.

problems.statement: Erdos358.f id = fun n => {d вҲҲ n.divisors | Odd d}.card
problems.docstring: When $A_n = n$, the function $f$ defined above counts the number of odd divisors of $n$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ A, StrictMono A вҲ§ Filter.Tendsto (Erdos358.f A) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Let $A=\{a_1 < \cdots\}$ be an infinite sequence of integers. Let $f(n)$ count the number of
solutions to \[n=\sum_{u\leq i\leq v}a_i.\]
Is there such an $A$ for which $f(n)\to \infty$ as $n\to \infty$?

problems.statement: вҲғ A, StrictMono A вҲ§ вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, 1 вүӨ Erdos358.g A n
problems.docstring:
It is conjectured that if $A =\{a_1 < \cdots\}$ and $g$ counts the number of representations
\[n=\sum_{u\leq i\leq v}a_i\] such that the sum has at least two terms, then for all $n$ we have
$1 \leq g(n)$ for sufficiently large $n$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ A, StrictMono A вҲ§ вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, 2 вүӨ Erdos358.f A n
problems.docstring:
Let $A=\{a_1 < \cdots\}$ be an infinite sequence of integers. Let $f(n)$ count the number of
solutions to \[n=\sum_{u\leq i\leq v}a_i.\]
Is there an $A$ such that $f(n)\geq 2$ for all large $n$?

problems.statement: вЁҶ f, Filter.liminf (fun r => Erdos513.ratio r вҶ‘f) Filter.atTop > 1 / 2
problems.docstring: For all transcendental entire function `f`, `liminf (fun r : в„қ => ratio r f) atTop > 1 / 2`.

problems.statement: sorry = вЁҶ f, Filter.liminf (fun r => Erdos513.ratio r вҶ‘f) Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Let `f` be a transcendental entire function. What is the greatest possible value of
`liminf (fun r : в„қ => ratio r f) atTop`?

problems.statement: вҲғ c > 0, вЁҶ f, Filter.liminf (fun r => Erdos513.ratio r вҶ‘f) Filter.atTop вүӨ 2 / Real.pi - c
problems.docstring:
For all transcendental entire function `f`, `liminf (fun r : в„қ => ratio r f) atTop вүӨ 2 / ПҖ - c`
for some `c > 0`. This is proved in [ClHa64].

problems.statement:
вҲғ c,
  вҲғ (_ : 0 < c),
    вҲҖб¶  (N : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
      вҲҖ (A : Finset в„•), 0 вҲү A вҶ’ A.card = N вҶ’ вҲғ Оё, вҲ‘ n вҲҲ A, Real.cos (вҶ‘n * Оё) < -c * вҶ‘N ^ (1 / 7)
problems.docstring: Bedert [Be25c] proved an upper bound of $-c N^{1/7}$.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ c,
    вҲғ (_ : 0 < c),
      вҲҖб¶  (N : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҲҖ (A : Finset в„•), 0 вҲү A вҶ’ A.card = N вҶ’ вҲғ Оё, вҲ‘ n вҲҲ A, Real.cos (вҶ‘n * Оё) < -c * вҲҡвҶ‘N
problems.docstring:
**Chowla's cosine problem**

If $A\subset \mathbb{N}$ is a finite set of positive integers of size $N > 0$ then is there some
absolute constant $c>0$ and $\theta$ such that
$$\sum_{n\in A}\cos(n\theta) < -cN^{1/2}?$$

problems.statement:
вҲғ c,
  вҲғ (_ : 0 < c),
    вҲҖб¶  (N : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
      вҲҖ (A : Finset в„•), 0 вҲү A вҶ’ A.card = N вҶ’ вҲғ Оё, вҲ‘ n вҲҲ A, Real.cos (вҶ‘n * Оё) < -Real.exp (c * вҲҡ(Real.log вҶ‘N))
problems.docstring: Ruzsa [Ru04] proved an upper bound of $-\exp(O(\sqrt{\log N})$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), A.upperDensity > 0 вҶ’ вҲғ B C, B.Infinite вҲ§ C.Infinite вҲ§ B + C вҠҶ A
problems.docstring:
Any $A\subseteq \mathbb{N}$ of positive upper density contains a sumset $B+C$ where both $B$ and $C$
are infinite.

The ErdЕ‘s sumset conjecture. Proved by Moreira, Richter, and Robertson [MRR19].

Let $c > 0$ and $n$ be some large integer. What is the size of the largest set
$A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, \lfloor c n \rfloor\}$ such that $n$ is not a sum of a subset of $A$?
Does this depend on $n$ in an irregular way?

Let $c > 0$ and $n$ be some large integer. What is the size of the largest set
$A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, \lfloor c n \rfloor\}$ such that $n$ is not a sum of a subset of $A$?
Does this depend on $n$ in an irregular way?

        (fun n => вҶ‘(A n)) =o[Filter.atTop] sorry

Let $c > 0$ and $n$ be some large integer. What is the size of the largest set
$A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, \lfloor c n \rfloor\}$ such that $n$ is not a sum of a subset of $A$?
Does this depend on $n$ in an irregular way?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (d : в„•), Erdos1059.DecidableAllFactorialSubtractionsComposite d вҶ” Erdos1059.AllFactorialSubtractionsComposite d
В¬Erdos1059.AllFactorialSubtractionsComposite 89
вҲҖ (d : в„•), Erdos1059.IsFactorial d вҶ” Erdos1059.DecidableIsFactorial d
Erdos1059.AllFactorialSubtractionsComposite 211
вҲҖ (n : в„•), Erdos1059.factorialsLessThanN n = вҶ‘(Erdos1059.decidableFactorialsLessThanN n)
True вҶ” {p | Nat.Prime p вҲ§ Erdos1059.AllFactorialSubtractionsComposite p}.Infinite
problems.docstring: Are there infinitely many primes $p$ such that $p - k!$ is composite for each $k$ such that $1 вүӨ k! < p$?

problems.statement: Erdos1059.AllFactorialSubtractionsComposite 101
Erdos1059.factorialsLessThanN 100 = {1, 2, 6, 24}

problems.docstring: Are there infinitely many binomial coefficients with deficiency 1?
Are there only finitely many binomial coefficients with deficiency > 1?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), (n + 1).choose 2 вүӨ sSup {x | вҲғ A, вҲғ (_ : A.IsIsosceles), A.ncard = x}
problems.docstring:
Alweiss has observed a lower bound of $\binom{n + 1}{2}$ follows from considering the subset of
$\mathbb{R}^{n + 1}$ formed of all vectors $e_i + e_j$ where $e_i$, $e_j$ are distinct coordinate
vectors. This set can be viewed as a subset of some $\mathbb{R}^n$, and is easily checked to have
the required property.

problems.statement: IsGreatest {x | вҲғ A, вҲғ (_ : A.IsIsosceles), A.ncard = x} 8
problems.docstring:
When $n = 3$, the answer is 8 (due to Croft [Cr62]).

[Cr62] Croft, H. T., $9$-point and $7$-point configurations in $3$-space. Proc. London Math. Soc. (3) (1962), 400-424.

problems.statement: IsGreatest {x | вҲғ A, вҲғ (_ : A.IsIsosceles), A.ncard = x} 6
problems.docstring:
When $n = 2$, the answer is 6 (due to Kelly [ErKe47] - an alternative proof is given by KovГЎcs [Ko24c]).

[ErKe47] ErdЕ‘s, Paul and Kelly, L. M., Elementary Problems and Solutions: Solutions: E735. Amer. Math. Monthly (1947), 227-229.
[Ko24c] Z. KovГЎcs, A note on ErdЕ‘s's mysterious remark. arXiv:2412.05190 (2024).

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n m : в„•), m вҲҲ {x | вҲғ A, вҲғ (_ : A.IsIsosceles), A.ncard = x} вҶ’ m вүӨ (n + 2).choose 2
problems.docstring:
The best upper bound known in general is due to Blokhius [Bl84] who showed that
$$
|A| \leq \binom{n + 2}{2}
$$

[Bl84] Blokhuis, A., Few-distance sets. (1984), iv+70.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), IsGreatest {x | вҲғ A, вҲғ (_ : A.IsIsosceles), A.ncard = x} sorry
problems.docstring:
What is the size of the largest $A \subseteq \mathbb{R}^n$ such that every three points from $A$
determine an isosceles triangle? That is, for any three points $x$, $y$, $z$ from $A$, at least two
of the distances $|x - y|$, $|y - z|$, $|x - z|$ are equal.

problems.statement: вҲғ c > 0, Asymptotics.IsEquivalent Filter.atTop (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos912.h n)) fun n => c * (вҶ‘n / Real.log вҶ‘n) ^ (1 / 2)
problems.docstring:
Prove that there exists some $c>0$ such that
$$h(n) \sim c \left(\frac{n}{\log n}\right)^{1/2}$$
as $n\to \infty$.

problems.statement: (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos912.h n)) =Оҳ[Filter.atTop] fun n => (вҶ‘n / Real.log вҶ‘n) ^ (1 / 2)
problems.docstring: ErdЕ‘s and Selfridge prove in [Er82c] that $h(n) \asymp \left(\frac{n}{\log n}\right)^{1/2}$.

problems.statement: Asymptotics.IsEquivalent Filter.atTop (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos912.h n)) fun n => вҲҡ(2 * Real.pi) * (вҶ‘n / Real.log вҶ‘n) ^ (1 / 2)
problems.docstring:
A heuristic of Tao using the CramГ©r model for the primes suggests this is true with
$c=\sqrt{2\pi}$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ Оө > 0, Оө вүӨ 1 вҶ’ вҲғ A Оҙ, 0 вҲү A вҲ§ 1 - Оө < Оҙ вҲ§ A.HasDensity Оҙ вҲ§ Erdos786.Set.IsMulCardSet A
problems.docstring:
Let $\epsilon > 0$. Is there some set $A\subset\mathbb{N}$ of density $> 1 - \epsilon$
such that $a_1\cdots a_r = b_1\cdots b_s$ with $a_i, b_j\in A$ can only hold when
$r = s$?

problems.statement: Erdos786.consecutivePrimesFrom 2 1 = {2, 3}

True вҶ”
  вҲғ A f,
    вҲғ (_ : f =o[Filter.atTop] 1),
      вҲҖ (N : в„•), A N вҠҶ Set.Icc 1 (N + 1) вҲ§ (1 - f N) * вҶ‘N вүӨ вҶ‘(A N).ncard вҲ§ Erdos786.Set.IsMulCardSet (A N)
problems.docstring:
Is there some set $A\subset\{1, ..., N\}$ of size $\geq (1 - o(1))N$ such that
$a_1\cdots a_r = b_1\cdots b_s$ with $a_i, b_j\in A$ can only hold when
$r = s$?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (Оө : в„қ),
  0 < Оө вҲ§ Оө < 1 / Real.exp 1 вҶ’
    вҲҖб¶  (p : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
      Nat.Prime p вҶ’
        вҲғ k,
          вҲ‘ q вҲҲ Erdos786.consecutivePrimesFrom p k, 1 / вҶ‘q < 1 вҲ§
            1 < вҲ‘ q вҲҲ Erdos786.consecutivePrimesFrom p (k + 1), 1 / вҶ‘q вҲ§
              {n | вҲ‘ q вҲҲ Erdos786.consecutivePrimesFrom p k, n.factorization q = 1}.HasDensity (1 / Real.exp 1 - Оө) вҲ§
                Erdos786.Set.IsMulCardSet {n | вҲ‘ q вҲҲ Erdos786.consecutivePrimesFrom p k, n.factorization q = 1}
problems.docstring:
Let $\epsilon > 0$ be given. Then, for a sufficiently large prime `p`, take the sequence of
consecutive primes $p_1 < \cdots < p_k$ such that
$$
\sum_{i=1}^k \frac{1}{p_i} < 1 < \sum_{i=1}^{k + 1} \frac{1}{p_i},
$$
and let $A$ be the set of all naturals divisible by exactly one of $p_1, ..., p_k$ (with
multiplicity $1$). Then $A$ has density $\frac{1}{e} - \epsilon$ and has the property
that $a_1\cdots a_r = b_1\cdots b_s$ with $a_i, b_j\in A$ can only hold when $r = s$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {p : в„•}, Nat.Prime p вҶ’ Nat.nth (fun q => Nat.Prime q вҲ§ p вүӨ q) 0 = p
вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), A = {n | n % 4 = 2} вҶ’ A.HasDensity (1 / 4) вҲ§ Erdos786.Set.IsMulCardSet A
problems.docstring: An example of such a set with density $\frac 1 4$ is given by the integers $\equiv 2\pmod{4}$

problems.statement: вҲҖ {p : в„•}, Nat.Prime p вҶ’ Erdos786.consecutivePrimesFrom p 0 = {p}
True вҶ” вҲғ o, o =o[Filter.atTop] 1 вҲ§ вҲҖб¶  (x : в„қ) in Filter.atTop, x ^ (1 - o x) < Erdos830.A x
problems.docstring:
**Erdos Problem 830, Part 2**
We say that $a,b\in \mathbb{N}$ are an amicable pair if $\sigma(a)=\sigma(b)=a+b$.
If $A(x)$ counts the number of amicable $1\leq a\leq b\leq x$ then is it true that
\[A(x) > x^{1-o(1)}?\]

problems.statement: вҲҖб¶  (x : в„қ) in Filter.atTop, Erdos830.A x вүӨ x * Real.exp (-Real.nthRoot 3 (Real.log x))
problems.docstring:
We say that $a,b\in \mathbb{N}$ are an amicable pair if $\sigma(a)=\sigma(b)=a+b$.
If $A(x)$ counts the number of amicable $1\leq a\leq b\leq x$ then one can show that
$A(x) \leq x \exp(-(\log x)^{1/3})$.

problems.docstring:
**Erdos Problem 830, Part 1**
We say that $a,b\in \mathbb{N}$ are an amicable pair if $\sigma(a)=\sigma(b)=a+b$. Are there
infinitely many amicable pairs?

problems.statement: Erdos830.A =o[Filter.atTop] id
problems.docstring:
We say that $a,b\in \mathbb{N}$ are an amicable pair if $\sigma(a)=\sigma(b)=a+b$.
If $A(x)$ counts the number of amicable $1\leq a\leq b\leq x$ then one can show that $A(x) = o(x)$.

problems.statement:
вҲғ o,
  o =o[Filter.atTop] 1 вҲ§
    вҲҖб¶  (x : в„қ) in Filter.atTop, Erdos830.A x вүӨ x * Real.exp (-(1 / 2 + o x) * вҲҡ(Real.log x * Real.log (Real.log x)))
problems.docstring:
We say that $a,b\in \mathbb{N}$ are an amicable pair if $\sigma(a)=\sigma(b)=a+b$.
If $A(x)$ counts the number of amicable $1\leq a\leq b\leq x$ then one can show that
$A(x) \leq x \exp(-(\tfrac{1}{2}+o(1))(\log x\log\log x)^{1/2})$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” Irrational (вҲ‘' (n : в„•), вҶ‘(Nat.nth Nat.Prime n) / 2 ^ n)
problems.docstring: Is $\sum_{n=1}^\infty \frac{p_n}{2^n}$ irrational? Here $p_n$ is the $n$-th prime ($p_1=2, p_2=3, \dots$).

problems.statement:
False вҶ”
  вҲҖ k вүҘ 3,
    вҲҖ (r : в„қ вҶ’ в„қ вҶ’ Prop),
      IsStrictTotalOrder в„қ r вҶ’ вҲғ s, s.IsAPOfLength k вҲ§ (List.Pairwise r s вҲЁ List.Pairwise (flip r) s)
problems.docstring:
Let $k\geq 3$. Must any ordering of $\mathbb{R}$ contain a monotone $k$-term arithmetic progression,
that is, some $x_1 <\cdots < x_k$ which forms an increasing or decreasing $k$-term arithmetic
progression?

The answer is no, even for $k=3$, as shown by Ardal, Brown, and JungiДҮ [ABJ11].

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ L,
    Filter.Tendsto
      (fun x => вҶ‘(Nat.totient '' {m | 1 вүӨ m вҲ§ вҶ‘m вүӨ x}).ncard / вҶ‘{k | k вҲҲ Set.range Nat.totient вҲ§ вҶ‘k вүӨ x}.ncard)
      Filter.atTop (nhds L)
problems.docstring:
Let\[V'(x)=\#\{\phi(m) : 1\leq m\leq x\}\]and\[V(x)=\#\{\phi(m) \leq x : 1\leq m\}.\]
Does $\lim V(x)/V'(x)$ exist?

Formalization note: We formalize the limit of the inverse fraction V'(x)/V(x)
to ensure the limit is finite (bounded between 0 and 1).

problems.statement:

  вҲғ L < 1,
    Filter.Tendsto
      (fun x => вҶ‘(Nat.totient '' {m | 1 вүӨ m вҲ§ вҶ‘m вүӨ x}).ncard / вҶ‘{k | k вҲҲ Set.range Nat.totient вҲ§ вҶ‘k вүӨ x}.ncard)
      Filter.atTop (nhds L)
problems.docstring: Is it $>1$?

From [Kanold]: diameter $\geq n^{3/4}$.

problems.docstring: Is the diameter of $A$ at least $Cn$ for some constant $C > 0$?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ k вүҘ 2,
  вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
    вҲғ m вҲҲ Finset.Ico n (n + (вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.range (k - 1), Nat.nth Nat.Prime i) * Nat.nth Nat.Prime k),
      k < ArithmeticFunction.cardDistinctFactors m
problems.docstring:
Schinzel deduced from PГіlya's theorem [Po18] (that the sequence of $k$-smooth integers has unbounded
gaps) that this is true with $p_1\cdots p_k$ replaced by $p_1\cdots p_{k-1}p_{k+1}$.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ k вүҘ 2,
    вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
      вҲғ m вҲҲ Finset.Ico n (n + вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.range k, Nat.nth Nat.Prime i), k < ArithmeticFunction.cardDistinctFactors m
problems.docstring:
Let $2=p_1 < p_2 < \cdots$ be the primes and $k\geq 2$. Is it true that, for all sufficiently large
$n$, there must exist an integer in $[n,n+p_1\cdots p_k)$ with $>k$ many prime factors?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ k вүҘ 2,
  вҲғб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
    вҲҖ m вҲҲ Finset.Ico n (n + вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.range k, Nat.nth Nat.Prime i - 1), ArithmeticFunction.cardDistinctFactors m вүӨ k
problems.docstring:
Weisenberg has observed that Dickson's conjecture implies the answer is no if we replace
$p_1\cdots p_k$ with $p_1\cdots p_k-1$. Indeed, let $L_k$ be the lowest common multiple of all
integers at most $p_1\cdots p_k$. By Dickson's conjecture [Wikipedia], there are infinitely many
$n'$ such that $\frac{L_k}{m}n'+1$ is prime for all $1\leq m < p_1\cdots p_k$. It follows that,
if $n=L_kn'+1$, then all integers in $[n,n+p_1\cdots p_k-1)$ have at most $k$ prime factors.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҲғ m вҲҲ Finset.Ico n (n + 6), 3 вүӨ ArithmeticFunction.cardDistinctFactors m
problems.docstring:
This is unknown even for $k=2$ - that is, is it true that in every interval of $6$
(sufficiently large) consecutive integers there must exist one with at least $3$ prime factors?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (A : Set в„•),
  Erdos32.IsAdditiveComplementToPrimes A вҶ’
    1 < Filter.liminf (fun N => вҶ‘{x вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 N | x вҲҲ A}.card / вҶ‘(Real.log вҶ‘N)) Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Must every additive complement $A$ to the primes satisfy
$\liminf_{N \to \infty} \frac{|A \cap \{1, \ldots, N\}|}{\log N} > 1$?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ A,
    Erdos32.IsAdditiveComplementToPrimes A вҲ§
      (fun N => вҶ‘{x вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 N | x вҲҲ A}.card) =O[Filter.atTop] fun N => Real.log вҶ‘N
problems.docstring:
Can the bound $O(\log N)$ be achieved for an additive complement to the primes? [Guy04] writes
that ErdЕ‘s offered \$50 for the solution.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ A,
    Erdos32.IsAdditiveComplementToPrimes A вҲ§
      (fun N => вҶ‘{x вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 N | x вҲҲ A}.card) =o[Filter.atTop] fun N => Real.log вҶ‘N ^ 2
problems.docstring:
Does there exist a set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ such that $|A \cap \{1, \ldots, N\}| = o((\log N)^2)$
and every sufficiently large integer can be written as $p + a$ for some prime $p$ and $a \in A$?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (A : Set в„•),
  Erdos32.IsAdditiveComplementToPrimes A вҶ’
    вҶ‘(Real.exp Real.eulerMascheroniConstant) вүӨ
      Filter.liminf (fun N => вҶ‘{x вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 N | x вҲҲ A}.card / вҶ‘(Real.log вҶ‘N)) Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Ruzsa proved that any additive complement $A$ to the primes must satisfy
$\liminf_{N \to \infty} \frac{|A \cap \{1, \ldots, N\}|}{\log N} \geq e^\gamma$,
where $\gamma$ is the Euler-Mascheroni constant.

problems.statement:
вҲғ A,
  Erdos32.IsAdditiveComplementToPrimes A вҲ§
    (fun N => вҶ‘{x вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 N | x вҲҲ A}.card) =O[Filter.atTop] fun N => Real.log вҶ‘N ^ 2
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s proved in [Erd54] that there exists an additive complement $A$ to the primes with
$|A \cap \{1, \ldots, N\}| = O((\log N)^2)$.

problems.statement:
False вҶ”
  вҲғ n a,
    have D := {d | d вҲЈ n вҲ§ d > 1};
    (вҲҖ (x : в„Ө), вҲғ d вҲҲ D, x вүЎ a d [ZMOD вҶ‘d]) вҲ§
      вҲҖ d вҲҲ D, вҲҖ d' вҲҲ D, d вү  d' вҶ’ (вҲғ x, x вүЎ a d [ZMOD вҶ‘d] вҶ’ x вүЎ a d' [ZMOD вҶ‘d']) вҶ’ d.gcd d' = 1
problems.docstring:
Are there $n$ such that there is a covering system with moduli the divisors of $n$ which is 'as
disjoint as possible'?

That is, for all $d\mid n$ with $d>1$ there is an associated $a_d$ such that every integer is
congruent to some $a_d\pmod{d}$, and if there is some integer $x$ with
\[x\equiv a_d\pmod{d}\textrm{ and }x\equiv a_{d'}\pmod{d'}\]then $(d,d')=1$.

The density of such $n$ is zero. ErdЕ‘s and Graham believed that no such $n$ exist.

Adenwalla [Ad25] has proved there are no such $n$.

This was formalized by van Doorn in Lean using Aristotle.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (d : в„•),
  (interior {x | вҲғ A, A.Infinite вҲ§ (Summable fun n => 1 / вҶ‘вҶ‘n) вҲ§ x = fun i => вҲ‘' (n : вҶ‘A), 1 / (вҶ‘вҶ‘n + вҶ‘вҶ‘i)}).Nonempty
problems.docstring:
Let `X` be the set of points in `Fin d вҶ’ в„қ` of the shape
`fun i : Fin d => вҲ‘' n : A, (1 : в„қ) / (n + i)` for some infinite subset `A вҠҶ в„•` such that
`1 / n` is summable over `A`. `X` has nonempty interior. This is proved in [KoTa24].

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ n > 0, вҲғ! m, m.totient = n
problems.docstring:
Carmichael has asked whether there is an integer $n$ for which $\phi(m) = n$ has
exactly one solution, that is $\frac{f_\max(n)}{f_\min(n)} = 1$.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (max min : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
  (вҲҖ (n : в„•), IsGreatest (Nat.totient вҒ»В№' {n}) (max n)) вҶ’
    (вҲҖ (n : в„•), IsLeast (Nat.totient вҒ»В№' {n}) (min n)) вҶ’
      вҲҖ (x : в„•), IsGreatest {x_1 | вҲғ n, вҲғ (_ : n вүӨ x), вҶ‘(max n) / вҶ‘(min n) = x_1} sorry
problems.docstring:
Let $f_\max(n)$ be the largest $m$ such that $\phi(m) = n$, and
$f_\min(n)$ be the smallest such $m$, where $\phi$ is Euler's
totient function. Investigate
$$
  \max_{n\leq x}\frac{f_\max(n)}{f_\min(n)}.
$$

problems.statement: (вҲғ n > 0, вҲғ! m, m.totient = n) вҶ’ {n | вҲғ! m, m.totient = n}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s has proved that if there exists an integer $n$ for which $\phi(m) = n$ has
exactly one solution, then there must be infinitely many such $n$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), вҶ‘(Erdos385.F n) вүӨ вҶ‘n + вҲҡвҶ‘n
problems.docstring: Note that trivially $F(n) \leq n + \sqrt{n}$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ e, вҲғ (_ : e =o[Filter.atTop] 1), вҲҖ (n : в„•), вҶ‘n + (1 - e n) * вҲҡвҶ‘n вүӨ вҶ‘(Erdos385.F n)
problems.docstring:
A question of ErdЕ‘s, Eggleton, and Selfridge, who write that in fact it is possible that
this quantity is always at least $n+(1-o(1))\sqrt{n}$

problems.statement: True вҶ” Filter.Tendsto (fun n => Erdos385.F n - n) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Let $F(n) := \max\{m + p(m) \mid  \textrm{$m < n$ composite}\}\}$ where $p(m)$ is the least
prime divisor of $m$. Does $F(n) - n \to \infty$ as $n\to\infty$?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, n < Erdos385.F n
problems.docstring:
Let $F(n) := \max\{m + p(m) \mid  \textrm{$m < n$ composite}\}\}$ where $p(m)$ is the least
prime divisor of $m$. Is it true that $F(n)>n$ for all sufficiently large $n$?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (f : в„• вҶ’ в„•) (S : Set в„•),
    S = {k | вҲғ n, StrictMono n вҲ§ 0 вҲү Set.range n вҲ§ 1 = вҲ‘ i, 1 / вҶ‘(n i)} вҶ’
      (вҲҖ k вҲҲ S,
          IsLeast {x | вҲғ n, вҲғ (_ : StrictMono n) (_ : 0 вҲү Set.range n) (_ : 1 = вҲ‘ i, 1 / вҶ‘(n i)), n (Fin.last k) = x}
            (f k)) вҶ’
        вҲғ o, вҲғ (_ : o =o[Filter.atTop] 1), вҲҖ k вҲҲ S, вҶ‘(f k) = (1 + o k) * Real.exp 1 / (Real.exp 1 - 1) * (вҶ‘k + 1)
problems.docstring:
Let $f(k)$ be the minimal value of $n_k$ such that there exist $n_1 < n_2 < \dots < n_k$ with
$$
  1 = \frac{1}{n_1} + \cdots + \frac{1}{n_k}.
$$
Is it true that
$$
  f(k) = (1 + o(1)) \frac{e}{e - 1} k ?
$$

Proved by Martin [Ma00].

[Ma00] Martin, Greg, _Denser Egyptian fractions_. Acta Arith. (2000), 231-260.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (f : в„• вҶ’ в„•) (S : Set в„•),
  S = {k | вҲғ n, StrictMono n вҲ§ 0 вҲү Set.range n вҲ§ 1 = вҲ‘ i, 1 / вҶ‘(n i)} вҶ’
    (вҲҖ k вҲҲ S,
        IsLeast {x | вҲғ n, вҲғ (_ : StrictMono n) (_ : 0 вҲү Set.range n) (_ : 1 = вҲ‘ i, 1 / вҶ‘(n i)), n (Fin.last k) = x}
          (f k)) вҶ’
      вҲғ o, вҲғ (_ : o =o[Filter.atTop] 1), вҲҖ k вҲҲ S, (1 + o k) * Real.exp 1 / (Real.exp 1 - 1) * (вҶ‘k + 1) вүӨ вҶ‘(f k)
problems.docstring: It is trivial that $f(k)\geq (1 + o(1)) \frac{e}{e - 1}k$.

problems.statement: Erdos1142.Erdos1142Prop 21
Erdos1142.Erdos1142Prop 75
Erdos1142.Erdos1142Prop 7
problems.docstring: $7$ satisfies the ErdЕ‘s 1142 property: $7 - 2 = 5$ and $7 - 4 = 3$ are prime.

problems.statement: Erdos1142.Erdos1142Prop 105
Erdos1142.Erdos1142Prop 4
problems.docstring: $4$ satisfies the ErdЕ‘s 1142 property: $4 - 2 = 2$ is prime.

problems.statement: True вҶ” Infinite вҶ‘{n | Erdos1142.Erdos1142Prop n}
problems.docstring:
Are there infinitely many $n > 2$ such that $n - 2^k$ is prime for all $k \geq 1$ with $2^k < n$?

The only known such $n$ are $4, 7, 15, 21, 45, 75, 105$ (OEIS [A039669](https://oeis.org/A039669)).

problems.statement: Erdos1142.Erdos1142Prop 45
Erdos1142.Erdos1142Prop 15

problems.docstring:
Mientka and Weitzenkamp [MiWe69] proved that the only $n \leq 2^{44}$ such that $n > 2$ and
$n - 2^k$ is prime for all $k \geq 1$ with $2^k < n$ are $4, 7, 15, 21, 45, 75, 105$.

problems.statement: В¬Erdos1142.Erdos1142Prop 106
problems.docstring: $106$ does not satisfy the ErdЕ‘s 1142 property ($106 - 2 = 104 = 8 \times 13$).

problems.statement: вҲҖ (k : в„•), 0 < k вҶ’ ((SimpleGraph.johnson (2 * k) k).chromaticNumber = вҶ‘k + 1 вҶ” Erdos835.Property k)

problems.docstring: Is the chromatic number of `J(2 * k, k)` always at least `k + 2`?

problems.statement: вҲҖ {n k : в„•}, вҶ‘вҢҲвҶ‘(n.choose k) / вҶ‘(Erdos835.johnsonBound n 4 k)вҢүвӮҠ вүӨ (SimpleGraph.johnson n k).chromaticNumber
problems.docstring: Johnson's bound for the chromatic number of the Johnson graph.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {k : в„•}, 3 вүӨ k вҶ’ k вүӨ 8 вҶ’ вҶ‘k + 1 < (SimpleGraph.johnson (2 * k) k).chromaticNumber
problems.docstring:
It is known that for $3 \leq k \leq 8$, the chromatic number of $J(2k, k)$ is greater than
$k+1$, see [Johnson graphs](https://aeb.win.tue.nl/graphs/Johnson.html).

problems.statement: вҲҖ (k : в„•), 2 < k вҶ’ (k + 1).Composite вҶ’ вҶ‘k + 1 < (SimpleGraph.johnson (2 * k) k).chromaticNumber
problems.docstring:
