Ma and Tang have proved that the chromatic number of $J(2k,k)$ is $>k+1$ for all $k>2$ not of the
form $p-1$ for prime $p$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (k : в„•), 3 вүӨ k вҶ’ k вүӨ 8 вҶ’ (SimpleGraph.johnson (2 * k) k).chromaticNumber > вҶ‘k + 1
problems.docstring:
It is known that for $3 \leq k \leq 8$, the chromatic number of $J(2k, k)$ is greater than $k+1$,
see [Johnson graphs](https://aeb.win.tue.nl/graphs/Johnson.html).

problems.statement: (SimpleGraph.johnson 18 9).chromaticNumber > 9 + 1
problems.docstring:
The smallest case not on this page is $k=9$:
But that one can be solved as well:
The chromatic number of $J(18, 9)$ is at least $11$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {k : в„•}, 3 вүӨ k вҶ’ k вүӨ 300 вҶ’ Odd k вҶ’ вҶ‘k + 1 < (SimpleGraph.johnson (2 * k) k).chromaticNumber
problems.docstring:
It is also known that for $3 \leq k \leq 203$ odd, the chromatic number of $J(2k, k)$ is
greater than $k+1$, see [Johnson graphs](https://aeb.win.tue.nl/graphs/Johnson.html).

problems.statement: (вҲғ l, (SimpleGraph.johnson (2 * (l + 3)) (l + 3)).chromaticNumber = вҶ‘(l + 3) + 1) вҶ” True
problems.docstring:
Alternative statement of ErdЕ‘s Problem 835 using the chromatic number of the Johnson graph.
This is equivalent to asking whether there exists $k > 2$ such that the chromatic number of the
Johnson graph $J(2k, k)$ is $k+1$.

problems.statement: (вҲғ k > 2, Erdos835.Property k) вҶ” True
problems.docstring:
Does there exist a $k>2$ such that the $k$-sized subsets of {1,...,2k} can be coloured with
$k+1$ colours such that for every $A\subset \{1,\ldots,2k\}$ with $\lvert A\rvert=k+1$ all $k+1$
colours appear among the $k$-sized subsets of $A$?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (ОівӮҖ : в„қ),
  ОівӮҖ = вҲ‘' (k : в„•), Real.log (вҶ‘k + 2) / 2 ^ (k + 2) вҶ’
    вҲғ o,
      вҲғ (_ : Filter.Tendsto o Filter.atTop (nhds 0)),
        вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.cofinite, Erdos377.sumInvPrimesNotDvdCentralBinom n = ОівӮҖ + o n
problems.docstring:
Erdos, Graham, Ruzsa, and Straus proved that if
$$
  f(n) = \sum_{p \leq n} 1_{p\nmid {2n \choose n}}\frac{1}{p}
$$
and
$$
  \gamma_0 = \sum_{k = 2}^{\infty} \frac{\log k}{2^k}
$$
then for almost all integers $f(m) = \gamma_0 + o(1)$.

[EGRS75] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Graham, R. L. and Ruzsa, I. Z. and Straus, E. G., _On the prime factors of $\binom{2n}{n}$_. Math. Comp. (1975), 83-92.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (ОівӮҖ : в„қ),
  ОівӮҖ = вҲ‘' (k : в„•), Real.log (вҶ‘k + 2) / 2 ^ (k + 2) вҶ’
    Filter.Tendsto (fun x => 1 / вҶ‘x * вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 x, Erdos377.sumInvPrimesNotDvdCentralBinom n ^ 2) Filter.atTop
      (nhds (ОівӮҖ ^ 2))
problems.docstring:
Erdos, Graham, Ruzsa, and Straus proved that if
$$
  f(n) = \sum_{p \leq n} 1_{p\nmid {2n \choose n}}\frac{1}{p}
$$
and
$$
  \gamma_0 = \sum_{k = 2}^{\infty} \frac{\log k}{2^k}
$$
then
$$
  \lim_{x\to\infty} \frac{1}{x}\sum_{n\leq x} f(n)^2 = \gamma_0^2
$$

[EGRS75] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Graham, R. L. and Ruzsa, I. Z. and Straus, E. G., _On the prime factors of $\binom{2n}{n}$_. Math. Comp. (1975), 83-92.

problems.statement: вҲғ c < 1, вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, Erdos377.sumInvPrimesNotDvdCentralBinom n вүӨ c * Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘n)
problems.docstring:
Erdos, Graham, Ruzsa, and Straus proved that if
$$
  f(n) = \sum_{p \leq n} 1_{p\nmid {2n \choose n}}\frac{1}{p}
$$
then there is some constant $c < 1$ such that for all large $n$
$$
  f(n) \leq c \log\log n.
$$

[EGRS75] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Graham, R. L. and Ruzsa, I. Z. and Straus, E. G., _On the prime factors of $\binom{2n}{n}$_. Math. Comp. (1975), 83-92.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (ОівӮҖ : в„қ),
  ОівӮҖ = вҲ‘' (k : в„•), Real.log (вҶ‘k + 2) / 2 ^ (k + 2) вҶ’
    Filter.Tendsto (fun x => 1 / вҶ‘x * вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 x, Erdos377.sumInvPrimesNotDvdCentralBinom n) Filter.atTop
      (nhds ОівӮҖ)
problems.docstring:
Erdos, Graham, Ruzsa, and Straus proved that if
$$
  f(n) = \sum_{p \leq n} 1_{p\nmid {2n \choose n}}\frac{1}{p}
$$
and
$$
  \gamma_0 = \sum_{k = 2}^{\infty} \frac{\log k}{2^k}
$$
then
$$
  \lim_{x\to\infty} \frac{1}{x}\sum_{n\leq x} f(n) = \gamma_0
$$

[EGRS75] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Graham, R. L. and Ruzsa, I. Z. and Straus, E. G., _On the prime factors of $\binom{2n}{n}$_. Math. Comp. (1975), 83-92.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ C > 0, вҲҖ (n : в„•), Erdos377.sumInvPrimesNotDvdCentralBinom n вүӨ C
problems.docstring:
Is there some absolute constant $C > 0$ such that
$$
  \sum_{p \leq n} 1_{p\nmid {2n \choose n}}\frac{1}{p} \leq C
$$
for all $n$?

problems.statement: False вҶ” В¬вҲғ n x y k, 1 < x * y вҲ§ 2 < k вҲ§ (n.factorial = x ^ k + y ^ k вҲЁ n.factorial + y ^ k = x ^ k)
problems.docstring:
Is it true that there are no solutions to `n! = x^k Вұ y^k` with `x,y,n вҲҲ в„•`, `x*y > 1`, and
`k > 2`?

The answer is no: Jonas Barfield found the counterexample `10! = 48^4 - 36^4` (equivalently,
`10! + 36^4 = 48^4`).

This is discussed in problem D2 of Guy's collection [Gu04].

This was formalized in Lean by Lu using Codex.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {n x y : в„•}, x.Coprime y вҶ’ 1 < x * y вҶ’ n.factorial вү  x ^ 4 + y ^ 4
problems.docstring:
Cambie has also observed that considerations modulo $8$ rule out any solutions to $n!=x^4+y^4$ with
$(x,y)=1$ and $xy>1$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {n x y : в„•}, 1 < x * y вҶ’ n.factorial = x ^ 2 + y ^ 2 вҶ’ n = 6 вҲ§ (x = 12 вҲ§ y = 24 вҲЁ x = 24 вҲ§ y = 12)
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and OblГЎth observed that the Bertrand-style fact (first proved by Breusch [Br32]) that, if
$q_i$ is the sequence of primes congruent to $3\pmod{4}$ then $q_{i+1}<2q_i$ except for $q_1=3$,
together with Fermat's theorem on the sums of two squares implies that the only solution to
$n!=x^2+y^2$ is $6!=12^2+24^2$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n x : в„•), n.factorial + 1 вү  x ^ 4
problems.docstring: Pollack and Shapiro [PoSh73] proved there are no solutions to $n!=x^4-1$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {n x y k : в„•}, x.Coprime y вҶ’ 1 < x * y вҶ’ 2 < k вҶ’ k вү  4 вҶ’ n.factorial вү  x ^ k + y ^ k вҲ§ n.factorial + y ^ k вү  x ^ k
problems.docstring: ErdЕ‘s and OblГЎth [ErOb37] proved this is true when $(x,y)=1$ and $k\neq 4$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (k : в„•), {p | Nat.Prime p вҲ§ (вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.Icc 0 k, (p ^ 2 + i)).maxPrimeFac = p}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
Is it true that for every $k$ there are infinitely many primes $p$ such that the largest prime
divisor of
$$
  \prod_{i = 0}^k (p ^ 2 + i)
$$
is $p$?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ a, IsLucasSequence a вҲ§ (вҲҖ (k : в„•), (a k).Composite) вҲ§ вҲҖ n > 1, вҲғ k, n.gcd (a k) = 1
problems.docstring:
Is there an infinite Lucas sequence $a_0, a_1, \ldots$ where $a_{n+2} = a_{n+1} + a_n$ for
$n \ge 0$ such that all $a_k$ are composite, and yet no integer has a common factor with every
term of the sequence?

problems.statement: вҲғ cвӮӮ, 2 вүӨ cвӮӮ вҲ§ вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҶ‘(Erdos1104.triangleFreeMaxChromatic n) вүӨ cвӮӮ * вҲҡвҶ‘n / вҲҡ(Real.log вҶ‘n)
problems.docstring:
Upper bound (DaviesвҖ“Illingworth 2022).
There exists a constant $c_2 \ge 2$ such that, for sufficiently large $n$,
$$
f(n) \le c_2 \sqrt{\frac{n}{\log n}},
$$
where $f(n)$ denotes the maximum chromatic number of a triangle-free graph on
$n$ vertices, formalized as `triangleFreeMaxChromatic n`.

problems.statement: вҲғ cвӮҒ, 0 < cвӮҒ вҲ§ cвӮҒ вүӨ 1 вҲ§ вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, cвӮҒ * вҲҡвҶ‘n / вҲҡ(Real.log вҶ‘n) вүӨ вҶ‘(Erdos1104.triangleFreeMaxChromatic n)
problems.docstring:
Lower bound (HeftyвҖ“HornвҖ“KingвҖ“Pfender 2025).
There exists a constant $c_1 \in (0,1]$ such that, for sufficiently large $n$,
$$
c_1 \sqrt{\frac{n}{\log n}} \le f(n),
$$
where $f(n)$ denotes the maximum chromatic number of a triangle-free graph on
$n$ vertices, formalized as `triangleFreeMaxChromatic n`.

problems.statement: Filter.limsup (fun n => (Erdos979.solutionSet n 3).encard) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ
problems.docstring: ErdЕ‘s (unpublished)

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ k вүҘ 2, Filter.limsup (fun n => (Erdos979.solutionSet n k).encard) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ
problems.docstring:
Let $k вүҘ 2$, and let $f_k(n)$ count the number of solutions to $n = p_1^k + \dots + p_k^k$,
where the $p_i$ are prime numbers. Is it true that $\limsup f_k(n) = \infty$?

problems.statement: Filter.limsup (fun n => (Erdos979.solutionSet n 2).encard) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s [Er37b] proved that if $f_2(n)$ counts the number of solutions to $n = p_1^2 + p_2^2$, where $p_1$ and $p_2$ are prime numbers, then $\limsup f_2(n) = \infty$.

[Er37b] ErdЕ‘s, Paul, On the Sum and Difference of Squares of Primes. J. London Math. Soc. (1937), 133--136.

problems.statement: IsGreatest {K | Infinite вҶ‘{n | {d вҲҲ Finset.Ioo вҢҠвҲҡвҶ‘nвҢӢ вҢҲвҲҡвҶ‘n + вҶ‘n ^ (1 / 4)вҢү | d вҲЈ n}.card = K}} 4
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and Rosenfeld, ask whether $4$ is the best possible $K$ for the infinitude of $n$
with $K$ divisors in $(n^{\frac{1}{2}}, n^{\frac{1}{2}} + n^{\frac{1}{4}})$.

problems.statement: вҲғ K, вҲҖ C > 0, вҲҖб¶  (n : в„Ө) in Filter.atTop, {d вҲҲ Finset.Ioo вҢҠвҲҡвҶ‘nвҢӢ вҢҲвҲҡвҶ‘n + C * вҶ‘n ^ (1 / 4)вҢү | d вҲЈ n}.card вүӨ K
problems.docstring:
Is there an absolute constant $K$ such that, for every $C > 0$, if $n$ is sufficiently large then
$n$ has at most $K$ divisors in $(n^{\frac{1}{2}}, n^{\frac{1}{2}} + C n^{\frac{1}{4}})$.

problems.statement: Infinite вҶ‘{n | {d вҲҲ Finset.Ioo вҢҠвҲҡвҶ‘nвҢӢ вҢҲвҲҡвҶ‘n + вҶ‘n ^ (1 / 4)вҢү | d вҲЈ n}.card = 4}
problems.docstring:
A question of ErdЕ‘s and Rosenfeld, who proved that there are infinitely many $n$ with $4$ divisors
in $(n^{\frac{1}{2}}, n^{\frac{1}{2}} + n^{\frac{1}{4}})$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ C > 0, вҲҖб¶  (n : в„Ө) in Filter.atTop, {d вҲҲ Finset.Ioo вҢҠвҲҡвҶ‘nвҢӢ вҢҲвҲҡвҶ‘n + C * вҶ‘n ^ (1 / 4)вҢү | d вҲЈ n}.card вүӨ sorry
problems.docstring:
Is there an absolute constant $K$ such that, for every $C > 0$, if $n$ is sufficiently large then
$n$ has at most $K$ divisors in $(n^{\frac{1}{2}}, n^{\frac{1}{2}} + C n^{\frac{1}{4}})$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ m вүҘ 2, вҲҖ n вүҘ 2, вҲғ i j, (вҮ‘(ArithmeticFunction.sigma 1))^[i] m = (вҮ‘(ArithmeticFunction.sigma 1))^[j] n
problems.docstring:
Let $Пғ_1(n)=Пғ(n)$, the sum of divisors function, and $Пғ_k(n) = Пғ(Пғ_{k-1}(n))$.
Is it true that, for every $m, n вүҘ 2$, there exist some $i, j$ such that $Пғ_i(m) = Пғ_j(n)$?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {f : в„Ӯ вҶ’ в„Ӯ} {n : в„• вҶ’ в„•},
  HasFabryGaps n вҶ’
    вҲҖ {a : в„• вҶ’ в„Ӯ},
      (вҲҖ (n : в„•), a n вү  0) вҶ’
        (вҲҖ (z : в„Ӯ), HasSum (fun k => a k * z ^ n k) (f z)) вҶ’
          OfFiniteOrder f вҶ’ Filter.limsup (fun r => Erdos516.ratio r f) Filter.atTop = 1
problems.docstring:
Let `f = вҲ‘ aвӮ–zвҒҝвӮ–` be an entire function of finite order such that `nвӮ– / k вҶ’ вҲһ`.
Then `limsup (fun r => ratio r f) atTop = 1`. This is proved in [Fu63].

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ {f : в„Ӯ вҶ’ в„Ӯ} {n : в„• вҶ’ в„•},
    HasFejerGaps n вҶ’
      вҲҖ {a : в„• вҶ’ в„Ӯ},
        (вҲҖ (n : в„•), a n вү  0) вҶ’
          (вҲҖ (z : в„Ӯ), HasSum (fun k => a k * z ^ n k) (f z)) вҶ’
            Filter.limsup (fun r => Erdos516.ratio r f) Filter.atTop = 1
problems.docstring:
Is it true that for all entire functions `f = вҲ‘ aвӮ–zвҒҝвӮ–` such that `вҲ‘' 1 / nвӮ– < вҲһ`,
`limsup (fun r => ratio r f) atTop = 1`?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {f : в„Ӯ вҶ’ в„Ӯ} {n : в„• вҶ’ в„•},
  (вҲғ c > 0, вҲҖ (k : в„•), вҶ‘(n k) > вҶ‘k * Real.log вҶ‘k ^ (2 + c)) вҶ’
    вҲҖ {a : в„• вҶ’ в„Ӯ},
      (вҲҖ (n : в„•), a n вү  0) вҶ’
        (вҲҖ (z : в„Ӯ), HasSum (fun k => a k * z ^ n k) (f z)) вҶ’
          Filter.limsup (fun r => Erdos516.ratio r f) Filter.atTop = 1
problems.docstring:
Let `f = вҲ‘ aвӮ–zвҒҝвӮ–` be an entire function such that `nвӮ– > k (log k) ^ (2 + c)`.
Then `limsup (fun r => ratio r f) atTop = 1`. This is proved in [Ko65].

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ cвӮҒ > 0,
    вҲҖ cвӮӮ > 0,
      вҲҖб¶  (x : в„қ) in Filter.atTop,
        вҲғ k,
          cвӮҒ * Real.log x < вҶ‘k вҲ§
            вҲғ f m,
              (вҲҖ (i : Fin k), вҶ‘(f i) вүӨ x вҲ§ f i = Nat.nth Nat.Prime (m + вҶ‘i)) вҲ§
                вҲҖ (i : Fin (k - 1)), cвӮӮ < primeGap (m + вҶ‘i)
problems.docstring:
Let `cвӮҒ, cвӮӮ > 0`. Is it true that for any sufficiently large `x`, there exists more than
`cвӮҒ * log x` many consecutive primes `вүӨ x` such that the difference between any two is `> cвӮӮ`?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ cвӮӮ > 0,
  вҲҖб¶  (cвӮҒ : в„қ) in nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ioi 0),
    вҲҖб¶  (x : в„қ) in Filter.atTop,
      вҲғ k,
        cвӮҒ * Real.log x < вҶ‘k вҲ§
          вҲғ f m,
            (вҲҖ (i : Fin k), вҶ‘(f i) вүӨ x вҲ§ f i = Nat.nth Nat.Prime (m + вҶ‘i)) вҲ§ вҲҖ (i : Fin (k - 1)), cвӮӮ < primeGap (m + вҶ‘i)
problems.docstring: It is well-known that the conjecture above is true when `cвӮҒ` is sufficiently small.

problems.statement:
вҲғ p o,
  Filter.Tendsto o Filter.atTop (nhds 0) вҲ§
    Filter.Tendsto p Filter.atTop (nhds 0) вҲ§
      вҲҖ (О© : Type u_3) [inst : MeasureTheory.MeasureSpace О©] [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure MeasureTheory.volume]
        (n : в„•),
        2 вүӨ n вҶ’
          вҲҖ (f : Erdos522.KacPolynomial n {-1, 1} О© MeasureTheory.volume),
            (MeasureTheory.volume {Пү | |вҶ‘(f.roots Пү).card / Real.log вҶ‘n - 2 / Real.pi| вүҘ o n}).toReal вүӨ p n
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and Offord showed that the number of real roots of a random degree `n` polynomial with `Вұ1`
coefficients is `(2/ПҖ+o(1))log n`.

problems.statement:
вҲғ p,
  Filter.Tendsto p Filter.atTop (nhds 0) вҲ§
    вҲҖ (О© : Type u_3) [inst : MeasureTheory.MeasureSpace О©] [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure MeasureTheory.volume]
      (n : в„•),
      2 вүӨ n вҶ’
        вҲҖ (f : Erdos522.KacPolynomial n {-1, 1} О© MeasureTheory.volume),
          (MeasureTheory.volume
                {Пү |
                  |вҶ‘(Multiset.countP (fun x => x вҲҲ Metric.closedBall 0 1) (f.roots Пү)) - вҶ‘n / 2| вүҘ
                    вҶ‘n ^ (9 / 10)}).toReal вүӨ
            p n
problems.docstring: Yakir proved that almost all Kac polynomials have `n/2+O(n^(9/10))` many roots in `{zвҲҲC:|z|вүӨ1}`.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (a : sorry),
  вҲғ p o,
    Filter.Tendsto o Filter.atTop (nhds 0) вҲ§
      Filter.Tendsto p Filter.atTop (nhds 0) вҲ§
        вҲҖ (О© : Type u_3) [inst : MeasureTheory.MeasureSpace О©] [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure MeasureTheory.volume]
          (n : в„•),
          1 вүӨ n вҶ’
            вҲҖ (f : Erdos522.KacPolynomial n {-1, 1} О© MeasureTheory.volume),
              (MeasureTheory.volume
                    {Пү |
                      |вҶ‘(Multiset.countP (fun x => x вҲҲ Metric.closedBall 0 1) (f.roots Пү)) - вҶ‘n / 2| вүҘ
                        o n * вҶ‘n}).toReal вүӨ
                p n
problems.docstring:
Let `f(z)=вҲ‘_{0вүӨkвүӨn} Пө_k z^k` be a random polynomial, where `Пө_kвҲҲ{вҲ’1,1}` independently uniformly at
random for `0вүӨkвүӨn`.
Is it true that the number of roots of `f(z)` in `{zвҲҲC:|z|вүӨ1}` is, almost surely, `(1/2+o(1))n`?

Formalization note: here the goal seems to mean that
` в„ҷ(| #roots of f in unit disk - n/2 | вүҘ o(1)) вҶ’ 0` as `n вҶ’ вҲһ`
This is quite awkward to formalise!

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ k вүҘ 2, вҲғб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, k вүӨ n - 2 вҲ§ вҲғ p q, n.choose k = вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.Ico p q, Nat.nth Nat.Prime i
problems.docstring:
There is a $k$, such that $2 \le k \le n - 2$ and
$\binom{n}{k}$ can be the product of consecutive primes infinitely often?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, 2 вүӨ n - 2 вҲ§ вҲғ p q, n.choose 2 = вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.Ico p q, Nat.nth Nat.Prime i
problems.docstring: Can $\binom{n}{2}$ be the product of consecutive primes infinitely often?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ k вүҘ 2, вҲғб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, k вүӨ n - 2 вҲ§ вҲғ p q, n.choose k = вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.Ico p q, Nat.nth Nat.Prime i
problems.docstring:
For all $2 \le k \le n - 2$,
can $\binom{n}{k}$ be the product of consecutive primes infinitely often?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (N : в„•), 1 вүӨ N вҶ’ вҲғ A вҠҶ Finset.Icc 1 N, IsSidon вҶ‘A вҲ§ A.card вүҘ N.sqrt
problems.docstring: The greedy construction gives a Sidon set of size approximately `вҲҡN`.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (N : в„•), 1 вүӨ N вҶ’ вҶ‘(Finset.Icc 1 N).maxSidonSubsetCard вүӨ 2 * вҲҡвҶ‘N
problems.docstring: The maximum size of a Sidon set in `{1, ..., N}` is less than or equal to `2 * вҲҡN`.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ Оө > 0, вҲҖб¶  (M : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҲғ A вҠҶ Finset.Icc 1 M, IsSidon вҶ‘A вҲ§ (1 - Оө) * вҲҡвҶ‘M вүӨ вҶ‘A.card
problems.docstring: The case where we start with an empty set (constructing large Sidon sets).

problems.statement: вҲҖ (N : в„•), 1 вүӨ N вҶ’ вҲғ A вҠҶ Finset.Icc 1 N, IsSidon вҶ‘A вҲ§ N.sqrt / 2 вүӨ A.card
problems.docstring: For any `N`, there exists a Sidon set of size at least `вҲҡN/2`.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ N вүҘ 1,
    вҲҖ A вҠҶ Finset.Icc 1 N,
      IsSidon вҶ‘A вҶ’ вҲҖ Оө > 0, вҲғ M > N, вҲғ B вҠҶ Finset.Icc (N + 1) M, IsSidon (вҶ‘A вҲӘ вҶ‘B) вҲ§ (1 - Оө) * вҲҡвҶ‘M вүӨ вҶ‘(A вҲӘ B).card
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘s Problem 44:** Let N вүҘ 1 and `A вҠҶ {1,вҖҰ,N}` be a Sidon set. Is it true that, for any Оө > 0,
there exist M = M(Оө) and `B вҠҶ {N+1,вҖҰ,M}` such that `A вҲӘ B вҠҶ {1,вҖҰ,M}` is a Sidon set
of size at least `(1вҲ’Оө)M^{1/2}`?

This problem asks whether any Sidon set can be extended to achieve a density
arbitrarily close to the optimal density for Sidon sets.
The set `{1, 2, 4, 8, 13}` is a Sidon set in `{1, ..., 13}`.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ c > 0,
    вҲғ o,
      o =o[Filter.atTop] 1 вҲ§
        (вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҶ‘(Erdos942.erdos_942.h n) < Real.log вҶ‘n ^ (c + o n)) вҲ§
          {n | вҶ‘(Erdos942.erdos_942.h n) > Real.log вҶ‘n ^ (c - o n)}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
Is there some constant $c > 0$ such that $h(n) < (\log n)^{c + o(1)}$ and, for infinitely many $n$,
$h(n) > (\log n)^{c - o(1)}$.

problems.statement: Filter.limsup ((fun n => вҶ‘n) вҲҳ Erdos942.erdos_942.h) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ
problems.docstring: It is not hard to prove that $\limsup h(n) = \infty$.

problems.statement: вҲғ Оҙ, вҲҖ (l : в„•), {n | Erdos942.erdos_942.h n = l}.HasDensity (Оҙ l) вҲ§ вҲ‘' (l : в„•), Оҙ l = 1
problems.docstring:
It is not hard to prove that the density $\delta_l$ of integers for which $h(n) = l$ exists
and satisfies $$\sum_l \delta_l = 1$$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” (fun n => 3 ^ n) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos817.g 3 n)
problems.docstring:
Let $k \geq 3$. Define $g_k(n)$ to be the minimal $N$ such that
$\{1, ..., N\}$ contains some $A$ of size $|A| = n$ such that
$$
  \langle A\rangle = \left\{\sum_{a \in A} \epsilon_a a : \epsilon_a \in\{0, 1\}\right\}
$$
contains no non-trivial $k$-term arithmetic progression. Estimate $g_k(n)$. In
particular, is it true that
$$
  g_3(n) \gg 3^n
$$

problems.statement: вҲғ O > 0, (fun n => 3 ^ n / вҶ‘n ^ O) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos817.g 3 n)
problems.docstring:
A problem of ErdЕ‘s and SГЎrkГ¶zy who proved
$$
  g_3(n) \gg \frac{3^n}{n^{O(1)}}.
$$

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (k : в„•),
  Filter.liminf (fun n => вҲ‘ i вҲҲ Finset.range k, вҶ‘(ArithmeticFunction.cardDistinctFactors (n + i))) Filter.atTop вүҘ
    вҶ‘k + вҶ‘k.primeCounting - 1
problems.docstring:
A question of ErdЕ‘s and Selfridge [ErSe67], who observe that
$\liminf_{n\to \infty}\sum_{0\leq i < k}\omega(n+i)\geq k+\pi(k)-1$ for every $k$. This follows from
PГіlya's theorem that the set of $k$-smooth integers has unbounded gaps - indeed,
$n(n+1)\cdots (n+k-1)$ is divisible by all primes $\leq k$ and, provided $n$ is large, all but at
most one of $n,n+1,\ldots,n+k-1$ has a prime factor $>k$ by PГіlya's theorem.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ k вүҘ 1,
    Filter.liminf (fun n => вҲ‘ i вҲҲ Finset.range k, вҶ‘(ArithmeticFunction.cardDistinctFactors (n + i))) Filter.atTop вүӨ
      вҶ‘k + вҶ‘k.primeCounting
problems.docstring:
If $\omega(n)$ counts the number of distinct prime factors of $n$, then is it true that,
for every $k\geq 1$, $\liminf_{n\to \infty}\sum_{0\leq i < k}\omega(n+i)\leq k+\pi(k)?$

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ k вүҘ 1,
    Filter.limsup
        (fun n =>
          (вҲ‘ i вҲҲ Finset.range k, вҶ‘(ArithmeticFunction.cardDistinctFactors (n + i))) *
            (вҶ‘(Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘n)) / вҶ‘(Real.log вҶ‘n)))
        Filter.atTop =
      1
problems.docstring:
Is it true that
$\limsup_{n\to \infty}\left(\sum_{0\leq i < k}\omega(n+i)\right) \frac{\log\log n}{\log n}=1?$

problems.statement:
Filter.limsup (fun n => вҶ‘(ArithmeticFunction.cardDistinctFactors n) * (вҶ‘(Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘n)) / вҶ‘(Real.log вҶ‘n)))
    Filter.atTop =
  1
problems.docstring: It is a classical fact that $\limsup_{n\to \infty}\omega(n)\frac{\log\log n}{\log n}=1.$

problems.statement: True вҶ” Filter.Tendsto (fun n => (вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 n, Erdos1106.p i).primeFactors.card) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Let $p(n)$ be the partition number of $n$ and $F(n)$ be the number of distinct prime factors of
$вҲҸ_{i= 1} ^ {n} p(n)$, then $F(n)$ tends to infinity when $n$ tends to infinity.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, (вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 n, Erdos1106.p i).primeFactors.card > n
problems.docstring:
Let $p(n)$ be the partition number of $n$ and $F(n)$ be the number of distinct prime factors of
$вҲҸ_{i= 1} ^ {n} p(n)$, $F(n)>n$ for sufficiently large $n$.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ Оө > 0,
    вҲҖб¶  (N : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
      вҲҖ A вҠҶ Finset.Icc 1 N,
        Оө * вҶ‘N вүӨ вҶ‘A.card вҶ’ вҲғ a вҲҲ A, вҲғ b вҲҲ A, вҲғ c вҲҲ A, {a, b, c}.card = 3 вҲ§ a.lcm b = b.lcm c вҲ§ b.lcm c = a.lcm c
problems.docstring:
Let $\epsilon>0$ and $N$ be sufficiently large. Is it true that if $A\subseteq \{1,\ldots,N\}$ has
size at least $\epsilon N$ then there must be distinct $a,b,c\in A$ such that
$$[a, b]=[b, c]=[a, c],$$
where $[\cdot, \cdot]$ denotes the least common multiple?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, Erdos1148.Erdos1148Prop n
problems.docstring: Can every large integer $n$ be written as $n=x^2+y^2-z^2$ with $\max(x^2,y^2,z^2)\leq n$?

problems.statement: В¬Erdos1148.Erdos1148Prop 6563
problems.docstring: The largest integer known which cannot be written this way is $6563$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), Erdos1148.erdos_1148_weaker_prop n
problems.docstring: [Va99] reports this is 'obvious' if we replace $\leq n$ with $\leq n+2\sqrt{n}$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {A : Set в„қ}, sorry = sSup {c | Erdos757.IsAdmissible c}
problems.docstring: What is the supremum of the set of admissible numbers?

problems.statement: вҲҖ {A : Set в„қ}, 1 / 2 < sSup {c | Erdos757.IsAdmissible c}
problems.docstring: The supremum is strictly larger than `1 / 2`, which is proved in [GyLe95].

problems.statement: вҲҖ {A : Set в„қ}, sSup {c | Erdos757.IsAdmissible c} < 3 / 5
problems.docstring: In [GyLe95], the authors also prove that the supremum is smaller than `3 / 5`.

problems.statement: True вҶ” 0 < ({x | (Nat.digits 3 x).toFinset вҠҶ {0, 1}} + {x | (Nat.digits 4 x).toFinset вҠҶ {0, 1}}).upperDensity
problems.docstring:
Let $A = {вҲ‘ Оө_{k} 3^{k} : Оө_{k} вҲҲ {0,1}}$ be the set of integers which
have only the digits $0, 1$ when written base 3, and $B = {вҲ‘ Оө_{k} 4^{k} : Оө_{k} вҲҲ {0,1}}$
be the set of integers which have only the digits $0, 1$ when written base 4.
Does $A + B$ have positive upper density?

problems.statement: False вҶ” ({x | (Nat.digits 3 x).toFinset вҠҶ {0, 1}} + {x | (Nat.digits 4 x).toFinset вҠҶ {0, 1}}).HasPosDensity
True вҶ” 0 < ({x | (Nat.digits 3 x).toFinset вҠҶ {0, 1}} + {x | (Nat.digits 4 x).toFinset вҠҶ {0, 1}}).lowerDensity
problems.docstring:
Let $A = {вҲ‘ Оө_{k} 3^{k} : Оө_{k} вҲҲ {0,1}}$ be the set of integers which
have only the digits $0, 1$ when written base 3, and $B = {вҲ‘ Оө_{k} 4^{k} : Оө_{k} вҲҲ {0,1}}$
be the set of integers which have only the digits $0, 1$ when written base 4.
Does $A + B$ have positive lower density?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ a,
    Monotone a вҲ§
      Filter.Tendsto (fun n => вҶ‘(a (n + 1)) / вҶ‘(a n)) Filter.atTop (nhds 2) вҲ§
        вҲҖ (О№ : в„• вҶ’ в„•), (Set.range О№)б¶ң.Finite вҶ’ (subsetSums (Set.range (a вҲҳ О№))).HasDensity 1
problems.docstring:
Is there a sequence $A=\{a_1\leq a_2\leq \cdots\}$ of integers with
\[\lim \frac{a_{n+1}}{a_n}=2\]
such that
\[P(A')= \left\{\sum_{n\in B}n : B\subseteq A'\textrm{ finite }\right\}\]
has density $1$ for every cofinite subsequence $A'$ of $A$?

This has been solved in the affirmative by ebarschkis in the comments (based on idea of Tao and
van Doorn, also in the comments).

Thos was formalized in Lean by Barschkis using Aristotle.

problems.statement: True вҶ” {n | вҶ‘n.maxPrimeFac < вҲҡвҶ‘n вҲ§ вҶ‘(n + 1).maxPrimeFac < вҲҡ(вҶ‘n + 1)}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
Are there infinitely many $n$ such that the largest prime factor of $n$ is $< n^{\frac{1}{2}}$ and
the largest prime factor of $n + 1$ is $< (n + 1)^{\frac{1}{2}}$.

Steinerberger has pointed out this problem has a trivial solution.

This was formalized in Lean by Alexeev using Aristotle.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ Оө > 0, вҲғ A, 1 - Оө вүӨ (A + A).lowerDensity вҲ§ (Nat.cast вҲҳ AdditiveCombinatorics.sumRep A) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => 1
problems.docstring:
Let $\epsilon>0$. Does there exist $A\subseteq \mathbb{N}$
such that the lower density of $A+A$ is at least $1-\epsilon$
and yet $1_A\ast 1_A(n) \ll_\epsilon 1$ for all $n$?

problems.statement:
False вҶ”
  вҲғ c > 0,
    вҲҖ (V : Type) [inst : Fintype V] [Nonempty V] (G : SimpleGraph V) (X Y : Set V),
      Erdos1080.IsBipartition G X Y вҶ’
        X.ncard = вҢҠвҶ‘(Fintype.card V) ^ (2 / 3)вҢӢвӮҠ вҶ’
          вҶ‘G.edgeSet.ncard вүҘ c * вҶ‘(Fintype.card V) вҶ’ вҲғ v walk, walk.IsCycle вҲ§ walk.length = 6
problems.docstring:
Let $G$ be a bipartite graph on $n$ vertices such that one part has $\lfloor n^{2/3}\rfloor$
vertices. Is there a constant $c>0$ such that if $G$ has at least $cn$ edges then $G$ must
contain a $C_6$?

The answer is no, as shown by De Caen and SzГ©kely [DeSz92], who in fact show a stronger result.
Let $f(n,m)$ be the maximum number of edges of a bipartite graph between $n$ and $m$ vertices which
does not contain either a $C_4$ or $C_6$. A positive answer to this question would then imply
$f(n,\lfloor n^{2/3}\rfloor)\ll n$. De Caen and SzГ©kely prove
$n^{10/9}\gg f(n,\lfloor n^{2/3}\rfloor) \gg n^{58/57+o(1)}$ for $m\sim n^{2/3}$. They also prove
more generally that, for $n^{1/2}\leq m\leq n$, $f(n,m) \ll (nm)^{2/3},$ which was also proved by
Faudree and Simonovits.

This was formalized in Lean by Alexeev using Aristotle.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ n > 1, Filter.Tendsto (fun k => вҶ‘((вҮ‘(ArithmeticFunction.sigma 1))^[k] n) ^ (1 / вҶ‘k)) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Let $Пғ_1(n) = Пғ(n)$, the sum of divisors function, and $Пғ_k(n) = Пғ(Пғ_{k-1}(n))$.

Is it true that $\lim_{k вҶ’ вҲһ} Пғ_k(n)^{\frac 1 k} = вҲһ$?

This is problem (iii) from
Erdos, Granville, Pomerance, Spiro
"On the normal behavior of the iterates of some arithmetical functions"
(page 169 of the book "Analytic Number Theory", 1990).

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {О© : Type u_1} [inst : MeasureTheory.MeasureSpace О©] [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure MeasureTheory.volume],
  True вҶ”
    вҲғ c > 0,
      вҲҖ (f : в„• вҶ’ О© вҶ’ в„қ),
        Erdos520.IsRademacherMultiplicative f вҶ’
          вҲҖбөҗ (Пү : О©),
            Filter.limsup (fun N => вҲ‘ m вҲҲ Finset.Iic N, f m Пү / вҲҡ(вҶ‘N * Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘N))) Filter.atTop = c
problems.docstring:
Let $f$ be a Rademacher multiplicative function.
Does there exist some constant $c > 0$ such that, almost surely,
\[
  \limsup_{N \to \infty} \frac{\sum_{m \leq N} f(m)}{\sqrt{N \log \log N}} = c?
\]

problems.statement: (вҲҖ (x : в„қ), HasSum (fun n => вҶ‘((ArithmeticFunction.sigma 1) n) / 2 ^ n) x вҶ’ Irrational x) вҶ” True
problems.docstring:
Is
$$
  \sum_{n=1}^\infty \frac{\sigma(n)}{2^n}
$$
irrational? Here $\sigma(n)$ is the sum of divisors function.

The answer is yes, as shown by Nesterenko [Ne96].

[Ne96] Nesterenko, Yu V., _Modular functions and transcendence questions_,
Mat. Sb. 187 *9* (1996), 1319--1348.

problems.statement: False вҶ” {(M, N) | Disjoint M N вҲ§ вҲҸ i вҲҲ M, i.centralBinom = вҲҸ j вҲҲ N, j.centralBinom}.Finite
problems.docstring:
Are there only finitely many solutions to
$$
  \prod_i \binom{2m_i}{m_i}=\prod_j \binom{2n_j}{n_j}
$$
with the $m_i,n_j$ distinct?

Somani, using ChatGPT, has given a negative answer. In fact, for any $a\geq 2$, if $c=8a^2+8a+1$,
$\binom{2a}{a}\binom{4a+4}{2a+2}\binom{2c}{c}= \binom{2a+2}{a+1}\binom{4a}{2a}\binom{2c+2}{c+1}.$
Further families of solutions are given in the comments by SharkyKesa.

This was earlier asked about in a [MathOverflow] question, in response to which Elkies also gave an
alternative construction which produces solutions - at the moment it is not clear whether Elkies'
argument gives infinitely many solutions (although Bloom believes that it can).

This was formalized in Lean by Wu using Aristotle.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ Оө > 0, {n | Erdos413.IsBarrier (fun n => Оө * вҶ‘(ArithmeticFunction.cardDistinctFactors n)) n}.Infinite
problems.docstring: Does there exist some `Оө > 0` such that there are infinitely many `Оө`-barriers for `Пү`?

problems.statement: True вҶ” {n | Erdos413.IsBarrier (fun m => вҶ‘(ArithmeticFunction.cardDistinctFactors m)) n}.Infinite
problems.docstring: Are there infinitely many barriers for `Пү`?

problems.statement: {n | Erdos413.IsBarrier (fun m => вҶ‘(Erdos413.expProd m)) n}.HasPosDensity
problems.docstring: ErdЕ‘s proved that the barrier set for `expProd` is infinite and even has positive density.

problems.statement: True вҶ” {n | Erdos413.IsBarrier (fun m => вҶ‘(ArithmeticFunction.cardFactors m)) n}.Infinite
problems.docstring: ErdЕ‘s believed there should be infinitely many barriers for `О©`, the total prime multiplicity.

problems.statement: IsGreatest {n | n < 10 ^ 5 вҲ§ Erdos413.IsBarrier (fun m => вҶ‘(ArithmeticFunction.cardFactors m)) n} 99840
problems.docstring: Selfridge computed that the largest `О©`-barrier below `10^5` is `99840`.

problems.statement: Erdos427.ShiuTheorem
problems.docstring:
**Shiu's theorem**: for any $k \geq 1$ and $(a, q) = 1$ there exist infinitely many $k$-tuples of consecutive primes
$p_m, \dots, p_{m + k - 1}$ all of which are congruent to $a$ modulo $q$.

[Sh00] Shiu, D. K. L., _Strings of congruent primes_. J. London Math. Soc. (2) (2000), 359-373.

problems.statement: Erdos427.ShiuTheorem вҶ’ Erdos427.erdos427
problems.docstring: Cedric Pilatte has observed that a positive solution to ErdЕ‘s Problem 427 follows from Shiu's theorem.

problems.statement: True вҶ” Erdos427.erdos427
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘s Problem 427**: is it true that, for every $n$ and $d$, there exists $k$ such that
$$
  d \mid p_{n + 1} + \cdots + p_{n + k},
$$
where $p_r$ denotes the $r$th prime?

problems.statement: (16, [14, 5, 2]) вҲҲ Erdos373.S вҲ§ вҲҖ s вҲҲ Erdos373.S, s.1 вүӨ 16
problems.docstring:
Hickerson conjectured the largest solution the equation `n!=a_1!a_2!В·В·В·a_k!`, with
`nвҲ’1 > a_1 вүҘ a_2 вүҘ В·В·В· вүҘ a_k`, is `16!=14!5!2!`.

problems.statement: (вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, 4 * Real.log вҶ‘n < вҶ‘(n * (n - 1)).maxPrimeFac) вҶ’ Erdos373.S.Finite
problems.docstring:
Show that if `P(n(nвҲ’1)) > 4 log n` for large enough `n`, where `P(m)` denotes the
largest prime factor of `m`, then the equation `n!=a_1!a_2!В·В·В·a_k!`, with
`nвҲ’1 > a_1 вүҘ a_2 вүҘ В·В·В· вүҘ a_k`, has only finitely many solutions.

problems.statement: {(n, a, b) | n.factorial = a.factorial * b.factorial вҲ§ 1 < n вҲ§ 1 < a вҲ§ 1 < b вҲ§ b вүӨ a вҲ§ a + 1 вү  n} = {(10, 7, 6)}
problems.docstring:
SurГЎnyi was the first to conjecture that the only non-trivial solution to `a!b!=n!`
is `6!7!=10!`.

problems.statement: Erdos373.S.Finite
problems.docstring:
Show that the equation `n!=a_1!a_2!В·В·В·a_k!`, with `nвҲ’1 > a_1 вүҘ a_2 вүҘ В·В·В· вүҘ a_k`, has
only finitely many solutions.

problems.statement: Filter.Tendsto (fun n => вҶ‘(n * (n + 1)).maxPrimeFac / Real.log вҶ‘n) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop вҶ’ Erdos373.S.Finite
problems.docstring:
Show that if `P(n(n+1)) / log n вҶ’ вҲһ` where `P(m)` denotes the largest prime factor of `m`, then
the equation `n!=a_1!a_2!В·В·В·a_k!`, with `nвҲ’1 > a_1 вүҘ a_2 вүҘ В·В·В· вүҘ a_k`, has only
finitely many solutions.

problems.statement: IsGreatest {n | n.isPowerOfTwo вҲ§ Nat.digits 3 n вҠҶ [1, 2]} (2 ^ 15)
problems.docstring:
If we only allow the digits $1$ and $2$ then $2^{15}$ seems to be the largest such power
of $2$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” {n | n.isPowerOfTwo вҲ§ Nat.digits 3 n вҠҶ [0, 1]}.Finite
problems.docstring:
Is it true that there are only finitely many powers of $2$ which have only the digits $0$
and $1$ when written in base $3$?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (a : в„•), 0 < a вҶ’ вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҲғ x y z, 1 вүӨ x вҲ§ x < y вҲ§ y < z вҲ§ вҶ‘a / вҶ‘n = 1 / вҶ‘x + 1 / вҶ‘y + 1 / вҶ‘z
problems.docstring:
Schinzel conjectured (see [Si56]) the generalisation that, for any fixed $a$, if $n$ is sufficiently
large in terms of $a$ then there exist distinct integers $1\leq x < y < z$ such that
$\frac{a}{n} = \frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{y}+\frac{1}{z}.$

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), 2 < n вҶ’ вҲғ x y z, 1 вүӨ x вҲ§ x < y вҲ§ y < z вҲ§ 4 / вҶ‘n = 1 / вҶ‘x + 1 / вҶ‘y + 1 / вҶ‘z
problems.docstring:
For every $n>2$ there exist distinct integers $1 вүӨ x < y < z$
such that $\frac 4 n = \frac 1 x + \frac 1 y + \frac 1 z$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ c, 0 < c вҲ§ Asymptotics.IsEquivalent Filter.atTop Erdos1061.S fun x => c * x
problems.docstring:
How many (ordered) solutions are there to `Пғ(a) + Пғ(b) = Пғ(a + b)` with `a + b вүӨ x`?
Is it true that this number is asymptotic to `c * x` for some constant `c > 0`?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (P : Polynomial в„Ӯ) (n : в„•),
  (вҲҖ i вүӨ P.natDegree, P.coeff i = -1 вҲЁ P.coeff i = 1) вҶ’ P.natDegree = n вҶ’ вЁҶ z, вҖ–Polynomial.eval (вҶ‘z) PвҖ– вүҘ вҲҡ(вҶ‘n + 1)
problems.docstring:
The trivial lower bound from Parseval's identity: for any polynomial $P$ of degree $n$ with
coefficients in $\{-1, 1\}$, we have $\max_{|z|=1} |P(z)| \geq \sqrt{n+1}$.

This follows from Parseval's identity:
$$\frac{1}{2\pi} \int_0^{2\pi} |P(e^{i\theta})|^2 d\theta = \sum_{k=0}^{n} |a_k|^2 = n+1$$
since each $|a_k|^2 = 1$.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ c > 0,
    вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
      вҲҖ (P : Polynomial в„Ӯ),
        (вҲҖ i вүӨ P.natDegree, P.coeff i = -1 вҲЁ P.coeff i = 1) вҶ’
          P.natDegree = n вҶ’ вЁҶ z, вҖ–Polynomial.eval (вҶ‘z) PвҖ– > (1 + c) * вҲҡвҶ‘n
problems.docstring:
Is there some constant $c > 0$ such that, for all large enough $n$ and all polynomials $P$ of
degree $n$ with coefficients in $\{-1, 1\}$,
$$\max_{|z|=1} |P(z)| > (1 + c) \sqrt{n}?$$

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (n : в„•) (f : Polynomial в„Ӯ),
  n вүҘ 2 вҶ’
    f.natDegree = n вҶ’
      f.Monic вҶ’
        f.rootSet в„Ӯ вҠҶ Metric.ball 0 1 вҶ’
          вҲғ C вҠҶ {z | вҖ–Polynomial.eval z fвҖ– < 1}, IsConnected C вҲ§ 2 вүӨ (Multiset.filter (fun x => x вҲҲ C) f.roots).card
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘sвҖ“HerzogвҖ“Piranian Component Lemma** (Metric Properties of Polynomials, 1958):
If $f$ is a monic degree $n$ polynomial with all roots in the unit disk,
then some connected component
of $\{z \mid |f(z)| < 1\}$ contains at least two roots with multiplicity.

See p. 139, above Problem 5:
[EHP58] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Herzog, F. and Piranian, G., _Metric properties of polynomials_.
  J. Analyse Math. (1958), 125-148.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (n : в„•) (f : Polynomial в„Ӯ),
  n вүҘ 2 вҶ’
    f.natDegree = n вҶ’
      f.Monic вҶ’
        f.rootSet в„Ӯ вҠҶ Metric.ball 0 1 вҶ’
          вҲғ zвӮҒ zвӮӮ,
            вҲғ (_ : {zвӮҒ, zвӮӮ} вүӨ f.roots),
              вҲғ Оі, Set.range вҮ‘Оі вҠҶ {z | вҖ–Polynomial.eval z fвҖ– < 1} вҲ§ Erdos1041.length (Set.range вҮ‘Оі) < 2
problems.docstring:
Let
$$ f(z) = \prod_{i=1}^{n} (z - z_i) \in \mathbb{C}[x] $$
with $|z_i| < 1$ for all $i$.

Conjecture: Must there always exist a path of length less than 2 in
$$ \{ z \in \mathbb{C} \mid |f(z)| < 1 \} $$
which connects two of the roots of $f$?

problems.statement:
Set.InjOn
  (fun x =>
    match x with
    | (a, b) => a ^ 5 + b ^ 5)
  {(a, b) | a < b}
problems.docstring:
Probably $f(x) = x^5$ has the property that the sums $f(a)+f(b)$ with
$a < b$ nonnegative integers are distinct.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ f,
    Set.InjOn
      (fun x =>
        match x with
        | (a, b) => Polynomial.eval (вҶ‘a) f + Polynomial.eval (вҶ‘b) f)
      {(a, b) | a < b}
problems.docstring:
Does there exist a polynomial $f(x)\in\mathbb{Z}[x]$ such that all the sums $f(a)+f(b)$ with
$a < b$ nonnegative integers are distinct?

problems.statement: (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos390.f n) - 2 * вҶ‘n) =Оҳ[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘n / Real.log вҶ‘n
problems.docstring: `f n - 2 * n = Оё (n / log n)`. This is proved in [EGS82].

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ c, Asymptotics.IsEquivalent Filter.atTop (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos390.f n) - 2 * вҶ‘n) fun n => c * вҶ‘n / Real.log вҶ‘n
problems.docstring: Does there exists a constant `c` such that `f n - 2 * n ~ c * (n / log n)`?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {f : в„Ӯ вҶ’ в„Ӯ} {n : в„• вҶ’ в„•},
  HasFejerGaps n вҶ’
    вҲҖ {a : в„• вҶ’ в„Ӯ},
      (вҲҖ (k : в„•), a k вү  0) вҶ’ (вҲҖ (z : в„Ӯ), HasSum (fun k => a k * z ^ n k) (f z)) вҶ’ вҲҖ (z : в„Ӯ), {x | f x = z}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
If `f(z) = вҲ‘ aвӮ–zвҒҝвӮ–` is an entire function (with `aвӮ– вү  0` for all `k`) such that `вҲ‘ 1 / nвӮ– < вҲһ`,
then `f` assumes every value infinitely often. This theorem is proved in [Bi28].

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ {f : в„Ӯ вҶ’ в„Ӯ} {n : в„• вҶ’ в„•},
    HasFabryGaps n вҶ’
      вҲҖ {a : в„• вҶ’ в„Ӯ},
        (вҲҖ (k : в„•), a k вү  0) вҶ’ (вҲҖ (z : в„Ӯ), HasSum (fun k => a k * z ^ n k) (f z)) вҶ’ вҲҖ (z : в„Ӯ), {x | f x = z}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
If `f(z) = вҲ‘ aвӮ–zвҒҝвӮ–` is an entire function (with `aвӮ– вү  0` for all `k`) such that `nвӮ– / k вҶ’ вҲһ`,
is it true that `f` assumes every value infinitely often?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {G : Type u_1} [inst : Group G],
  1 < ENat.card G вҶ’
    вҲҖ {О№ : Type u_2} [inst_1 : Fintype О№],
      1 < Fintype.card О№ вҶ’
        вҲҖ (P : Erdos274.Group.ExactCovering G О№), вҲғ i j, i вү  j вҲ§ (P.parts i).index = (P.parts j).index
problems.docstring:
Let $G$ be a group, and let $A = \{a_1G_1, \dots, a_kG_k\}$ be a finite system of left cosets of
subgroups $G_1, \dots, G_k$ of $G$.

Herzog and SchГ¶nheim conjectured that if $A$ forms a partition of $G$ with $k > 1$, then the
indices $[G:G_1], \dots, [G:G_k]$ cannot be distinct.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ G h,
    вҲғ (_ : 1 < ENat.card G),
      вҲғ О№ x P, 1 < Fintype.card О№ вҲ§ (Set.range P.parts).Pairwise fun A B => Cardinal.mk вҶҘA вү  Cardinal.mk вҶҘB
problems.docstring:
Does there exist a group `G` with an exact covering by more than one cosets of
different sizes? (i.e. each element is contained in exactly one of the cosets.)

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {G : Type u_1} [inst : Fintype G] [inst_1 : CommGroup G],
  1 < Fintype.card G вҶ’
    вҲҖ {О№ : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype О№] (P : Erdos274.Group.ExactCovering G О№),
      1 < Fintype.card О№ вҶ’ вҲғ i j, i вү  j вҲ§ Cardinal.mk вҶҘ(P.parts i) = Cardinal.mk вҶҘ(P.parts j)
problems.docstring:
If `G` is a finite abelian group then there cannot exist an exact covering of `G` by more
than one cosets of different sizes? (i.e. each element is contained in exactly one
of the cosets.)

problems.statement: (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos236.f n)) =o[Filter.atTop] fun n => Real.log вҶ‘n
problems.docstring: Let $f(n)$ count the number of solutions to $n=p+2^k$ for prime $p$ and $k\geq 0$. Show that $f(n)=o(\log n)$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” {n | Set.InjOn вҮ‘(n * (n + 1)).factorization вҶ‘(n * (n + 1)).primeFactors}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
Are there infinitely many $n$ such that if
$$
  n(n + 1) = \prod_i p_i^{k_i}
$$
is the factorisation into distinct primes then all exponents $k_i$ are distinct?

problems.statement:
{p | Nat.Prime p вҲ§ Nat.Prime (8 * p ^ 2 - 1)}.Infinite вҶ’
  {n | Set.InjOn вҮ‘(n * (n + 1)).factorization вҶ‘(n * (n + 1)).primeFactors}.Infinite
problems.docstring: If there are infinitely many primes $p$ such that $8p^2 - 1$ is prime, then this is true.

problems.statement: {p | Nat.Prime p вҲ§ Nat.Prime (8 * p ^ 2 - 1)}.Infinite
problems.docstring: It is likely that there are infinitely many primes $p$ such that $8p^2 - 1$ is also prime.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ r вүҘ 2, вҲҖб¶  (x : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҲғ S, S.card вүӨ r вҲ§ (вҲҖ s вҲҲ S, r.Full s) вҲ§ x = S.sum
problems.docstring: It is not known if all large integers are the sum of at most $r$-many $r$-powerful numbers.

problems.statement: True вҶ” {n | вҲғ S, S.card вүӨ 3 вҲ§ n = (Multiset.map (fun x => x ^ 3) S).sum}.HasDensity 0
problems.docstring: Is it true that the set of integers which are the sum of at most three cubes has density $0$?

problems.statement: вҲҖб¶  (x : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҲғ S, S.card вүӨ 3 вҲ§ (вҲҖ s вҲҲ S, Nat.Full 2 s) вҲ§ x = S.sum
problems.docstring:
Heath-Brown [He88] has proved that all large numbers are the sum of at most three
$2$-powerful numbers.

[He88] Heath-Brown, D. R., Ternary quadratic forms and sums of three square-full numbers. (1988), 137--163.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ r вүҘ 3, {n | вҲғ S, S.card вүӨ r вҲ§ (вҲҖ s вҲҲ S, r.Full s) вҲ§ n = S.sum}.HasDensity 0
problems.docstring:
Let $r \ge 3$. Is it true that the set of integers which are the sum of at most $r$ $r$-powerful numbers
has density $0$?

problems.statement: {n | вҲғ S, S.card вүӨ 2 вҲ§ (вҲҖ s вҲҲ S, Nat.Full 2 s) вҲ§ n = S.sum}.HasDensity 0
problems.docstring: The set of integers which are the sum of at most two $2$-powerful numbers has density $0$.

problems.statement:
{x |
    вҲғ m n,
      вҲғ (_ : m < n),
        вҲғ a,
          вҲғ (_ : Monotone a) (_ :
            вҲҖ (s : Finset в„•), s.card = m вҶ’ IsAddComplete (Set.range (Function.updateFinset a s 0))) (_ :
            вҲҖ (t : Finset в„•), t.card = n вҶ’ В¬IsAddComplete (Set.range (Function.updateFinset a t 0))), (m, n) = x} =
  sorry
problems.docstring:
For what values of $0 \leq m < n$ is there a complete sequence
$A = \{a_1 \leq a_2 \leq \cdots\}$ of integers such that
 1. $A$ remains complete after removing any $m$ elements, but
 2. $A$ is not complete after removing any $n$ elements.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), sorry A вҶ’ Erdos332.HasBoundedGaps (Erdos332.D_A A)
problems.docstring:
Let $A\subseteq \mathbb{N}$ and $D(A)$ be the set of those numbers which occur infinitely often as
$a_1 - a_2$ with $a_1, a_2\in A$. What conditions on $A$ are sufficient to ensure $D(A)$ has bounded
gaps?

This is formalised here using the `answer(sorry)` mechanism. In order to solve this problem one
has to provide what the sufficient conditions are, and proof that they imply the desired condition.
If the condition is a solution to the problem is up to human judgement.

problems.statement: вҲғ O > 0, вҲғ O' > 0, вҲҖб¶  (N : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҶ‘(Erdos587.MaxNotSqSum N) вүӨ O' * Real.nthRoot 3 вҶ‘N * Real.log вҶ‘N ^ O
problems.docstring: Nguyen and Vu proved that $|A| \ll N^{1/3} (\log N)^{O(1)}$.

problems.statement: IsLeast {p | Nat.Prime p вҲ§ В¬Erdos17.IsClusterPrime p} 97
problems.docstring: $97$ is the smallest prime that is not a cluster prime.

problems.statement: True вҶ” {p | Erdos17.IsClusterPrime p}.Infinite
problems.docstring: **ErdЕ‘s ProblemВ 17.**В Are there infinitely many cluster primes?

problems.statement:
вҲғ C,
  0 < C вҲ§
    вҲҖ c вҲҲ Set.Ioo 0 (1 / 8),
      Asymptotics.IsBigOWith C Filter.atTop (fun x => вҶ‘(Erdos17.clusterPrimeCount x)) fun x =>
        вҶ‘x * Real.exp (-c * Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘x) ^ 2)
problems.docstring:
In 2003, Elsholtz [El03] refined the upper bound to
$$\pi^{\mathcal{C}}(x) \ll x\,\exp\!\bigl(-c(\log\log x)^2\bigr)$$
for every real $0 < c < 1/8$.

[El03] Elsholtz, Christian, On cluster primes. Acta Arith. (2003), 281--284.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {A : в„қ}, 0 < A вҶ’ (fun x => вҶ‘(Erdos17.clusterPrimeCount x)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun x => вҶ‘x / Real.log вҶ‘x ^ A
problems.docstring:
In 1999 Blecksmith, ErdЕ‘s, and Selfridge [BES99] proved the upper bound
$$\pi^{\mathcal{C}}(x) \ll_A x(\log x)^{-A}$$ for every real $A > 0$.

[BES99] Blecksmith, Richard and Erd\H os, Paul and Selfridge, J. L., Cluster primes. Amer. Math. Monthly (1999), 43--48.

problems.statement: (fun N => вҶ‘(Erdos321.R N)) =O[Filter.atTop] sorry
problems.docstring: Let $R(N)$ be the size of the largest $A\subseteq\{1, ..., N\}$ such that all sums $\sum_{n\in S} \frac{1}{n}$ are distinct for $S\subseteq A$. Find the simplest $g(N)$ such that $R(N) = O(g(N))$.

problems.statement: (fun N => вҶ‘(Erdos321.R N)) =o[Filter.atTop] sorry
problems.docstring: Let $R(N)$ be the size of the largest $A\subseteq\{1, ..., N\}$ such that all sums $\sum_{n\in S} \frac{1}{n}$ are distinct for $S\subseteq A$. Find the simplest $g(N)$ such that $R(N) = o(g(N))$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (N k : в„•), 4 вүӨ k вҶ’ вҶ‘k вүӨ Real.log^[k] вҶ‘N вҶ’ вҶ‘N / Real.log вҶ‘N * вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.Icc 3 k, Real.log^[i] вҶ‘N вүӨ вҶ‘(Erdos321.R N)
problems.docstring:
Let $R(N)$ be the maximal such size. Results of Bleicher and ErdЕ‘s from [BlEr75] and [BlEr76b] imply that
$$
\frac{N}{\log N} \prod_{i=3}^{k} \log_i N \le R(N),
$$
valid for any $k \ge 4$ with $\log_k N \ge k$ and any $r \ge 1$ with $\log_{2r} N \ge 1$. (In these bounds $\log_i n$ denotes the $i$-fold iterated logarithm.)

[BlEr75] Bleicher, M. N. and ErdЕ‘s, P., _The number of distinct subsums of $\sum \sb{1}\spN\,1/i$_. Math. Comp. (1975), 29-42.
[BlEr76b] Bleicher, Michael N. and ErdЕ‘s, Paul, _Denominators of Egyptian fractions. II_. Illinois J. Math. (1976), 598-613.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (N : в„•), Erdos321.R N = sorry
problems.docstring:
Let $R(N)$ be the size of the largest $A\subseteq\{1, ..., N\}$ such that all sums
$\sum_{n\in S} \frac{1}{n}$ are distinct for $S\subseteq A$. What is $R(N)$?

problems.statement: (fun N => вҶ‘(Erdos321.R N)) =Оҳ[Filter.atTop] sorry
problems.docstring:
Let $R(N)$ be the size of the largest $A\subseteq\{1, ..., N\}$ such that all sums
$\sum_{n\in S} \frac{1}{n}$ are distinct for $S\subseteq A$. What is $\Theta(R(N))$?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (N r : в„•),
  1 вүӨ r вҶ’
    1 вүӨ Real.log^[2 * r] вҶ‘N вҶ’
      вҶ‘(Erdos321.R N) вүӨ 1 / Real.log 2 * Real.log^[r] вҶ‘N * вҶ‘N / Real.log вҶ‘N * вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.Icc 3 r, Real.log^[i] вҶ‘N
problems.docstring:
Let $R(N)$ be the maximal such size. Results of Bleicher and ErdЕ‘s from [BlEr75] and [BlEr76b] imply that
$$
R(N) \le \frac{1}{\log 2} \log_r N \left( \frac{N}{\log N} \prod_{i=3}^{r} \log_i N \right),
$$
valid for any $k \ge 4$ with $\log_k N \ge k$ and any $r \ge 1$ with $\log_{2r} N \ge 1$. (In these bounds $\log_i n$ denotes the $i$-fold iterated logarithm.)

[BlEr75] Bleicher, M. N. and ErdЕ‘s, P., _The number of distinct subsums of $\sum \sb{1}\spN\,1/i$_. Math. Comp. (1975), 29-42.
[BlEr76b] Bleicher, Michael N. and ErdЕ‘s, Paul, _Denominators of Egyptian fractions. II_. Illinois J. Math. (1976), 598-613.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (c : в„• вҶ’ Bool), вҲғ x d, 0 < x вҲ§ x < d вҲ§ вҲғ C, c x = C вҲ§ c (x + d) = C вҲ§ c (x + 2 * d) = C
problems.docstring:
If в„• is $2$-coloured then there must exist a monochromatic three-term arithmetic progression
$x,x+d,x+2d$ such that $d>x$.

This was first proved by Brown and Landman [BrLa99], who in fact show that this is always possible
with $d>f(x)$ for any increasing function $f$.

This was formalized in Lean by Alexeev using Aristotle and ChatGPT.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ n вүҘ 1,
    вҲҖ k вүҘ 1,
      k вүӨ n вҶ’
        IsGreatest
          {x |
            вҲғ S,
              вҲғ (_ : S вҠҶ Finset.Icc 1 n) (_ : S.card = k) (_ : S.gcd id = 1), Erdos434.Nat.NcardUnrepresentable вҶ‘S = x}
          (Erdos434.Nat.NcardUnrepresentable (Set.Icc (n - k + 1) n))
problems.docstring:
Let $k \le n$. Out of all $A\subseteq\{1, \dots, n\}$ (with $\text{gcd}(A) = 1$) of size $|A| = k$,
does $A = \{n, n - 1, \dots, n - k + 1\}$ maximise the number of integers
not representable as the sum of finitely many elements from $A$ (with repetitions allowed)?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (n k : в„•),
  1 вүӨ n вҶ’
    1 вүӨ k вҶ’
      k вүӨ n вҶ’
        IsGreatest
          {x |
            вҲғ S,
              вҲғ (_ : S вҠҶ Finset.Icc 1 n) (_ : S.card = k) (_ : S.gcd id = 1), Erdos434.Nat.NcardUnrepresentable вҶ‘S = x}
          (Erdos434.Nat.NcardUnrepresentable sorry)
problems.docstring:
Let $k \le n$. What choice of $A\subseteq\{1, \dots, n\}$ (with $\text{gcd}(A) = 1$) of size $|A| = k$
maximises the number of integers not representable as the sum of finitely
many elements from $A$ (with repetitions allowed)?
Is it $\{n, n - 1, \dots, n - k + 1\}$?

problems.statement: (fun x => вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 вҢҠxвҢӢвӮҠ, вҶ‘(Erdos394.t 2 n)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun x => вҶ‘x ^ 2 / Real.log вҶ‘x
problems.docstring: Since $t_2(p)=p-1$ for prime $p$ it is trivial that $\sum_{n\leq x}t_2(n)\gg \frac{x^2}{\log x}$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (k : в„•), 2 вүӨ k вҶ’ k < 10 вҶ’ Erdos394.t k (Nat.factorial 10) < Erdos394.t (k - 1) (Nat.factorial 10) - 1
problems.docstring: They proved (with Selfridge) that this holds for $n=10$.

problems.statement:
(fun x => вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 вҢҠxвҢӢвӮҠ, вҶ‘(Erdos394.t 2 n)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun x =>
  вҶ‘x ^ 2 * (Real.log (Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘x)) / Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘x))
problems.docstring:
In [ErGr80] they mention a conjecture of ErdЕ‘s that the sum is $o(x^2)$. This was proved by ErdЕ‘s
and Hall [ErHa78], who proved that in fact
$\sum_{n\leq x}t_2(n)\ll \frac{\log\log\log x}{\log\log x}x^2.$

problems.statement: True вҶ” {n | вҲҖ (k : в„•), 2 вүӨ k вҶ’ k < n вҶ’ Erdos394.t k n.factorial < Erdos394.t (k - 1) n.factorial - 1}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
They ask about the behaviour of $t_{n-3}(n!)$ and also ask whether, for infinitely many $n$,
$t_k(n!)< t_{k-1}(n!)-1$ for all $1\leq k < n$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ c > 0, (fun x => вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 вҢҠxвҢӢвӮҠ, вҶ‘(Erdos394.t 2 n)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun x => вҶ‘x ^ 2 / Real.log вҶ‘x ^ c
problems.docstring: Is it true that $\sum_{n\leq x}t_2(n)\ll \frac{x^2}{(\log x)^c}$ for some $c>0$?

problems.statement: вҲҖ c < Real.log 2, (fun x => вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 вҢҠxвҢӢвӮҠ, вҶ‘(Erdos394.t 2 n)) =o[Filter.atTop] fun x => x ^ 2 / Real.log x ^ c
problems.docstring: ErdЕ‘s and Hall conjecture that the sum is $o(x^2/(\log x)^c)$ for any $c<\log 2$.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ k вүҘ 2,
    (fun x => вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 вҢҠxвҢӢвӮҠ, вҶ‘(Erdos394.t (k + 1) n)) =o[Filter.atTop] fun x =>
      вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 вҢҠxвҢӢвӮҠ, вҶ‘(Erdos394.t k n)
problems.docstring: Is it true that, for $k\geq 2$, $\sum_{n\leq x}t_{k+1}(n) =o\left(\sum_{n\leq x}t_k(n)\right)?$

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (A : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
  (вҲҖ n > 0, IsLeast {x | вҲғ t, вҲғ (_ : вҲғ a, n.factorial = вҲҸ i, a i вҲ§ Monotone a вҲ§ a (Fin.last t) вүӨ n), t + 1 = x} (A n)) вҶ’
    (fun n => вҶ‘(A n) - вҶ‘n + вҶ‘n / Real.log вҶ‘n) =o[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘n / Real.log вҶ‘n
problems.docstring:
If we change the condition to $a_t \leq n$ it can be shown that
$$
  A(n) = n - \frac{n}{\log n} + o\left(\frac{n}{\log n}\right)
$$

problems.statement:
(вҲҖ (A : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
    (вҲҖ n > 0,
        IsLeast {x | вҲғ t, вҲғ (_ : вҲғ a, n.factorial = вҲҸ i, a i вҲ§ Monotone a вҲ§ a (Fin.last t) вүӨ n ^ 2), t + 1 = x} (A n)) вҶ’
      (fun n => вҶ‘(A n) - вҶ‘n / 2 + вҶ‘n / (2 * Real.log вҶ‘n)) =o[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘n / Real.log вҶ‘n) вҶ’
  вҲҖ (A : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
    (вҲҖ n > 0,
        IsLeast {x | вҲғ t, вҲғ (_ : вҲғ a, n.factorial = вҲҸ i, a i вҲ§ Monotone a вҲ§ a (Fin.last t) вүӨ n), t + 1 = x} (A n)) вҶ’
      (fun n => вҶ‘(A n) - вҶ‘n + вҶ‘n / Real.log вҶ‘n) =o[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘n / Real.log вҶ‘n
problems.docstring:
Cambie has observed that a positive answer follows from the result above with $a_t \leq n$, simply
by pairing variables together, e.g. taking $a'_i = a_{2i-1}a_{2i}$ (and the lower bound follows from
Stirling's approximation).

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (A : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
  (вҲҖ n > 0,
      IsLeast {x | вҲғ t, вҲғ (_ : вҲғ a, n.factorial = вҲҸ i, a i вҲ§ Monotone a вҲ§ a (Fin.last t) вүӨ n ^ 2), t + 1 = x} (A n)) вҶ’
    (fun n => вҶ‘(A n) - вҶ‘n / 2 + вҶ‘n / (2 * Real.log вҶ‘n)) =o[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘n / Real.log вҶ‘n
problems.docstring:
Let $A(n)$ denote the least value of $t$ such that
$$
  n! = a_1 \cdots a_t
$$
with $a_1 \leq \cdots \leq a_t\leq n^2$. Then
$$
  A(n) = \frac{n}{2} - \frac{n}{2\log n} + o\left(\frac{n}{\log n}\right).
$$

problems.statement: вҲҖ card > 12, Erdos494.Erdos494Unique 4 card
вҲғ A B, A.card = B.card вҲ§ Erdos494.prodMultiset A 3 = Erdos494.prodMultiset B 3 вҲ§ A вү  B
вҲҖ k > 2, вҲҖб¶  (card : в„•) in Filter.atTop, Erdos494.Erdos494Unique k card
problems.docstring:
Gordon, Fraenkel, and Straus [GRS62] proved that the claim is true for all $k > 2$ when
$|A|$ is sufficiently large.

problems.statement: вҲҖ k > 2, В¬Erdos494.Erdos494Unique k k
problems.docstring: Kruyt noted that the conjecture fails when $|A| = k$, by rotating $A$ around an appropriate point.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (card : в„•), (вҲғ l, card = 2 ^ l) вҶ’ В¬Erdos494.Erdos494Unique 2 card
вҲҖ card > 6, Erdos494.Erdos494Unique 3 card
problems.docstring:
Selfridge and Straus [SeSt58] also showed that the conjecture is true when
1) $k = 3$ and $|A| > 6$ or
2) $k = 4$ and $|A| > 12$.
More generally, they proved that $A$ is determined by $A_k$ (and $|A|$) if $|A|$ is divisible by
a prime greater than $k$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (k card p : в„•), Nat.Prime p вҶ’ k вҲҲ Set.Ioo 0 p вҶ’ p вҲЈ card вҶ’ Erdos494.Erdos494Unique k card
вҲҖ k > 2, В¬Erdos494.Erdos494Unique k (2 * k)
problems.docstring:
Similarly, Tao noted that the conjecture fails when $|A| = 2k$, by taking $A$ to be a set of
the total sum 0 and considering $-A$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (card : в„•), (вҲҖ (l : в„•), card вү  2 ^ l) вҶ’ Erdos494.Erdos494Unique 2 card
problems.docstring:
Selfridge and Straus [SeSt58] showed that the conjecture is true when $k = 2$ and
$|A| \ne 2^l$ for $l \ge 0$.
They also gave counterexamples when $k = 2$ and $|A| = 2^l$.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))),
    EuclideanGeometry.NonTrilinear вҶ‘A вҶ’ A.card / 2 вүӨ EuclideanGeometry.distinctDistances A
problems.docstring:
Let $A\subset \mathbb{R}^2$ be a set of $n$ points with no three on a line.
Does $A$ determine at least $\lfloor n/2\rfloor$ distinct distances?

problems.statement:
False вҶ”
  вҲҖ (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))),
    A.Nonempty вҶ’
      EuclideanGeometry.NonTrilinear вҶ‘A вҶ’
        вҲғ a, вҲғ (_ : a вҲҲ A), A.card / 2 вүӨ EuclideanGeometry.distinctDistancesFrom A a - 1
problems.docstring:
Let $A\subset \mathbb{R}^2$ be a set of $n$ points with no three on a line.
Must there exist a single point from which there are at least $\lfloor n/2\rfloor$ distinct
distances?

This question has been answered negatively by Xichuan in the
[comments](https://www.erdosproblems.com/forum/thread/1082), who gave a set of $42$ points in
$\mathbb{R}^2$, with no three on a line, such that each point determines only $20$ distinct distances.

A smaller counterexample has been formalised here: it comprised of $8$ points, where each point only
determines $3$ distances.

This counterexample has originally been found by Heiko Harborth.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {V : Type u_1} {G : SimpleGraph V} [inst : Fintype V],
  True вҶ”
    (вҲҖ (V' : Set V),
        2 * V'.ncard + 1 вүҘ Fintype.card V вҶ’ 50 * (SimpleGraph.induce V' G).edgeSet.ncard > Fintype.card V ^ 2) вҶ’
      В¬G.CliqueFree 3
problems.docstring:
Let G be a graph with n vertices such that every subgraph on вүҘ $n/2$
vertices has more than $n^2/50$ edges. Must G contain a triangle?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (f : Polynomial в„Ө),
  Irreducible f вҶ’
    f.natDegree вү  0 вҶ’
      (вҲҖб¶  (n : в„Ө) in Filter.atTop, 1 вүӨ Polynomial.eval n f) вҶ’
        (fun x => x * Real.log x) =O[Filter.atTop] Erdos975.Erdos975Sum f
(fun x => Erdos975.Erdos975Sum (Polynomial.X ^ 2 + 1) x - 3 / Real.pi * x * Real.log x) =O[Filter.atTop] id
problems.docstring:
More concrete example for $f(n) = n^2 + 1$, where the asymptote is
$\sum_{n \le x} \tau(n^2 + 1) \sim \frac{3}{\pi} x \log x + O(x)$. See Tao's blog [T].

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (f : Polynomial в„Ө),
  Irreducible f вҶ’
    (вҲҖб¶  (n : в„Ө) in Filter.atTop, 1 вүӨ Polynomial.eval n f) вҶ’
      Erdos975.Erdos975Sum f =O[Filter.atTop] fun x => x * Real.log x
problems.docstring: The correctness of the growth rate is shown in [Va39] (lower bound) and [Er52b] (upper bound).

problems.statement:
вҲғ c > 0,
  Filter.Tendsto (fun x => Erdos975.Erdos975Sum (Polynomial.X ^ 2 + 1) x / (x * Real.log x)) Filter.atTop (nhds c)

True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (f : Polynomial в„Ө),
    f.natDegree вү  0 вҶ’
      Irreducible f вҶ’
        (вҲҖб¶  (n : в„Ө) in Filter.atTop, 1 вүӨ Polynomial.eval n f) вҶ’
          вҲғ c > 0, Filter.Tendsto (fun x => Erdos975.Erdos975Sum f x / (x * Real.log x)) Filter.atTop (nhds c)
problems.docstring:
For an irreducible polynomial $f \in \mathbb{Z}[x]$ with $f(n) \ge 1$ for sufficiently large $n$,
does there exists a constant $c = c(f) > 0$ such that
$\sum_{n \le x} \tau(f(n)) \approx c \cdot x \log x$?

Note that it is unclear whether the polynomial should have integer coefficients or merely be
integer-valued. We assume the former.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (f : Polynomial в„Ө),
  Irreducible f вҶ’
    (вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, 1 вүӨ Polynomial.eval (вҶ‘n) f) вҶ’
      f.degree = 2 вҶ’
        вҲҖ (c : в„қ),
          c = sorry вҶ’
            0 < c вҲ§ Filter.Tendsto (fun x => Erdos975.Erdos975Sum f x / (x * Real.log x)) Filter.atTop (nhds c)
problems.docstring:
When $f$ is an irreducible quadratic polynomial, the question is answered first by Hooley [Ho63].
More compact expression of the constant in terms of Hurwitz class numbers (when $a = 1$)
is given by McKey in [Mc95], [Mc97], [Mc99].

TODO: formalize Hurwitz class numbers and the expression of the constant in terms of them.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (N : в„•), вҲҖ A вҠҶ Finset.Icc 1 (2 * N), A.card вүҘ N + 2 вҶ’ вҲғ a вҲҲ A, вҲғ b вҲҲ A, a вү  b вҲ§ a + b вҲҲ A
problems.docstring:
It is a classical folklore fact that if $A\subseteq \{1,\ldots,2N\}$ has size $\geq N+2$ then
there are distinct $a,b\in A$ such that $a+b\in A$, which establishes the $k=2$ case.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (k : в„•), 3 вүӨ k вҶ’ вҲғ Оө > 0, вҲҖб¶  (N : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҶ‘(Erdos865.f N k) вүӨ (2 / 3 - Оө) * вҶ‘N
problems.docstring:
Choi, ErdЕ‘s, and SzemerГ©di [CES75] have proved that, for all $k\geq 3$, there exists $\epsilon_k>0$
such that (for large enough $N$) $f_k(N)\leq \left(\frac{2}{3}-\epsilon_k\right)N$.

problems.statement:
вҲғ C > 0,
  вҲҖб¶  (N : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
    вҲҖ A вҠҶ Finset.Icc 1 N,
      вҶ‘A.card вүҘ 5 / 8 * вҶ‘N + C вҶ’ вҲғ a вҲҲ A, вҲғ b вҲҲ A, вҲғ c вҲҲ A, a вү  b вҲ§ a вү  c вҲ§ b вү  c вҲ§ a + b вҲҲ A вҲ§ a + c вҲҲ A вҲ§ b + c вҲҲ A
problems.docstring:
There exists a constant $C>0$ such that, for all large $N$, if $A\subseteq \{1,\ldots,N\}$ has
size at least $\frac{5}{8}N+C$ then there are distinct $a,b,c\in A$ such that $a+b,a+c,b+c\in A$.

A problem of ErdЕ‘s and SГіs (also earlier considered by Choi, ErdЕ‘s, and SzemerГ©di [CES75], but ErdЕ‘s
had forgotten this).

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (k : в„•),
  2 вүӨ k вҶ’
    Asymptotics.IsEquivalent Filter.atTop (fun N => вҶ‘(Erdos865.f N k)) fun N =>
      1 / 2 * (1 + вҲ‘ r вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 (k - 2), (1 / 4) ^ r) * вҶ‘N
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and SГіs conjectured that
$f_k(N)\sim \frac{1}{2}\left(1+\sum_{1\leq r\leq k-2}\frac{1}{4^r}\right) N$,
where $f_k(N)$ is the minimal size of a subset of $\{1, \dots, N\}$ guaranteeing $k$ elements
have all pairwise sums in the set.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (k : в„•), Erdos56.WeaklyDivisible k вҲ…
вҲҖ {k : в„•}, k вү  0 вҶ’ вҲҖ (l : в„•), Erdos56.WeaklyDivisible k {l}
problems.docstring: A singleton is `k`-weakly divisble if `k вү  0`.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {k : в„•}, k вү  0 вҶ’ Erdos56.MaxWeaklyDivisible 1 k = 1
вҲҖ {A : Finset в„•}, A.Nonempty вҶ’ В¬Erdos56.WeaklyDivisible 0 A
problems.docstring: No non-empty set is `1`-weakly divisible.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (k : в„•), Erdos56.MaxWeaklyDivisible 0 k = 0

(вҲҖ k > 0, вҲҖ N вүҘ Nat.nth Nat.Prime (k - 1), Erdos56.MaxWeaklyDivisible N k = (Erdos56.FirstPrimesMultiples N k).card) вҶ”
  False
problems.docstring:
Suppose $A \subseteq \{1,\dots,N\}$ is such that there are no $k+1$ elements of $A$ which are
relatively prime. An example is the set of all multiples of the first $k$ primes.
Is this the largest such set?  To avoid trivial counterexamples, we must insist that $N$ be at
least the $k$th prime.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (N k : в„•), 1 вүӨ N вҶ’ Erdos56.WeaklyDivisible k (Erdos56.FirstPrimesMultiples N k)
problems.docstring:
An example of a `k`-weakly divisible set is the subset of `{1, ..., N}`
containing the multiples of the first `k` primes.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {A : Finset в„•}, Erdos56.WeaklyDivisible 0 A вҶ” A = вҲ…
вҲҖ (k : в„•), (Erdos56.FirstPrimesMultiples 1 k).card = 0
вҲҖ (N : в„•), (Erdos56.FirstPrimesMultiples N 0).card = 0
вҲҖ (N : в„•), Erdos56.MaxWeaklyDivisible N 0 = 0
вҲҖ n вҲү {0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 7, 8, 16}, 1 < Erdos889.vвӮҖ n
problems.docstring:
$v_0(n) > 1$ for all $n$ except $n$ = 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 7, 8, 16

[ErSe67] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Selfridge, J. L., Some problems on the prime factors of consecutive integers. Illinois J. Math. (1967), 428--430.

problems.statement: True вҶ” {n | Erdos889.v_l 1 n = 1}.Finite
problems.docstring:
Does $v_1(n) = 1$ have finite solutions?

[ErSe67] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Selfridge, J. L., Some problems on the prime factors of consecutive integers. Illinois J. Math. (1967), 428--430.

problems.statement: Filter.Tendsto Erdos889.vвӮҖ Filter.atTop (nhds вҠӨ)
problems.docstring:
Let $v(n,k)$ count the prime factors of $n+k$ which
do not divide $n+i$ for $0\leq i < k$. Is it true that
$v_0(n)=\max_{k\geq 0}v(n,k)\to \infty$ as $n\to \infty$?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (l : в„•), Filter.Tendsto (Erdos889.v_l l) Filter.atTop (nhds вҠӨ)
problems.docstring:
Let $v_l(n) = \max_{k\geq l} v(n,k)$. For every fixed $l$,
$v_l(n) \to \infty$ as $n \to \infty$

[ErSe67] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Selfridge, J. L., Some problems on the prime factors of consecutive integers. Illinois J. Math. (1967), 428--430.

problems.statement: True вҶ” {n | Erdos889.V_l 1 n = 1}.Finite
problems.docstring:
Does $V_1(n) = 1$ have finite solutions?

This is a modification of `erdos_889.variants.v1_eq_1_finite`,
which might make it more amenable to attack according to [ErSe67].

[ErSe67] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Selfridge, J. L., Some problems on the prime factors of consecutive integers. Illinois J. Math. (1967), 428--430.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ k вүҘ 1, вҲҖ Оө > 0, (fun x => вҶ‘x ^ (1 - Оө)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun x => вҶ‘(Erdos323.f k k x)
problems.docstring:
Is it true that $f_{k,k}(x) \gg_\epsilon x^{1-\epsilon}$ for all $\epsilon>0$?

This would have significant applications to Waring's problem. ErdЕ‘s and Graham describe this as
'unattackable by the methods at our disposal'.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (k m : в„•), 1 вүӨ m вҶ’ m < k вҶ’ (fun x => вҶ‘x ^ (вҶ‘m / вҶ‘k)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun x => вҶ‘(Erdos323.f k m x)
problems.docstring: Is it true that if $m < k$ then $f_{k,m}(x) \gg x^{m/k}$ for sufficiently large $x$?

problems.statement: вҲғ c > 0, Asymptotics.IsEquivalent Filter.atTop (fun x => вҶ‘(Erdos323.f 2 2 x)) fun x => c * вҶ‘x / вҲҡ(Real.log вҶ‘x)
problems.docstring:
The case $k=2$ was resolved by Landau, who showed $f_{2,2}(x) \sim \frac{cx}{\sqrt{\log x}}$ for
some constant $c>0$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ k > 2, (fun x => вҶ‘(Erdos323.f k k x)) =o[Filter.atTop] fun x => вҶ‘x
problems.docstring: For $k>2$ it is not known if $f_{k,k}(x)=o(x)$.

problems.statement:
have f := fun n k => 1 / вҶ‘(n + 2).factorial ^ (k + 1);
вҲ‘' (n : в„•), 1 / (вҶ‘(n + 2).factorial - 1) = вҲ‘' (n : в„•) (k : в„•), f n k
problems.docstring: $$\sum_{n=2}^\infty \frac{1}{n!-1} = \sum_{n=2}^\infty \sum_{k=1}^\infty \frac{1}{(n!)^k}$$

problems.statement: True вҶ” Irrational (вҲ‘' (n : в„•), 1 / (вҶ‘(n + 2).factorial - 1))
problems.docstring:
Is
$$\sum_{n=2}^\infty \frac{1}{n!-1}$$
irrational?

problems.statement: Asymptotics.IsEquivalent Filter.atTop (fun k => Real.log вҶ‘(Erdos1095.g k)) fun k => вҶ‘k / Real.log вҶ‘k
problems.docstring: Sorenson, Sorenson, and Webster [SSWE20] give heuristic evidence that $\log g(k) \asymp \frac{k}{\log k}$.

problems.statement: вҲғ f, Filter.Tendsto f Filter.atTop (nhds 0) вҲ§ вҲҖб¶  (k : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҶ‘(Erdos1095.g k) вүӨ Real.exp (вҶ‘k * (1 + f k))
problems.docstring: Ecklund, ErdЕ‘s, and Selfridge [EES74] conjectured $g(k)\leq \exp((1+o(1))k)$.

problems.statement: вҲғ c > 0, вҲҖб¶  (k : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҶ‘(Erdos1095.g k) вүҘ Real.exp (c * вҶ‘k / Real.log вҶ‘k)
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s, Lacampagne, and Selfridge [ELS93] write 'it is clear to every right-thinking person' that
$g(k)\geq\exp(c\frac{k}{\log k})$ for some constant $c>0$.

problems.statement: вҲғ c > 0, (fun k => Real.exp (c * Real.log вҶ‘k ^ 2)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun k => вҶ‘(Erdos1095.g k)
problems.docstring: The current record is $g(k) \gg \exp(c(\log k)^2)$ for some $c>0$, due to Konyagin [Ko99b]. -

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (N : в„•),
  вҲҖ S вҠҶ Finset.Icc 1 N,
    Erdos168.NonTernary S вҶ’
      (вҲҖ T вҠҶ Finset.Icc 1 N, Erdos168.NonTernary T вҶ’ S.card вүӨ T.card вҶ’ T.card = S.card) вҶ’ Erdos168.F N = S.card
problems.docstring:
Sanity check: if `S` is a maximal non ternary subset of `{1,..., N}` then `F N` is given by the
cardinality of `S`

problems.statement: вҲҖ (N : в„•) (S : Finset в„•), S вҲҲ Erdos168.IntervalNonTernarySets N вҶ” Erdos168.NonTernary S вҲ§ S вҠҶ Finset.Icc 1 N
problems.docstring:
Sanity check: elements of `IntervalNonTernarySets N` are precisely non ternary subsets of
`{1,...,N}`

problems.statement: вҲғ x, Filter.Tendsto (fun N => вҶ‘(Erdos168.F N) / вҶ‘N) Filter.atTop (nhds x)
problems.docstring: The limit $F(N)/N$ as $N \to \infty$ exists. (proved by Graham, Spencer, and Witsenhausen)

problems.statement: Erdos168.F 0 = 0
Filter.Tendsto (fun N => вҶ‘(Erdos168.F N) / вҶ‘N) Filter.atTop (nhds sorry)
problems.docstring: What is the limit $F(N)/N$ as $N \to \infty$?

problems.statement: Erdos168.F 3 = 2
Erdos168.F 2 = 2
Erdos168.F 1 = 1
True вҶ” Irrational (Filter.limsup (fun N => вҶ‘(Erdos168.F N) / вҶ‘N) Filter.atTop)
problems.docstring: Is the limit $F(N)/N$ as $N \to \infty$ irrational?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (N : в„•), Erdos170.TrivialRuler N вҲҲ Erdos170.PerfectRulersLengthN N
problems.docstring: Sanity Check: the trivial ruler is actually a perfect ruler if $K \geq N$

problems.statement:
вҲғ x вҲҲ Set.Icc Erdos170.lower_bound Erdos170.upper_bound,
  Filter.Tendsto (fun N => вҶ‘(Erdos170.F N) / вҲҡвҶ‘N) Filter.atTop (nhds x)
problems.docstring:
The existence of the limit has been proved by ErdЕ‘s and GГЎl [ErGa48].
The lower bound has been proven by Leech [Le56], who refined an argument of RГ©dei and RГ©nyi.
The upper bound is due to Wichmann [Wi63].

problems.statement: Filter.Tendsto (fun N => вҶ‘(Erdos170.F N) / вҲҡвҶ‘N) Filter.atTop (nhds sorry)
problems.docstring: The problem is to determine the limit of the sequence $\frac{F(N)}{\sqrt{N}}$ as $N \to \infty$.

problems.statement: вҲғ A, IsSidon A вҲ§ 0 < Erdos329.sidonUpperDensity A
problems.docstring: It is possible to construct a Sidon set with positive density.

problems.statement: вҲғ A, IsSidon A вҲ§ Erdos329.sidonUpperDensity A = 1 / вҲҡ2
problems.docstring:
KrГјckeberg ([Kr61]) exhibited an infinite Sidon set `A` with
`sidonUpperDensity A = 1 / Real.sqrt 2`, improving ErdЕ‘sвҖҷ earlier
`1 / 2` lower bound.

[Kr61] KrГјckeberg, Fritz, $B\sb{2}$-Folgen und verwandte Zahlenfolgen. J. Reine Angew. Math. (1961), 53-60.

problems.statement:
(вҲҖ (A : Finset в„•), IsSidon вҶ‘A вҶ’ вҲғ D n, вҶ‘A вҠҶ D вҲ§ IsPerfectDifferenceSet D n) вҶ’
  sSup {x | вҲғ A, вҲғ (_ : IsSidon A), Erdos329.sidonUpperDensity A = x} = 1
problems.docstring:
If any finite Sidon set can be embedded in a perfect difference set,
then the maximum density would be 1.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), IsSidon A вҶ’ Erdos329.sidonUpperDensity A вүӨ 1
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and TurГЎn [ErTu41] proved the upper bound of 1.

[ErTu41] ErdЕ‘s, P. and TurГЎn, P., On a problem of Sidon in additive number theory, and on some related problems. J. London Math. Soc. (1941), 212-215.

problems.statement: sSup {x | вҲғ A, вҲғ (_ : IsSidon A), Erdos329.sidonUpperDensity A = x} = sorry
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘s Problem 329.**
Let `A вҠҶ в„•` be a Sidon set. How large can
`lim sup_{N вҶ’ вҲһ} |A вҲ© {1,вҖҰ,N}| / N^{1/2}`
be?

problems.statement: вҲғ A, IsSidon A вҲ§ Erdos329.sidonUpperDensity A вүҘ 1 / 2
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s proved that upper density `1 / 2` can be attained; in particular,
there exists a Sidon set whose upper density is *at least* `1 / 2`.

problems.statement:
sSup {x | вҲғ A, вҲғ (_ : IsSidon A), Erdos329.sidonUpperDensity A = x} = 1 вҶ’
  вҲҖ (A : Finset в„•), IsSidon вҶ‘A вҶ’ вҲғ D n, вҶ‘A вҠҶ D вҲ§ IsPerfectDifferenceSet D n
problems.docstring:
The converse: if the maximum density is 1, then any finite Sidon set
can be embedded in a perfect difference set.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ C, вҲғ (_ : C > 0), вҲҖ (n : в„•), вҶ‘((ArithmeticFunction.sigma 1) n) > C * вҶ‘n вҶ’ вҲғ s вҠҶ n.properDivisors, n = s.sum id
problems.docstring:
Is there an absolute constant $C > 0$ such that every integer $n$ with
$\sigma(n) > Cn$ is the distinct sum of proper divisors of $n$?

This has been solved in the affirmative by Larsen - in fact, for any $\epsilon>0$ there exists $L$
such that if $n$ has only prime divisors $>L$ and $\sigma(n)>(2+\epsilon)n$ then $n$ is the distinct
sum of proper divisors of $n$.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (C : в„қ),
  0 < C вҶ’ (вҲҖ (n : в„•), вҶ‘((ArithmeticFunction.sigma 1) n) > C * вҶ‘n вҶ’ вҲғ s вҠҶ n.properDivisors, n = s.sum id) вҶ’ 2 < C
problems.docstring:
Show that if the constant $C > 0$ is such that every integer $n$ with
$\sigma(n) > Cn$ is the distinct sum of proper divisors of $n$, then we
must have $C > 2$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” Filter.liminf Erdos770.h Filter.atTop = вҠӨ
problems.docstring: Does `liminf h n = вҲһ`?

problems.statement: {n | Erdos770.h n = 3}.Infinite
problems.docstring: It is probably true that `h n = 3` for infinitely many `n`.

problems.statement: Set.Unbounded (fun x1 x2 => x1 вүӨ x2) (ENat.toNat '' (Erdos770.h '' Odd))
problems.docstring: For odd `n`, the values of `h n` form an unbounded set.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (p : в„•), Nat.Prime p вҶ’ вҲғ a, {n | Erdos770.h n = вҶ‘p}.HasDensity a
problems.docstring: For every prime `p`, does the density of integers with `h n = p` exist?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ Оө > 0,
    вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
      have p := sSup {m | Nat.Prime m вҲ§ m - 1 вҲЈ n};
      вҶ‘p > вҶ‘n ^ Оө вҶ’ Erdos770.h n = вҶ‘p
problems.docstring:
Is it true that if `p` is the greatest prime such that `p - 1 вҲЈ n` and `p > n ^ Оө`, then
`h n = p`?

problems.statement: вҲҖ {n : в„•}, 2 < n вҶ’ (Erdos770.h n = вҶ‘n + 1 вҶ” Nat.Prime (n + 1))
problems.docstring: `n + 1` is prime iff `h n = n + 1`.

problems.statement: True вҶ” Filter.Tendsto (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos893.f (2 * n)) / вҶ‘(Erdos893.f n)) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Does the limit $\lim_{n\to\infty} \frac{f(2n)}{f(n)}$ tend to infinity?

(Other finite limits have been ruled out by [KoLu25], see below)

problems.statement: В¬BddAbove (Set.range fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos893.f (2 * n)) / вҶ‘(Erdos893.f n))
problems.docstring:
KovaДҚ and Luca [KoLu25] (building on a heuristic independently found by
Cambie (personal communication)) have shown that there is no finite limit, in that
$\lim_{n\to\infty} \frac{f(2n)}{f(n)}$ is unbounded.

problems.statement: True вҶ” Erdos1145.Erdos1145Prop
problems.docstring:
Let $A=\{1\leq a_1 < a_2 < \cdots\}$ and $B=\{1\leq b_1 < b_2 < \cdots\}$ be sets of integers with
$a_n/b_n\to 1$.

If $A+B$ contains all sufficiently large positive integers then is it true that
$\limsup 1_A\ast 1_B(n)=\infty$?

A conjecture of ErdЕ‘s and SГЎrkГ¶zy.

problems.statement:
Erdos1145.Erdos1145Prop вҶ’
  вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), (A + A)б¶ң.Finite вҶ’ Filter.limsup (fun n => вҶ‘(AdditiveCombinatorics.sumRep A n)) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ
problems.docstring: A stronger form of [erdosproblems.com/28].

problems.statement: В¬вҲғ n, n.Powerful вҲ§ (n + 1).Powerful вҲ§ (n + 2).Powerful
problems.docstring: There is no consecutive triple of powerful numbers.

problems.statement: В¬вҲғ n, n.Powerful вҲ§ (n + 1).Powerful вҲ§ (n + 2).Powerful вҲ§ (n + 3).Powerful
problems.docstring:
There is no quadruple of powerful numbers, since at least one of the four numbers must be
$2 \pmod{4}$, which cannot be powerful (since $2$ divides it, but $2^2$ does not).

problems.statement:
вҲғ c,
  вҲғ (_ : c > 0), вҲҖ (k : в„•), вҶ‘(Nat.nth Nat.Powerful (k + 2)) - вҶ‘(Nat.nth Nat.Powerful k) > вҶ‘(Nat.nth Nat.Powerful k) ^ c
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s [Er76d] conjectured a stronger statement: if $n_k$ is the $k$th powerful number,
then $n_{k+2} - n_k > n_k^c$ for some constant $c > 0$.

[Er76d] ErdЕ‘s, P., Problems and results on number theoretic properties of consecutive integers and related questions. Proceedings of the Fifth Manitoba Conference on Numerical Mathematics (Univ. Manitoba, Winnipeg, Man., 1975) (1976), 25-44.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ t вүҘ 2, Irrational (вҲ‘' (n : в„•), вҶ‘(n + 1).divisors.card / вҶ‘t ^ n)
problems.docstring:
Is $\sum_n \frac{d(n)}{t^n}$ irrational, where $t вүҘ 2$ is an integer.

Solution: True (proved by ErdЕ‘s, see ErdЕ‘s Problems website)

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
    (вҲҖ (n : в„•), a n вү  0) вҶ’
      Filter.Tendsto a Filter.atTop Filter.atTop вҶ’
        Irrational (вҲ‘' (n : в„•), вҶ‘(n + 1).divisors.card / вҶ‘(вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 n, a i))
problems.docstring:
Let $a_n \to \infty$ be a sequence of non-zero natural numbers. Is
$\sum_n \frac{d(n)}{(a_1 ... a_n)}$ irrational, where $d(n)$ is the number of divisors of $n$?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ в„Ө),
    (вҲҖ (n : в„•), a n вү  0) вҶ’
      Monotone a вҶ’
        Filter.Tendsto a Filter.atTop Filter.atTop вҶ’
          Irrational (вҲ‘' (n : в„•), вҶ‘(n + 1).divisors.card / вҶ‘(вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 n, a i))
problems.docstring:
Let $a_n \to \infty$ be a monotone sequence of non-zero natural numbers.
Is $\sum_n \frac{d(n)}{(a_1 ... a_n)}$ irrational, where $d(n)$ is the number of divisors of $n$?

Solution: True (proved by ErdЕ‘s and Straus, see ErdЕ‘s Problems website).

problems.statement: True вҶ” Filter.Tendsto Erdos153.f Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
problems.docstring: Must `lim f n = вҲһ`?

problems.statement: Erdos9.Erdos9A.Infinite
problems.docstring:
The set is known to be infinite. In [Er77c] ErdЕ‘s credits Schinzel with proving that there are
infinitely many odd integers not of this form, but gives no reference.

[Er77c] ErdЕ‘s, P., _Problems and results on combinatorial number theory. III._.

problems.statement: 1 вҲҲ Erdos9.Erdos9A
True вҶ” 0 < Erdos9.Erdos9A.upperDensity
problems.docstring:
Is the upper density of the set of odd numbers that cannot be expressed as a prime plus
two powers of 2 positive?

problems.statement: 3 вҲҲ Erdos9.Erdos9A
5 вҲү Erdos9.Erdos9A
вҲҖ {a : в„• вҶ’ в„қ}, 1 < a 0 вҶ’ StrictMono a вҶ’ Erdos951.Erdos951_prop a вҶ’ IsBeurlingPrimes a
problems.docstring: If `a` has property `Erdos951_prop` and `1 < a 0`, then `a` is a set of Beurling prime numbers.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ в„қ),
  IsBeurlingPrimes a вҶ’
    (fun x => вҶ‘(BeurlingInteger a вҲ© Set.Iic x).ncard - x) =o[Filter.atTop] Real.log вҶ’ a = Nat.cast вҲҳ Nat.nth Nat.Prime
problems.docstring:
Beurling conjectured that if the number of Beurling integer in `[1, x]`
is `x + o(log x)`, then `a` must be the sequence of primes.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ в„қ), Erdos951.Erdos951_prop a вҶ’ вҲҖ (x : в„қ), {i | a i вүӨ x}.ncard вүӨ вҢҠxвҢӢвӮҠ.primeCounting
problems.docstring: If `a` has property `Erdos951_prop`, is it true that `#{a i вүӨ x} вүӨ ПҖ x`?

problems.statement: {n | primeGap n < primeGap (n + 1) вҲ§ primeGap (n + 1) < primeGap (n + 2)}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
There are infinitely many $n$ such that $d_n < d_{n+1} < d_{n+2}$, where $d$
denotes the prime gap function.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (m : в„•), {n | вҲҖ i вҲҲ Finset.range m, primeGap (n + i) < primeGap (n + i + 1)}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
For all $m$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that $d_n < d_{n+1} < \dots < d_{n+m}$,
where $d$ denotes the prime gap function.

Proved by Banks, Freiberg, and Turnage-Butterbaugh [BFT15] with an application of the
Maynard-Tao machinery concerning bounded gaps between primes [Ma15]

problems.statement: вҲҖ (m : в„•), {n | вҲҖ i вҲҲ Finset.range m, primeGap (n + i) > primeGap (n + i + 1)}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
For all $m$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that $d_n > d_{n+1} \dots > d_{n+m}$,
where $d$ denotes the prime gap function.

Proved by Banks, Freiberg, and Turnage-Butterbaugh [BFT15] with an application of the
Maynard-Tao machinery concerning bounded gaps between primes [Ma15]

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ o, o =o[Filter.atTop] 1 вҲ§ вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҶ‘(AdditiveCombinatorics.sumRep Nat.Powerful n) вүӨ вҶ‘n ^ o n
problems.docstring: Let $A$ be the set of powerful numbers. Is is true that $1_A\ast 1_A(n)=n^{o(1)}$ for every $n$?

problems.statement: True вҶ” Irrational (вҲ‘' (n : в„•), вҶ‘n.totient / 2 ^ n)
problems.docstring:
Is
$$\sum_{n} \frac{\phi(n)}{2^n}$$
irrational? Here $\phi$ is the Euler totient function.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ c > 0,
    вҲҖ (A : Set (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))),
      MeasurableSet A вҶ’
        вҶ‘(MeasureTheory.volume A) вүҘ вҶ‘c вҶ’
          вҲғ t,
            (вҲҖ (p : Fin 3), t.points p вҲҲ A) вҲ§ EuclideanGeometry.triangle_area (t.points 0) (t.points 1) (t.points 2) = 1
problems.docstring:
Is there some $c > 0$ such that every measurable $A \subseteq \mathbb{R}^2$ of measure $\geq c$
contains the vertices of a triangle of area 1?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ c, вҲҖ (n k : в„•), n > 0 вҶ’ Erdos20.f n k < c k ^ n
problems.docstring: Is it true that $f(n,k) < c_k^n$ for some constant $c_k>0$ and for all $n > 0$?

problems.statement: вҲҖ {Оұ : Type} (S : Set Оұ), Erdos20.IsSunflowerWithKernel вҲ… S
вҲҖ {Оұ : Type} (S A : Set Оұ), Erdos20.IsSunflowerWithKernel {A} S
Erdos20.f 0 1 = 1
вҲҖ {Оұ : Type}, Erdos20.IsSunflower вҲ…
вҲҖ {Оұ : Type} (A : Set Оұ), Erdos20.IsSunflower {A}
True вҶ” вҲҖ k вүҘ 3, (Erdos141.consecutivePrimeArithmeticProgressions k).Infinite
problems.docstring: Fix a $k \geq 3$. Is it true that there are infinitely many arithmetic prime progressions of length $k$?

problems.statement: Erdos141.Set.IsPrimeProgressionOfLength {3, 5, 7} 3
problems.docstring: The first three odd primes are an example of three consecutive primes.

problems.statement: вҲҖ k вүҘ 3, k вүӨ 10 вҶ’ вҲғ s, Erdos141.Set.IsAPAndPrimeProgressionOfLength s k
problems.docstring: The existence of such progressions has been verified for $kвүӨ10$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” (Erdos141.consecutivePrimeArithmeticProgressions 3).Infinite
problems.docstring: It is open, even for $k=3$, whether there are infinitely many such progressions.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ s, Erdos141.Set.IsAPAndPrimeProgressionOfLength s 11
problems.docstring: Are there $11$ consecutive primes in arithmetic progression?
