problems.statement: вҲғ s, Erdos141.Set.IsAPAndPrimeProgressionOfLength s 3
problems.docstring: There are 3 consecutive primes in arithmetic progression.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ k вүҘ 3, вҲғ s, Erdos141.Set.IsAPAndPrimeProgressionOfLength s k
problems.docstring: Let $kвүҘ3$. Are there $k$ consecutive primes in arithmetic progression?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҲғ k, k вү  0 вҲ§ (n + k).minFac > k ^ 2 + 1
problems.docstring:
Is it true that, for all sufficiently large $n$, there exists some $k$ such that
\[
p(n+k)>k^2+1,
\]
where $p(m)$ denotes the least prime factor of $m$?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ Оө > 0, вҲғ C > 0, В¬вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҲғ k, k вү  0 вҲ§ вҶ‘(n + k).minFac > Real.exp ((1 + Оө) * вҲҡвҶ‘k) + C
problems.docstring:
Can one prove this is false if we replace $k^2+1$ by $e^{(1+\epsilon)\sqrt{k}}+C_\epsilon$, for all
$\epsilon>0$, where $C_\epsilon>0$ is some constant?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (kвӮҒ kвӮӮ nвӮҒ nвӮӮ : в„•),
  kвӮӮ вүӨ kвӮҒ вҶ’
    3 вүӨ kвӮӮ вҶ’
      nвӮҒ + kвӮҒ вүӨ nвӮӮ вҶ’
        (вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 kвӮҒ, (nвӮҒ + i)).primeFactors = (вҲҸ j вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 kвӮӮ, (nвӮӮ + j)).primeFactors вҶ’
          вҲғ p, Nat.Prime p вҲ§ nвӮҒ вүӨ p вҲ§ p вүӨ nвӮӮ
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s was unable to prove that if the two products have the same factors
then there must exist a prime between $n_1$ and $n_2$.

problems.statement:
вҲғ kвӮҒ kвӮӮ,
  вҲғ (_ : kвӮӮ вүӨ kвӮҒ) (_ : 3 вүӨ kвӮӮ),
    {(nвӮҒ, nвӮӮ) |
        nвӮҒ + kвӮҒ вүӨ nвӮӮ вҲ§
          nвӮӮ вүӨ 2 * (nвӮҒ + kвӮҒ) вҲ§
            (вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 kвӮҒ, (nвӮҒ + i)).primeFactors = (вҲҸ j вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 kвӮӮ, (nвӮӮ + j)).primeFactors}.Nonempty
problems.docstring: In fact there exist counterexamples, like this one found by AlphaProof.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (kвӮҒ kвӮӮ : в„•),
    kвӮӮ вүҘ 3 вҶ’
      kвӮӮ вүӨ kвӮҒ вҶ’
        {(nвӮҒ, nвӮӮ) |
            nвӮҒ + kвӮҒ вүӨ nвӮӮ вҲ§
              (вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 kвӮҒ, (nвӮҒ + i)).primeFactors = (вҲҸ j вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 kвӮӮ, (nвӮӮ + j)).primeFactors}.Finite
problems.docstring:
Let $k_1 \geq k_2 \geq 3$. Are there only finitely many $n_2\geq n_1 + k_1$
such that
$$
  \prod_{1\leq i\leq k_1}(n_1 + i)\ \text{and}\ \prod_{1\leq j\leq k_2} (n_2 + j)
$$
have the same prime factors?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (kвӮҒ kвӮӮ : в„•),
    kвӮӮ вүҘ 3 вҶ’
      kвӮӮ вүӨ kвӮҒ вҶ’
        {(nвӮҒ, nвӮӮ) |
            nвӮҒ + kвӮҒ вүӨ nвӮӮ вҲ§
              nвӮӮ вүӨ 2 * (nвӮҒ + kвӮҒ) вҲ§
                (вҲҸ i вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 kвӮҒ, (nвӮҒ + i)).primeFactors = (вҲҸ j вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 kвӮӮ, (nвӮӮ + j)).primeFactors}.Finite
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s thought perhaps if the two products have the same factors then
$n_2 > 2(n_1 + k_1)$.
It is an open question whether this is true when allowing a finite number of counterexamples.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
  Filter.Tendsto (fun n => вҶ‘(a n) ^ (1 / 2 ^ n)) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop вҶ’ Irrational (вҲ‘' (n : в„•), 1 / вҶ‘(a n))
problems.docstring:
A folklore result states that any $a_n$ satisfying $\lim_{n \to \infty} a_n^{\frac{1}{2^n}} = \infty$
has $\sum \frac{1}{a_n}$ converging to an irrational number.

problems.statement:
False вҶ”
  вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
    Erdos263.IsIrrationalitySequence a вҶ’ Filter.Tendsto (fun n => вҶ‘(a n) ^ (1 / вҶ‘n)) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Must every irrationality sequence $a_n$ in the above sense
satisfy $a_n^{1/n} \to \infty$ as $n \to \infty$? 
Answer: false.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
  StrictMono a вҶ’
    (Summable fun n => 1 / вҶ‘(a n)) вҶ’
      Filter.Tendsto (fun n => вҶ‘(a (n + 1)) / вҶ‘(a n) ^ 2) Filter.atTop (nhds 0) вҶ’ В¬Erdos263.IsIrrationalitySequence a
problems.docstring:
KovaДҚ and Tao [KoTa24] proved that any strictly increasing sequence $a_n$ such that
$\sum \frac{1}{a_n}$ converges and $\lim \frac{a_{n+1}}{a_n^2} = 0$ is not
an irrationality sequence in the above sense.

[KoTa24] KovaДҚ, V. and Tao T., On several irrationality problems for Ahmes series.
         arXiv:2406.17593 (2024).

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
  (вҲҖ (n : в„•), a n > 0) вҶ’
    StrictMono a вҶ’
      (вҲғ Оө > 0, Filter.liminf (fun n => вҶ‘(a (n + 1)) / вҶ‘(a n) ^ (2 + Оө)) Filter.atTop > 0) вҶ’
        Erdos263.IsIrrationalitySequence a
problems.docstring:
On the other hand, if there exists some $\varepsilon > 0$ such that $a_n$ satisfies
$\liminf \frac{a_{n+1}}{a_n^{2+\varepsilon}} > 0$, then $a_n$ is an irrationality sequence
by the above folklore result `erdos_263.variants.folklore`.

problems.statement: вҲҖб¶  (Оұ : в„қ) in Filter.cocountable, Оұ > 1 вҶ’ Erdos263.IsIrrationalitySequence fun n => вҢҠОұ ^ 2 ^ nвҢӢвӮҠ
problems.docstring:
Koizumi [Ko25] showed that $a_n = \lfloor \alpha^{2^n} \rfloor$ is an irrationality sequence
for all but countably many $\alpha > 1$.

[Ko25] Koizumi, J., Irrationality of the reciprocal sum of doubly exponential sequences,
       arXiv:2504.05933 (2025).

problems.statement: True вҶ” Erdos263.IsIrrationalitySequence fun n => 2 ^ 2 ^ n
problems.docstring: Is $a_n = 2^{2^n}$ an irrationality sequence in the above sense?

problems.statement: Filter.Tendsto Erdos36.MinOverlapQuotient Filter.atTop (nhds sorry)
problems.docstring: Find the value of the limit of `MinOverlapQuotient`!

problems.statement: 1 - (вҲҡ2)вҒ»В№ < Filter.liminf Erdos36.MinOverlapQuotient Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
A lower bound of $1 - frac{1}{\sqrt 2}$.
Scherk (written communication), see
[On the minimal overlap problem of ErdГ¶s](https://eudml.org/doc/206397)
by *Leo Moser*, РҗСҒСӮР° Рҗrithmetica V, p. 117-119, 1959

problems.statement: Erdos36.M 4 = 2
0.379005 < Filter.liminf Erdos36.MinOverlapQuotient Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
A lower bound of $0.379005$.
See [ErdЕ‘s' minimum overlap problem](https://arxiv.org/abs/2201.05704)
by *Ethan Patrick White*, 2022

problems.statement: Filter.limsup Erdos36.MinOverlapQuotient Filter.atTop вүӨ 1 / 2
problems.docstring:
The example (with $N$ even), $A = \{\frac N 2 + 1, \dots, \frac{3N}{2}\}$
shows an upper bound of $\frac 1 2$.

problems.statement: Erdos36.M 1 = 1
problems.docstring:
This example calculates the value of $M 1$. The set is $\{1, 2\}$, so the only partition is
$A = \{1\}, B = \{2\}$ (or vice versa). The possible differences are $1 - 2 = -1$ and $2 - 1 = 1$.
The `Overlap` for $k=-1$ is 1 (if $A=\{1\}, B=\{2\}$) and for $k=1$ also 1 (if $A=\{2\}, B=\{1\}$ ).
The `MaxOverlap` is $1$, since the `Overlap` is $0$ for other $k$.
Thus, $M 1 = 1$.

problems.statement: Filter.limsup Erdos36.MinOverlapQuotient Filter.atTop вүӨ 0.38200298812318988
problems.docstring:
An upper bound of $0.38200298812318988$.
See [Advances in the Minimum Overlap Problem](https://doi.org/10.1006%2Fjnth.1996.0064)
by *Jan Kristian Haugland*, Journal of Number Theory Volume 58, Issue 1, p 71-78, 1996

problems.statement: (4 - 15 ^ (1 / 2)) ^ (1 / 2) < Filter.liminf Erdos36.MinOverlapQuotient Filter.atTop
problems.docstring: A lower bound of $\sqrt{4 - \sqrt{15}}$.

problems.statement: Filter.limsup Erdos36.MinOverlapQuotient Filter.atTop вүӨ 2 / 5
problems.docstring:
An upper bound of $\frac 2 5$.
See [Minimal overlapping under translation.](https://projecteuclid.org/journals/bulletin-of-the-american-mathematical-society/volume-62/issue-6)
by *T. S. Motzkin*, *K. E. Ralston* and *J. L. Selfridge*,
in "The summer meeting in Seattle" by *V. L. Klee Jr.*, Bull. Amer. Math. Soc.62, p. 558, 1956

problems.statement: Filter.limsup Erdos36.MinOverlapQuotient Filter.atTop вүӨ 0.3809268534330870
problems.docstring:
An upper bound of $0.3809268534330870$.
See [The minimum overlap problem](https://www.neutreeko.net/mop/index.htm)
by *Jan Kristian Haugland*

problems.statement: Erdos36.M 5 = 3
1 / 4 < Filter.liminf Erdos36.MinOverlapQuotient Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
A lower bound of $\frac 1 4$.
See [Some remarks on number theory (in Hebrew)](https://users.renyi.hu/~p_erdos/1955-13.pdf)
by *Paul ErdЕ‘s*, Riveon Lematematika 9, p.45-48,1955

problems.statement: вҲғ c, Filter.Tendsto Erdos36.MinOverlapQuotient Filter.atTop (nhds c) вҲ§ c < 0.385694
problems.docstring: The limit of `MinOverlapQuotient` exists and it is less than $0.385694$.

problems.statement: вҲғ c, 0.379005 < c вҲ§ c вүӨ Filter.liminf Erdos36.MinOverlapQuotient Filter.atTop вҲ§ c = sorry
problems.docstring: Find a better lower bound!

problems.statement: (4 - 6 ^ (1 / 2)) / 5 < Filter.liminf Erdos36.MinOverlapQuotient Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
A lower bound of $\frac{4 - \sqrt{6}}{5}.
See [On the intersection of a linear set with the translation of its complement](https://bibliotekanauki.pl/articles/969027)
by *StanisЕӮaw Еҡwierczkowski1*, Colloquium Mathematicum 5(2), p. 185-197, 1958

problems.statement: вҲғ c < 0.380926853433087, Filter.limsup Erdos36.MinOverlapQuotient Filter.atTop вүӨ c вҲ§ c = sorry
problems.docstring: Find a better upper bound!

problems.statement: Erdos36.M 3 = 2
Erdos36.M 2 = 1
True вҶ” вҲҖ n > 0, вҲҖ M вҲҲ doublyStochastic в„қ (Fin n), вҲғ Пғ, (вҲҖ (i : Fin n), M i (Пғ i) вү  0) вҲ§ 1 вүӨ вҲ‘ i, M i (Пғ i)
problems.docstring:
A weaker version of ErdЕ‘s' problem 499, which asks whether for every doubly stochastic matrix, there
exists a permutation $Пғ \in S_n$ with $M_{i, Пғ(i)} вү  0$ and such that
$$
\sum_{1 \leq i \leq n} M_{i, Пғ(i)} \geq 1
$$
Proved by Marcus and Ree [MaRe59].

[MaRe59] Marcus, M. and Ree, R., Diagonals of doubly stochastic matrices. Quart. J. Math. Oxford Ser. (2) (1959), 296-302.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (n : в„•), вҲҖ M вҲҲ doublyStochastic в„қ (Fin n), вҲғ Пғ, вҶ‘n ^ (-вҶ‘n) вүӨ вҲҸ i, M i (Пғ i)
problems.docstring:
Let $M$ be a real $n \times n$ doubly stochastic matrix. Does there exist some $Пғ \in S_n$ such that
$$
\prod_{1 \leq i \leq n} M_{i, Пғ(i)} \geq n^{-n}?
$$
This is true, and was proved by Marcus and Minc [MaMi62]

[MaMi62] Marcus, Marvin and Minc, Henryk, Some results on doubly stochastic matrices. Proc. Amer. Math. Soc. (1962), 571-579.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), вҲҖ M вҲҲ doublyStochastic в„қ (Fin n), вҶ‘n ^ (-вҶ‘n) * вҶ‘n.factorial вүӨ M.permanent
problems.docstring:
The conjecture of van der Waerden, which states that the permanent of a doubly stochastic matrix is
at least $n^{-n} n!$.

Proved by Gyires [Gy80], Egorychev [Eg81], and Falikman [Fa81].

[Gy80] Gyires, B., The common source of several inequalities concerning doubly stochastic matrices. Publ. Math. Debrecen (1980), 291-304.
[Eg81] Egorychev, G. P., The solution of the van der Waerden problem for permanents. Dokl. Akad. Nauk SSSR (1981), 1041-1044.
[Fa81] Falikman, D. I., Proof of the van der Waerden conjecture on the permanent of a doubly stochastic matrix. Mat. Zametki (1981), 931-938, 957.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), вҲҖ Оө > 0, Erdos14.almostSquareRoot Оө =O[Filter.atTop] Erdos14.nonUniqueSumCount A
problems.docstring:
Let $A вҠҶ \mathbb{N}$. Let $B вҠҶ \mathbb{N}$ be the set of integers which are representable
in exactly one way as the sum of two elements from $A$. Is it true that for all
$\epsilon > 0$ and large $N$, $|\{1,\ldots,N\} \setminus B| \gg_\epsilon N^{1/2 - \epsilon}$?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ A, Erdos14.nonUniqueSumCount A =o[Filter.atTop] Erdos14.squareRoot
problems.docstring: Is it possible that $|\{1,\ldots,N\} \setminus B| = o(N^\frac{1}{2})$?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (V : Type) (G : SimpleGraph V),
    G.chromaticCardinal = Cardinal.aleph 1 вҶ’ вҲғ s, s.Countable вҲ§ (SimpleGraph.induce s G).InfinitelyConnected
problems.docstring:
Does every graph with chromatic number $\aleph_1$ contain a countable subgraph which is
infinitely connected?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (n : в„•) (color : в„• вҶ’ Fin n) (m : в„•),
    вҲғ A, A.card вүҘ m вҲ§ вҲғ c, вҲҖ (S : Finset вҶҘA), S.Nonempty вҶ’ color (вҲ‘ x вҲҲ S, вҶ‘x) = c вҲ§ color (вҲҸ x вҲҲ S, вҶ‘x) = c
problems.docstring:
Is it true that in any finite colouring of $\mathbb{N}$ there exist arbitrarily large finite $A$ such that all sums
and products of distinct elements in $A$ are the same colour?

problems.statement: 1117175146 вҲү Erdos10.sumPrimeAndTwoPows 3
problems.docstring:
Bogdan Grechuk has observed that `1117175146` is not the sum of a prime
and at most $3$ powers of $2$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ k, Erdos10.sumPrimeAndTwoPows k = Set.univ \ {0, 1}
problems.docstring:
Is there some $k$ such that every integer is the sum of a prime and at most $k$
powers of $2$?

problems.statement: ({n | Even n} \ Erdos10.sumPrimeAndTwoPows 2).Infinite
problems.docstring: There are infinitely many even integers not the sum of a prime and $2$ powers of $2$

problems.statement: вҲҖ (Оө : в„қ), 0 < Оө вҶ’ вҲғ k, 1 - Оө вүӨ (Erdos10.sumPrimeAndTwoPows k).lowerDensity
problems.docstring:
Gallagher [Ga75] has shown that for any $Пө > 0$ there exists $k(Пө)$
such that the set of integers which are the sum of a prime and at most $k(Пө)$
many powers of $2$ has lower density at least $1 - Пө$.

Ref: Gallagher, P. X., _Primes and powers of 2_.

problems.statement: {n | Odd n вҲ§ 1 < n} вҠҶ Erdos10.sumPrimeAndTwoPows 3 вҲ§ {n | Even n вҲ§ n вү  0} вҠҶ Erdos10.sumPrimeAndTwoPows 4
problems.docstring:
Granville and Soundararajan [GrSo98] have conjectured that at most $3$
powers of $2$ suffice for all odd integers, and hence at most $4$ powers of $2$
suffice for all even integers.

Ref: Granville, A. and Soundararajan, K., _A Binary Additive Problem of ErdЕ‘s and the Order of $2$ mod $p^2$_

problems.statement: ({n | Even n} \ Erdos10.sumPrimeAndTwoPows 3).Infinite
problems.docstring:
Bogdan Grechuk has observed that $1117175146$ is not the sum of a prime and at most $3$
powers of $2$, and pointed out that parity considerations, coupled with the fact that there
are many integers not the sum of a prime and $2$ powers of $2$ suggest that there exist
infinitely many even integers which are not the sum of a prime and at most $3$ powers of $2$).

problems.statement: вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), (A + A)б¶ң.Finite вҶ’ Filter.limsup (fun n => вҶ‘(AdditiveCombinatorics.sumRep A n)) Filter.atTop = вҠӨ
problems.docstring:
If $A вҠҶ \mathbb{N}$ is such that $A + A$ contains all but finitely many integers then
$\limsup 1_A вҲ— 1_A(n) = \infty$.

problems.statement:
False вҶ”
  вҲҖ (A : Finset в„•),
    0 вҲү A вҶ’
      1 вҲү A вҶ’ вҲ‘ n вҲҲ A, 1 / вҶ‘n < 2 вҶ’ вҲғ AвӮҒ AвӮӮ, Disjoint AвӮҒ AвӮӮ вҲ§ A = AвӮҒ вҲӘ AвӮӮ вҲ§ вҲ‘ n вҲҲ AвӮҒ, 1 / вҶ‘n < 1 вҲ§ вҲ‘ n вҲҲ AвӮӮ, 1 / вҶ‘n < 1
problems.docstring:
Is it true that if $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}\setminus\{1\}$ is a finite set with
$\sum_{n \in A} \frac{1}{n} < 2$ then there is a partition $A=A_1 \sqcup A_2$
such that $\sum_{n \in A_i} \frac{1}{n} < 1$ for $i=1,2$?

This is not true in general, as shown by SГЎndor [Sa97].

The minimal counterexample is $\{2,3,4,5,6,7,10,11,13,14,15\}$, found by Tom Stobart.

This was formalized in Lean by Mehta.

problems.statement:
вҲғ A,
  0 вҲү A вҲ§
    1 вҲү A вҲ§
      (Multiset.map (fun x => 1 / x) do
              let a вҶҗ A
              pure вҶ‘a).sum <
          2 вҲ§
        вҲҖ (AвӮҒ AвӮӮ : Multiset в„•),
          A = AвӮҒ + AвӮӮ вҶ’
            1 вүӨ
                (Multiset.map (fun x => 1 / x) do
                    let a вҶҗ AвӮҒ
                    pure вҶ‘a).sum вҲЁ
              1 вүӨ
                (Multiset.map (fun x => 1 / x) do
                    let a вҶҗ AвӮӮ
                    pure вҶ‘a).sum
problems.docstring: This is not true if $A$ is a multiset, for example $2,3,3,5,5,5,5$.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (n : в„•),
  2 вүӨ n вҶ’
    вҲғ A,
      A.Nonempty вҲ§
        0 вҲү A вҲ§
          1 вҲү A вҲ§ вҲ‘ k вҲҲ A, 1 / вҶ‘k < вҶ‘n вҲ§ вҲҖ (P : Finpartition A), P.parts.card = n вҶ’ вҲғ p вҲҲ P.parts, 1 вүӨ вҲ‘ n вҲҲ p, 1 / вҶ‘n
problems.docstring:
This is not true in general, as shown by SГЎndor [Sa97], who observed that the proper divisors of
$120$ form a counterexample. More generally, SГЎndor shows that for any $n\geq 2$ there exists a
finite set $A\subseteq \mathbb{N}\backslash\{1\}$ with $\sum_{k\in A}\frac{1}{k} < n$ and no
partition into $n$ parts each of which has $\sum_{k\in A_i}\frac{1}{k}<1$.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ {f : Polynomial в„Ө},
    Irreducible f вҶ’
      f.natDegree > 3 вҶ’
        (В¬вҲғ l, f.natDegree = 2 ^ l) вҶ’
          0 < f.leadingCoeff вҶ’
            (В¬вҲғ p, Nat.Prime p вҲ§ вҲҖ (n : в„•), вҶ‘p ^ (f.natDegree - 1) вҲЈ Polynomial.eval (вҶ‘n) f) вҶ’
              {n | Powerfree (f.natDegree - 2) (Polynomial.eval (вҶ‘n) f)}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
If $k > 3$ (and $k \neq 2^l$), then are there infinitely many $n$ for which $f(n)$ is
$(k-2)$-power-free?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {f : Polynomial в„Ө},
  Irreducible f вҶ’
    9 вүӨ f.natDegree вҶ’
      (вҲҖ (p : в„•), Nat.Prime p вҶ’ вҲғ n, В¬вҶ‘p ^ (f.natDegree - 1) вҲЈ Polynomial.eval (вҶ‘n) f) вҶ’
        {n | Powerfree (f.natDegree - 2) (Polynomial.eval (вҶ‘n) f)}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
If the degree `k` of `f` is larger than or equal to `9`, then the set of `n` such that `f n` is
`(k - 2)`-th power free has infinitely many elements. This result is proved in [Br11].

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {f : Polynomial в„Ө},
  Irreducible f вҶ’
    2 < f.natDegree вҶ’
      (вҲҖ (x : в„•), f.natDegree вү  2 ^ x) вҶ’
        0 < f.leadingCoeff вҶ’ {n | Powerfree (f.natDegree - 1) (Polynomial.eval (вҶ‘n) f)}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
Let `f вҲҲ в„Ө[X]` be an irreducible polynomial with positive leading coefficient. Suppose that the
degree `k` of `f` is larger than `2` and is not equal to a power of `2`. Then the set of `n` such
that `f n` is `(k - 1)`-th power free is infinite, and this is proved in [Er53].

problems.statement: True вҶ” {n | Squarefree (n ^ 4 + 2)}.Infinite
problems.docstring: Does `n ^ 4 + 2` represent infinitely many squarefree numbers?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {f : Polynomial в„Ө},
  Irreducible f вҶ’
    2 < f.natDegree вҶ’
      (вҲҖ (x : в„•), f.natDegree вү  2 ^ x) вҶ’
        0 < f.leadingCoeff вҶ’
          (вҲҖ (p : в„•), Nat.Prime p вҶ’ вҲғ n, В¬вҶ‘p ^ (f.natDegree - 1) вҲЈ Polynomial.eval (вҶ‘n) f) вҶ’
            {n | Powerfree (f.natDegree - 1) (Polynomial.eval (вҶ‘n) f)}.HasPosDensity
problems.docstring:
Let `f вҲҲ в„Ө[X]` be an irreducible polynomial with positive leading coefficient. Suppose that the
degree `k` of `f` is larger than `2`, is not equal to a power of `2`, and `f n` has no fixed
`(k - 1)`-th power divisors other than `1`. Then the set of `n` such that `f n` is `(k - 1)`-th
power free has positive density, and this is proved in [Ho67].

problems.statement: вҲғ a, StrictMono a вҲ§ a 0 вүҘ 1 вҲ§ вҲҖ (t : в„ҡ), (В¬вҲғ n, t = -вҶ‘(a n)) вҶ’ вҲғ q, HasSum (fun n => 1 / (вҶ‘(a n) + вҶ‘t)) вҶ‘q
problems.docstring:
In fact, KovaДҚ and Tao proved in [KoTa24] that there exists a strictly increasing
sequence $a_n$ of positive integers such that $\sum \frac{1}{a_n + t}$ converges to a rational
number for all $t \in \mathbb{Q}$ such that $t \ne -a_n$ for any $n$.

[KoTa24] KovaДҚ, V. and Tao T., On several irrationality problems for Ahmes series.
         [arXiv:2406.17593](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.17593) (2024).

problems.statement:
В¬вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
    ((вҲҖ (n : в„•), a n вүҘ 1) вҲ§ Summable fun x => 1 / вҶ‘(a x)) вҶ’ вҲғ t вүҘ 1, Irrational (вҲ‘' (n : в„•), 1 / (вҶ‘(a n) + вҶ‘t))
problems.docstring:
Let $a_n$ be an infinite sequence of positive integers such that $\sum \frac{1}{a_n}$ converges.
There exists some integer $t \ge 1$ such that $\sum \frac{1}{a_n + t}$ is irrational.

This was disproven by KovaДҚ and Tao in [KoTa24].

[KoTa24] KovaДҚ, V. and Tao T., On several irrationality problems for Ahmes series.
         [arXiv:2406.17593](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.17593) (2024).

problems.statement:
False вҶ”
  вҲҖ (A : Set (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))),
    вҲҖ Оө > 0, A.Infinite вҶ’ Erdos846.NonTrilinearFor A Оө вҶ’ Erdos846.WeaklyNonTrilinear A
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘s Problem 846**
Let `A вҠӮ в„қВІ` be an infinite set for which there exists some `Пө>0` such that in any subset of `A`
of size `n` there are always at least `Пөn` with no three on a line.
Is it true that `A` is the union of a finite number of sets where no three are on a line?

In other words, prove or disprove the following statement: every infinite `Оө`-non-trilinear subset of the
plane is weakly non-trilinar.

problems.statement: {p | Nat.Prime p вҲ§ (orderOf 2 = p - 1 вҲЁ orderOf 3 = p - 1 вҲЁ orderOf 5 = p - 1)}.Infinite
problems.docstring: Heath-Brown proved that at least one of 2, 3, or 5 is a primitive root for infinitely many primes $p$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (p : в„•), Nat.Prime p вҶ’ p вү  2 вҶ’ вҲғ q, Nat.Prime q вҲ§ q < p вҲ§ orderOf вҶ‘q = p - 1
problems.docstring: Is it true that, for every prime $p$, there is a prime $q \leq p$ which is a primitive root modulo $p$?

problems.statement: False вҶ” вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), Infinite вҶ‘A вҶ’ Erdos847.HasFew3APs A вҶ’ вҲғ n S, (вҲҖ (i : Fin n), ThreeAPFree (S i)) вҲ§ A = вӢғ i, S i
problems.docstring:
Let $A \subset \mathbb{N}$ be an infinite set for which there exists some $\epsilon > 0$ such that
in any subset of $A$ of size $n$ there is a subset of size at least $\epsilon n$ which contains no
three-term arithmetic progression.

Is it true that $A$ is the union of a finite number of sets which contain no three-term arithmetic
progression?

A negative answer was given by Reiher, RГ¶dl, and Sales [RRS24], who proved that, for any
$0<\mu<1/2$, there exists $A\subseteq \mathbb{N}$ such that every finite colouring of $A$ contains
a three-term arithmetic progression, and yet every subset of $A$ of size $n$ contains a subset of
size $\geq \mu n$ without a three-term arithmetic progression.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ C > 0, вҲҖ (A : Finset в„Ө), вҶ‘(Erdos1097.CommonDifferencesThreeTermAP A).ncard вүӨ C * вҶ‘A.card ^ (3 / 2)
problems.docstring:
The main conjecture: for any finite set of integers $A$ with $|A| = n$, the number of distinct
common differences in three-term arithmetic progressions is $O(n^{3/2})$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (A : Finset в„Ө), (Erdos1097.CommonDifferencesThreeTermAP A).ncard вүӨ A.card ^ 2
problems.docstring: A weaker bound has been proven: there are always at most $n^2$ such values of $d$.

problems.statement: вҲғ c > 0, вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҲғ A, A.card = n вҲ§ c * вҶ‘n вүӨ вҶ‘(Erdos1097.CommonDifferencesThreeTermAP A).ncard
problems.docstring:
A trivial lower bound: for sufficiently large `n` there exist sets $A$ with $|A| = n$ that contain at least $\Omega(n)$
distinct common differences of three-term arithmetic progressions.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖб¶  (k : в„•) in Filter.atTop, {(m, n) | n + k вүӨ m вҲ§ Finset.lcmInterval m (k + 1) < Finset.lcmInterval n k}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
Write $M(n, k)$ be the least common multiple of $\{n+1, \dotsc, n+k\}$.
Let $k$ be sufficiently large. Are there infinitely many $m, n$ with $m \geq n + k$ such that
$$
M(n, k) > M(m, k + 1)
$$?
The answer is yes, as proved in a strong form by Cambie [Ca24].
[Ca24] S. Cambie, Resolution of an ErdЕ‘s' problem on least common multiples. arXiv:2410.09138 (2024).

This was formalized in Lean by Alexeev using Aristotle.

problems.statement: Finset.lcmInterval 104 8 < Finset.lcmInterval 96 7
problems.docstring:
The referee of [Er79] found the example $M(96, 7) > M(104, 8)$, showing that there are cases where
$M(n, k) > M(m, k + 1)$ with $m \geq n + k$.
[Er79] ErdЕ‘s, Paul, Some unconventional problems in number theory. Math. Mag. (1979), 67-70.

problems.statement: Finset.lcmInterval 139 8 < Finset.lcmInterval 132 7
problems.docstring:
The referee of [Er79] found the example $M(132, 7) > M(139, 8)$, showing that there are cases where
$M(n, k) > M(m, k + 1)$ with $m \geq n + k$.
[Er79] ErdЕ‘s, Paul, Some unconventional problems in number theory. Math. Mag. (1979), 67-70.

problems.statement: Finset.lcmInterval 62 8 < Finset.lcmInterval 52 7
problems.docstring:
Cambie [Ca24] found the example $M(52, 7) > M(62, 8)$.
[Ca24] S. Cambie, Resolution of an ErdЕ‘s' problem on least common multiples. arXiv:2410.09138 (2024).

problems.statement: Finset.lcmInterval 47 9 < Finset.lcmInterval 36 8
problems.docstring:
Cambie [Ca24] found the example $M(36, 8) > M(48, 9)$.
[Ca24] S. Cambie, Resolution of an ErdЕ‘s' problem on least common multiples. arXiv:2410.09138 (2024).

problems.statement:
вҲғ o,
  вҲғ (_ : o =o[Filter.atTop] 1),
    вҲҖ (N : в„•) (A : Finset в„•), Erdos1.IsSumDistinctSet A N вҶ’ (вҲҡ(2 / Real.pi) - o A.card) * 2 ^ A.card / вҲҡвҶ‘A.card вүӨ вҶ‘N
problems.docstring:
A number of improvements of the constant $\frac{1}{4}$ have been given, with the current
record $\sqrt{2 / \pi}$ first provied in unpublished work of Elkies and Gleason.

problems.statement: IsLeast {N | вҲғ A, Erdos1.IsSumDistinctSet A N вҲ§ A.card = 9} 161
problems.docstring:
The minimal value of $N$ such that there exists a sum-distinct set with nine
elements is $161$.

https://oeis.org/A276661

problems.statement:
вҲғ o,
  вҲғ (_ : o =o[Filter.atTop] 1),
    вҲҖ (N : в„•) (A : Finset в„•), Erdos1.IsSumDistinctSet A N вҶ’ (1 / 4 - o A.card) * 2 ^ A.card / вҲҡвҶ‘A.card вүӨ вҶ‘N
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and Moser [Er56] proved
$$
  N \geq (\tfrac{1}{4} - o(1)) \frac{2^n}{\sqrt{n}}.
$$

[Er56] ErdЕ‘s, P., _Problems and results in additive number theory_. Colloque sur la Th\'{E}orie des Nombres, Bruxelles, 1955 (1956), 127-137.

problems.statement: IsLeast {N | вҲғ A, Erdos1.IsSumDistinctSet A N вҲ§ A.card = 5} 13
problems.docstring:
The minimal value of $N$ such that there exists a sum-distinct set with five
elements is $13$.

https://oeis.org/A276661

problems.statement: вҲғ C > 0, вҲҖ (N : в„•) (A : Finset в„•), Erdos1.IsSumDistinctSet A N вҶ’ N вү  0 вҶ’ C * 2 ^ A.card / вҶ‘A.card < вҶ‘N
problems.docstring: The trivial lower bound is $N \gg 2^n / n$.

problems.statement: вҲғ C > 0, вҲҖ (N : в„•) (A : Finset в„қ), Erdos1.IsSumDistinctRealSet A N вҶ’ N вү  0 вҶ’ C * 2 ^ A.card < вҶ‘N
problems.docstring:
A generalisation of the problem to sets $A \subseteq (0, N]$ of real numbers, such that the subset
sums all differ by at least $1$ is proposed in [Er73] and [ErGr80].

[Er73] ErdЕ‘s, P., _Problems and results on combinatorial number theory_. A survey of combinatorial theory (Proc. Internat. Sympos., Colorado State Univ., Fort Collins, Colo., 1971) (1973), 117-138.

[ErGr80] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Graham, R., _Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number theory_. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).

problems.statement: IsLeast {N | вҲғ A, Erdos1.IsSumDistinctSet A N вҲ§ A.card = 3} 4
problems.docstring:
The minimal value of $N$ such that there exists a sum-distinct set with three
elements is $4$.

https://oeis.org/A276661

problems.statement: вҲғ C > 0, вҲҖ (N : в„•) (A : Finset в„•), Erdos1.IsSumDistinctSet A N вҶ’ N вү  0 вҶ’ C * 2 ^ A.card < вҶ‘N
problems.docstring:
If $A\subseteq\{1, ..., N\}$ with $|A| = n$ is such that the subset sums $\sum_{a\in S}a$ are
distinct for all $S\subseteq A$ then
$$
  N \gg 2 ^ n.
$$

problems.statement: True вҶ” Filter.Tendsto (fun N => вҶ‘(Erdos295.k N) - (Real.exp 1 - 1) * вҶ‘N) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Let $k(N)$ denote the smallest $k$ such that there exists
$N вүӨ n_1 < вӢҜ < n_k$ with $\frac 1 {n_1} + ... + \frac 1 {n_k} = 1$

Is it true that $\lim_{N \to \infty} k(N) - (e - 1)N = \infty$?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (N : в„•), вҲғ k n, (вҲҖ (i : Fin k.succ), N вүӨ n i) вҲ§ StrictMono n вҲ§ вҲ‘ i, 1 / вҶ‘(n i) = 1
problems.docstring:
Helper lemma: for each $N$, there exists $k$ and $n_1 < ... < n_k$ such that
$N вүӨ n_1 < вӢҜ < n_k$ with $\frac 1 {n_1} + ... + \frac 1 {n_k} = 1$.

problems.statement:
вҲғ C > 0,
  вҲғ O > 0, вҲҖб¶  (N : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҶ‘(Erdos295.k N) - (Real.exp 1 - 1) * вҶ‘N вҲҲ Set.Ioc (-C) (O * вҶ‘N / Real.log вҶ‘N)
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and Straus have proved the existence of some constant $c>0$
such that $-c < k(N)-(e-1)N \ll \frac N {\log N}$

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), Odd n вҶ’ 1 < n вҶ’ вҲғ k l, Squarefree k вҲ§ n = k + 2 ^ l
problems.docstring: Is every odd $n > 1$ the sum of a squarefree number and a power of 2?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), В¬4 вҲЈ n вҶ’ 1 < n вҶ’ вҲғ k l, Squarefree k вҲ§ n = k + 2 ^ l
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s often asked this under the weaker assumption that $n > 1$
is not divisible by 4.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), Odd n вҶ’ n < 10 ^ 7 вҶ’ 1 < n вҶ’ вҲғ k l, Squarefree k вҲ§ n = k + 2 ^ l
problems.docstring: Every odd $1 < n < 10^7$ is the sum of a squarefree number and a power of 2.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), Odd n вҶ’ n < 2 ^ 50 вҶ’ 1 < n вҶ’ вҲғ k l, Squarefree k вҲ§ n = k + 2 ^ l
problems.docstring: Every odd $1 < n < 2^50$ is the sum of a squarefree number and a power of 2.

problems.statement: (вҲҖ (n : в„•), Odd n вҶ’ 1 < n вҶ’ вҲғ k l, Squarefree k вҲ§ n = k + 2 ^ l) вҶ’ {p | Nat.Prime p вҲ§ 2 ^ p вүЎ 2 [MOD p ^ 2]}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
Suppose that every odd $n$ is the sum of a squarefree number and a power of 2. Then the set of primes
$p$ such that $2 ^ p вүЎ 2 \mod p ^ 2$ is infinite. This is Theorem 1 in [GrSo98].
[GrSo98] Granville, A. and Soundararajan, K., A Binary Additive Problem of ErdЕ‘s and the Order of $2$ mod $p^2$. The Ramanujan Journal (1998), 283-298.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), Odd n вҶ’ 1 < n вҶ’ вҲғ k l m, Squarefree k вҲ§ n = k + 2 ^ l + 2 ^ m
problems.docstring: Is every odd $n > 1$ the sum of a squarefree number and two powers of 2?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ в„Ө), StrictMono a вҶ’ (вҲғ C > 0, вҲҖ (n : в„•), вҶ‘(a (n + 1)) вүҘ C * вҶ‘(a n) ^ 2) вҶ’ Irrational (Erdos1051.ErdosSeries a)
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s [Er88c] notes that if the sequence grows rapidly to infinity (specifically, if
$a_{n+1} \geq C \cdot a_n^2$ for some constant $C > 0$), then the series is irrational.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ в„Ө), StrictMono a вҶ’ Erdos1051.GrowthCondition a вҶ’ Irrational (Erdos1051.ErdosSeries a)
problems.docstring:
Is it true that if $a_0 < a_1 < a_2 < \cdots$ is a strictly increasing sequence
of integers with $\liminf a_n^{1/2^n} > 1$, then the series
$\sum_{n=0}^\infty \frac{1}{a_n \cdot a_{n+1}}$ is irrational?

This was solved in the affirmative by Aletheia [Fe26]. This was extended by Barreto, Kang, Kim,
KovaДҚ, and Zhang [BKKKZ26], who essentially give a complete answer: if $\phi=\frac{1+\sqrt{5}}{2}$
is the golden ratio and $1\leq a_1 < a_2 < \cdots$ is a monotonically increasing sequence of
integers such that $\limsup a_n^{1/\phi^{n}}=\infty$ then $\sum_{n=1}^\infty \frac{1}{a_na_{n+1}}$
is irrational. Conversely, for any $1 < C < \infty$ there exists a sequence of integers
$1\leq a_1<\cdots$ such that $\lim a_n^{1/\phi^{n}}=C$ where this infinite sum is a rational number.

(Further, more general, results are available in [BKKKZ26].)

This was formalized in Lean by Baretto.

problems.statement: True вҶ” (fun n => вҶ‘n ^ 2) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos152.f n)
problems.docstring: Must `f n вү« n ^ 2`?

problems.statement: True вҶ” Filter.Tendsto Erdos152.f Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
problems.docstring: Must `lim f n = вҲһ`?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (P : Finset в„•), (вҲҖ p вҲҲ P, Nat.Prime p) вҶ’ P.card вүҘ 2 вҶ’ Irrational (Erdos269.series вҶ‘P)
problems.docstring:
Let $P$ be a finite set of primes with $|P| \ge 2$ and let
$\{a_1 < a_2 < \dots\}$ be the set of positive integers whose prime factors
are all in $P$. Is the sum
$$ \sum_{n=1}^\infty \frac{1}{[a_1,\ldots,a_n]} $$
irrational?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (P : Set в„•), (вҲҖ p вҲҲ P, Nat.Prime p) вҶ’ P.Infinite вҶ’ Irrational (Erdos269.series P)
problems.docstring:
This theorem addresses the case where the set of primes $P$ is infinite. In this case the sum is
irrational.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (P : Finset в„•), (вҲҖ p вҲҲ P, Nat.Prime p) вҶ’ P.card вүҘ 2 вҶ’ вҲғ q, вҶ‘q = Erdos269.series вҶ‘P
problems.docstring:
Let $P$ be a finite set of primes with $|P| \ge 2$ and let
$\{a_1 < a_2 < \dots\}$ be the set of positive integers whose prime factors
are all in $P$. Is the sum
$$ \sum_{n=1}^\infty \frac{1}{[a_1,\ldots,a_n]} $$
rational?

problems.statement: True вҶ” Erdos936.EventuallyNotPowerful fun x => x.factorial - 1
problems.docstring: Is $n! - 1$ powerful for finitely many $n$?

problems.statement: True вҶ” Erdos936.EventuallyNotPowerful fun x => 2 ^ x - 1
problems.docstring: Is $2^n - 1$ powerful for finitely many $n$?

problems.statement: True вҶ” Erdos936.EventuallyNotPowerful fun x => 2 ^ x + 1
problems.docstring: Is $2^n + 1$ powerful for finitely many $n$?

problems.statement: True вҶ” Erdos936.EventuallyNotPowerful fun x => x.factorial + 1
problems.docstring: Is $n! + 1$ powerful for finitely many $n$?

problems.statement: вҲғ cвӮҖ < 1, вҲҖ c > cвӮҖ, вҲҖб¶  (p : в„•) in Filter.atTop, Nat.Prime p вҶ’ вҲҖ (n : в„•), Erdos445.Erdos445Prop c p n
problems.docstring: Heilbronn (unpublished) proved this for $c$ sufficiently close to $1$.

problems.statement: Erdos445.Erdos445Prop 1 5 1
problems.docstring:
Small example: for $p=5$, $c=1$, $n=0$, the pair $(2,3) \in (0,5)$ satisfies
$2 \cdot 3 = 6 \equiv 1 \pmod{5}$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ c > 1 / 2, вҲҖб¶  (p : в„•) in Filter.atTop, Nat.Prime p вҶ’ вҲҖ (n : в„•), Erdos445.Erdos445Prop c p n
problems.docstring:
Is it true that, for any $c>1/2$, if $p$ is a sufficiently large prime then, for any
$n\geq 0$, there exist $a,b\in(n,n+p^c)$ such that $ab\equiv 1\pmod{p}$?

This is discussed in this MathOverflow question [MathOverflow].

problems.statement: вҲҖ c > 3 / 4, вҲҖб¶  (p : в„•) in Filter.atTop, Nat.Prime p вҶ’ вҲҖ (n : в„•), Erdos445.Erdos445Prop c p n
problems.docstring: Heath-Brown [He00] used Kloosterman sums to prove this for all $c>3/4$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {k : в„•}, 3 вүӨ k вҶ’ Erdos1063.n k вүӨ k * (Finset.Icc 1 (k - 1)).lcm id
problems.docstring:
[Cambie observed](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1063) the improved bound
$n_k \le k \cdot \operatorname{lcm}(1, \dotsc, k - 1)$.

problems.statement: Erdos1063.n 2 = 4 вҲ§ Erdos1063.n 3 = 6 вҲ§ Erdos1063.n 4 = 9 вҲ§ Erdos1063.n 5 = 12
problems.docstring: The initial values satisfy $n_2 = 4$, $n_3 = 6$, $n_4 = 9$, and $n_5 = 12$ ([Gu04], Problem B31).

problems.statement: вҲҖ {n k : в„•}, 2 вүӨ k вҶ’ 2 * k вүӨ n вҶ’ вҲғ i < k, В¬n - i вҲЈ n.choose k
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and Selfridge noted that, for $n \ge 2k$ with $k \ge 2$, at least one of the numbers
$n - i$ for $0 \le i < k$ fails to divide $\binom{n}{k}$ ([ErSe83]).

problems.statement: вҲҖ {k : в„•}, 3 вүӨ k вҶ’ Erdos1063.n k вүӨ k.factorial
problems.docstring:
Monier observed that $n_k \le k!$ for $k \ge 3$ ([Mo85]).
TODO: Find reference

problems.statement: вҲғ f, Filter.Tendsto f Filter.atTop (nhds 0) вҲ§ вҲҖ (k : в„•), вҶ‘(Erdos1063.n k) вүӨ Real.exp ((1 + f k) * вҶ‘k)
problems.docstring: The least common multiple bound implies $n_k \le \exp((1 + o(1))k)$.
Estimate $n_k$ by finding a better upper bound.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ {q : в„• вҶ’ в„•},
    StrictMono q вҶ’
      (вҲҖ (i : в„•), Nat.Prime (q i)) вҶ’
        (вҲҖ (i : в„•), q (i + 1) % q i = 1) вҶ’ Filter.Tendsto (fun k => вҶ‘(q k) ^ (1 / вҶ‘k)) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Let $q_1 < q_2 < \cdots$ be a sequence of primes such that $q_{i + 1} \equiv 1 \pmod{q_i}$. Is it
true that
$$
\lim_{k \to \infty} q_k^{1/k} = \infty?
$$

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ q,
    StrictMono q вҲ§
      (вҲҖ (i : в„•), Nat.Prime (q i)) вҲ§
        (вҲҖ (i : в„•), q (i + 1) % q i = 1) вҲ§
          вҲғ o, o =o[Filter.atTop] 1 вҲ§ вҲҖ (k : в„•), вҶ‘(q k) вүӨ Real.exp ((вҶ‘k + 1) * Real.log (вҶ‘k + 1) ^ (1 + o k))
problems.docstring:
Is there a sequence of primes $q_1 < q_2 < \cdots$ such that $q_{i + 1} \equiv 1 \pmod{q_i}$ and
$$
q(k) \leq \exp(k (\log k)^{1 + o(1)})?
$$

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {Оұ : в„қ},
  Оұ вҲҲ Set.Icc 0 2 вҶ’
    вҲғ ОІ,
      Filter.Tendsto (fun x => 1 / x * вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Erdos145.A x, (вҶ‘(Erdos145.s (n + 1)) - вҶ‘(Erdos145.s n)) ^ Оұ) Filter.atTop
        (nhds ОІ)
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s [Er51] proved this for all $0\leq \alpha\leq 2$.

[Er51] ErdГ¶s, P., Some problems and results in elementary number theory.
Publ. Math. Debrecen (1951), 103-109.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {Оұ : в„қ},
  Оұ вҲҲ Set.Icc 0 (11 / 3) вҶ’
    вҲғ ОІ,
      Filter.Tendsto (fun x => 1 / x * вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Erdos145.A x, (вҶ‘(Erdos145.s (n + 1)) - вҶ‘(Erdos145.s n)) ^ Оұ) Filter.atTop
        (nhds ОІ)
problems.docstring:
Greaves, Harman, and Huxley [GHH97] showed that this is true for $0 \leq \alpha\leq 11/3$.

[GHH97] Greaves, G. R. H. and Harman, G. and Huxley, M. N., Sieve Methods, Exponential Sums, and
their Applications in Number Theory. (1997).

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ Оұ вүҘ 0,
    вҲғ ОІ,
      Filter.Tendsto (fun x => 1 / x * вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Erdos145.A x, (вҶ‘(Erdos145.s (n + 1)) - вҶ‘(Erdos145.s n)) ^ Оұ) Filter.atTop
        (nhds ОІ)
problems.docstring:
Let $s_1 < s_2 < \cdots$ be the sequence of squarefree numbers. Is it true that, for any
$\alpha\geq 0$,
$$
\lim_{x\to\infty} \frac{1}{x}\sum_{s_n\leq x}(s_{n+1}-s_n)^\alpha
$$
exists?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {Оұ : в„қ},
  Оұ вҲҲ Set.Icc 0 3 вҶ’
    вҲғ ОІ,
      Filter.Tendsto (fun x => 1 / x * вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Erdos145.A x, (вҶ‘(Erdos145.s (n + 1)) - вҶ‘(Erdos145.s n)) ^ Оұ) Filter.atTop
        (nhds ОІ)
problems.docstring:
Hooley [Ho73] extended this to all $0 \leq \alpha\leq 3$.

[Ho73] Hooley, Christopher, On the intervals between consecutive terms of sequences. Proc. Symp. Pure Math, vol. 24, pp. 129-140. 1973.

problems.statement: (fun N => вҶ‘N * Real.log вҶ‘N ^ 2) =O[Filter.atTop] fun N => вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Finset.range N, вҶ‘(primeGap n) ^ 2
problems.docstring:
The prime number theorem immediately implies a lower bound of $\gg N(\log N)^2$ for the sum of
squares of gaps between consecutive primes.

problems.statement: (fun N => вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Finset.range N, вҶ‘(primeGap n) ^ 2) =O[Filter.atTop] fun N => вҶ‘N * Real.log вҶ‘N ^ 2
problems.docstring:
A conjecture by Heath-Brown:
The sum of squares of the first $N$ gaps between consecutive primes behaves like $N * (log N)^2$.

problems.statement: RiemannHypothesis вҶ’ (fun N => вҲ‘ n вҲҲ Finset.range N, вҶ‘(primeGap n) ^ 2) =O[Filter.atTop] fun N => вҶ‘N * Real.log вҶ‘N ^ 4
problems.docstring: CramГ©r proved an upper bound of $O(N(\log N)^4)$ conditional on the Riemann hypothesis.

problems.statement: Erdos123.IsDComplete (вҶ‘(Submonoid.powers 2) * вҶ‘(Submonoid.powers 3))
problems.docstring:
A simpler case: the set of numbers of the form $2^k 3^l$ ($k, l вүҘ 0$) is d-complete.

This was initially conjectured by ErdЕ‘s in 1992, who called it a "nice and difficult"
problem, but it was quickly proven by Jansen and others using a simple inductive argument:
- If $n = 2m$ is even, apply the inductive hypothesis to $m$ and double all summands.
- If $n$ is odd, let $3^k$ be the largest power of $3$ with $3^k вүӨ n$, and apply the
  inductive hypothesis to $n - 3^k$ (which is even).

Reference: [Er92b] ErdЕ‘s, Paul, _Some of my favourite problems in various branches
of combinatorics_. Matematiche (Catania) (1992), 231-240.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ a > 1,
    вҲҖ b > 1,
      вҲҖ c > 1,
        Erdos123.PairwiseCoprime a b c вҶ’
          Erdos123.IsDComplete (вҶ‘(Submonoid.powers a) * вҶ‘(Submonoid.powers b) * вҶ‘(Submonoid.powers c))
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘s Problem #123**

Let $a, b, c$ be three integers which are pairwise coprime. Is every large integer
the sum of distinct integers of the form $a^k b^l c^m$ ($k, l, m вүҘ 0$), none of which
divide any other?

Equivalently: is the set $\{a^k b^l c^m : k, l, m \geq 0\}$ d-complete?

Note: For this not to reduce to the two-integer case, we need the integers
to be greater than one and distinct.

problems.statement: Erdos123.IsDComplete (вҶ‘(Submonoid.powers 3) * вҶ‘(Submonoid.powers 5) * вҶ‘(Submonoid.powers 7))
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and Lewin proved this conjecture when $a = 3$, $b = 5$, and $c = 7$.

Reference: [ErLe96] ErdЕ‘s, P. and Lewin, Mordechai,
_$d$-complete sequences of integers_. Math. Comp. (1996), 837-840.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ Оө > 0,
    вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
      вҲғ A,
        вҶ‘A вҠҶ вҶ‘(Submonoid.powers 2) * вҶ‘(Submonoid.powers 3) * вҶ‘(Submonoid.powers 5) вҲ§
          Erdos123.IsSnug Оө A вҲ§ вҲ‘ x вҲҲ A, x = n
problems.docstring:
A stronger conjecture for numbers of the form $2^k 3^l 5^j$.

For any $Оө > 0$, all large integers $n$ can be written as the sum of distinct integers
$b_1 < ... < b_t$ of the form $2^k 3^l 5^j$ where $b_t < (1 + Пө) b_1$.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  {P |
      (вҶ‘P).IsAPOfLength 3 вҲ§
        вҲғ k, P = {Nat.nth Nat.Powerful k, Nat.nth Nat.Powerful (k + 1), Nat.nth Nat.Powerful (k + 2)}}.Finite
problems.docstring:
Let $A=\{n_1 < n_2 < \cdots\}$ be the sequence of powerful numbers (if $p\mid n$ then $p^2\mid n$).
Are there only finitely many three-term progressions of consecutive terms $n_k,n_{k+1},n_{k+2}$?

problems.statement: вҲғ A, A.HasPosDensity вҲ§ В¬Erdos318.PвӮҒ A
problems.docstring:
There exists a set `A` with positive density that does not have property `PвӮҒ`.
#TODO: prove this lemma by assuming `erdos_318.contain_single_even`.

problems.statement: Erdos318.PвӮҒ {n | Odd n}
problems.docstring: Sattler proved in [Sa75] that the set of odd numbers has property `PвӮҒ`.

problems.statement: В¬Erdos318.PвӮҒ {n | IsSquare n}
problems.docstring: The set of squares does not have property `PвӮҒ`.

problems.statement: True вҶ” Erdos318.PвӮҒ ({n | IsSquare n} \ {1})
problems.docstring: Does the set of squares excluding 1 have property `PвӮҒ`?

problems.statement: Erdos318.PвӮҒ Set.univ
problems.docstring: `в„•` has property `PвӮҒ`. This is proved in [ErSt75].

problems.statement: вҲҖ {A : Set в„•}, {n | n вҲҲ A вҲ§ Even n}.ncard = 1 вҶ’ В¬Erdos318.PвӮҒ {n | IsSquare n}
problems.docstring:
For any set `A` containing exactly one even number, `A` does not have property `PвӮҒ`. Sattler
[Sa82] credits this observation to ErdЕ‘s, who presumably found this after [ErGr80].

problems.statement: вҲҖ {A : Set в„•}, A.IsAPOfLength вҠӨ вҶ’ Erdos318.PвӮҒ A
problems.docstring: Every infinite arithmetic progression has property `PвӮҒ`. This is proved in [Sa82b].

problems.statement: вҲҖ k вүҘ 2, вҲҖ (n x l : в„•), 2 вүӨ l вҶ’ вҲҸ x вҲҲ Finset.Ioc n (n + k), x вү  x ^ l
problems.docstring:
Let $k\geq 2$. ErdЕ‘s and Selfridge [ES75] proved that the product of any $k$ consecutive
integers $N$ cannot be a perfect power.

[ES75] P. ErdГ¶s, J. L. Selfridge, "The product of consecutive integers is never a power",
  Illinois J. Math. 19(2): 292-301, 1975

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ k вүҘ 3, вҲҖ (n : в„•), В¬(вҲҸ x вҲҲ Finset.Ioc n (n + k), x).Powerful
problems.docstring:
Let $k\geq 3$. Can the product of any $k$ consecutive integers $N$ ever be powerful? That is,
must there always exist a prime $p\mid N$ such that $p^2\nmid N$?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (k : в„•),
  вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
    вҲҖ (m : в„•),
      0 < m вҶ’
        вҲғ P,
          P.card = k вҲ§ вҲҖ p вҲҲ P, Nat.Prime p вҲ§ p вҲЈ вҲҸ x вҲҲ Finset.Ioc m (m + n), x вҲ§ В¬p ^ 2 вҲЈ вҲҸ x вҲҲ Finset.Ioc m (m + n), x
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s [Er82c] conjectures that, if $k$ is fixed, then for all $n$ sufficiently large and all
positive integers $m$, there must be at least $k$ distinct primes $p$ such that
$p\mid m(m+1)\cdots (m+n)$ and yet $p^2$ does not divide the right hand side.

[Er82c] ErdЕ‘s, Paul, "Miscellaneous problems in number theory". Congr. Numer. (1982), 25-45.,

problems.statement: True вҶ” (Set.range fun n => n + n.totient).HasPosDensity
problems.docstring:
Does the set of integers of the form $n + \varphi(n)$ have positive (lower) density?

[GIL24] proved this was true.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ E,
    вҲҖ (n i j : в„•),
      1 вүӨ i вҶ’ i < j вҶ’ j вүӨ n / 2 вҶ’ (n, i, j) вҲү E вҶ’ вҲғ p, Nat.Prime p вҲ§ i < p вҲ§ p вҲЈ (n.choose i).gcd (n.choose j)
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s and Szekeres conjectured that, apart from a finite exceptional set of triples `(n, i, j)`,
one can always take `p > i` in the prime divisor statement.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (n i j : в„•), 1 вүӨ i вҶ’ i < j вҶ’ j вүӨ n / 2 вҶ’ вҲғ p, Nat.Prime p вҲ§ i вүӨ p вҲ§ p вҲЈ (n.choose i).gcd (n.choose j)
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘s Problem 699.** Is it true that for every $1 \le i < j \le n / 2$ there exists a prime
$p \ge i$ with $p \mid \gcd\big(\binom{n}{i}, \binom{n}{j}\big)$?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n i : в„•), 1 вүӨ i вҶ’ i вүӨ n / 2 вҶ’ вҲғ p, Nat.Prime p вҲ§ i < p вҲ§ p вҲЈ n.choose i
problems.docstring: Sylvester and Schur: for $1 \le i \le n/2$ there is a prime $p > i$ dividing `n.choose i`.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (p : в„•) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin p)),
    (вҲҖ (S : Finset (Fin p)), 2 вүӨ S.card вҶ’ (SimpleGraph.induce (вҶ‘S) G).edgeSet.ncard вүӨ 2 * S.card - 3) вҶ’
      вҲғ c > 0,
        вҲҖ (n : в„•) (H : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [inst : DecidableRel H.Adj],
          (вҲҖ (v : Fin n), 0 < H.degree v) вҶ’ вҶ‘(G.sizeRamsey H) вүӨ c * вҶ‘H.edgeSet.ncard
problems.docstring:
Let $G$ be such that any subgraph on $k$ vertices has at most $2k-3$ edges.
Is it true that, if $H$ has $m$ edges and no isolated vertices, then $\hat{r}(G,H) \ll m$?

In other words: if $G$ is sparse (every induced subgraph on $k$ vertices has $вүӨ 2k-3$ edges),
is $G$ Ramsey size linear?

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ {V : Type} [inst : Fintype V] [DecidableEq V] [Nonempty V] (G : SimpleGraph V),
    вҲғ D, (вҲҖ H вҲҲ D, Erdos184.IsCycleOrEdge H.coe) вҲ§ вӢғ H вҲҲ D, H.edgeSet = G.edgeSet вҲ§ вҶ‘D.card вүӨ вҶ‘(Fintype.card V) - 1
problems.docstring:
In [Er71] ErdЕ‘s suggests that only $n-1$ many cycles and edges are required if we do not
require them to be edge-disjoint.

вҲҖ Оө > 0,

problems.docstring:
Conlon, Fox, and Sudakov [CFS14] proved that $O_\epsilon(n)$ cycles and edges suffice if $G$ has
minimum degree at least $\epsilon n$, for any $\epsilon>0$.

вҲғ f,
  (f =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘n * Real.log вҶ‘n) вҲ§
    вҲҖ {V : Type u_1} [inst : Fintype V] [DecidableEq V] (G : SimpleGraph V),
      вҲғ D, (вҲҖ H вҲҲ D, Erdos184.IsCycleOrEdge H.coe) вҲ§ Erdos184.IsDecomposition G D вҲ§ вҶ‘D.card вүӨ f (Fintype.card V)
problems.docstring: ErdЕ‘s and Gallai [EGP66] proved that $O(n \log n)$ many cycles and edges suffices.

problems.statement:
вҲғ f,
  (f =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘n * вҶ‘(вҶ‘n).iteratedLog) вҲ§
    вҲҖ {V : Type u_1} [inst : Fintype V] [DecidableEq V] (G : SimpleGraph V),
      вҲғ D, (вҲҖ H вҲҲ D, Erdos184.IsCycleOrEdge H.coe) вҲ§ Erdos184.IsDecomposition G D вҲ§ вҶ‘D.card вүӨ f (Fintype.card V)
problems.docstring:
The best bound available is due to BuciДҮ and Montgomery [BM22], who prove that $O(n\log^* n)$ many
cycles and edges suffice, where $\log^*$ is the iterated logarithm function.

problems.statement:
вҲғ c > 0,
  вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop,

problems.docstring:
The graph $K_{3,n-3}$ shows that at least $(1+c)n$ many cycles and edges are required, for some
constant $c>0$.

problems.docstring: Any graph on $n$ vertices can be decomposed into $O(n)$ many edge-disjoint cycles and edges.

$F(n) \le O(n^{1/2} \ln ^ {3/4} n)$

Theorem 1.4 from [AKS07]

[AKS07] Alon, N. and Krivelevich, M. and Sudakov, B., Large nearly regular induced subgraphs. arXiv:0710.2106 (2007).

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ o, o =o[Filter.atTop] 1 вҲ§ вҲҖ (x : в„•), Erdos1073.A x вүӨ вҶ‘x ^ o x
problems.docstring: Is it true that $A(x) \le x^{o(1)}$?

problems.statement: вҲ‘' (n : в„•), 1 / (2 ^ n - 1) = вҲ‘' (n : в„•), вҶ‘n.divisors.card / 2 ^ n
problems.docstring:
Show that
$$
\sum_{n} \frac{1}{2^n - 1} = \sum_{n} \frac{d(n)}{2^n},
$$
where $d(n)$ is the number of divisors of $n$.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), A.Infinite вҶ’ Irrational (вҲ‘' (n : вҶ‘A), 1 / (2 ^ вҶ‘n - 1))
problems.docstring:
Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{N}$ be an infinite set. Is
$$
\sum_{n\in A} \frac{1}{2^n - 1}
$$
irrational?

problems.statement: Irrational (вҲ‘' (n : в„•), вҶ‘n.divisors.card / 2 ^ n)
problems.docstring:
Show that
$$
\sum_{n} \frac{d(n)}{2^n}
$$
is irrational.

[Er48] ErdЕ‘s, P., _On arithmetical properties of Lambert series_. J. Indian Math. Soc. (N.S.) (1948), 63-66.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (A : Set в„•),
    A.Infinite вҶ’
      Filter.Tendsto (fun N => вҶ‘(A.interIcc 1 вҢҠNвҢӢвӮҠ).ncard / N) Filter.atTop (nhds 0) вҶ’
        3 вүӨ Filter.limsup (fun N => вҶ‘((A + A).interIcc 1 вҢҠNвҢӢвӮҠ).ncard / вҶ‘(A.interIcc 1 вҢҠNвҢӢвӮҠ).ncard) Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{N}$ be an infinite set such that $|A\cap \{1, ..., N\}| = o(N)$.
Is it true that
$$
\limsup_{N\to\infty}\frac{|(A + A)\cap \{1, ..., N\}|}{|A \cap \{1, ..., N\}|} \geq 3?
$$

The answer is yes, proved by Freiman [Fr73].

[Fr73] Fre\u{\i}man, G. A., _Foundations of a structural theory of set addition_. (1973), vii+108.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (A : Set в„•),
  A.Infinite вҶ’
    Filter.Tendsto (fun N => вҶ‘(A.interIcc 1 вҢҠNвҢӢвӮҠ).ncard / N) Filter.atTop (nhds 0) вҶ’
      2 вүӨ Filter.limsup (fun N => вҶ‘((A + A).interIcc 1 вҢҠNвҢӢвӮҠ).ncard / вҶ‘(A.interIcc 1 вҢҠNвҢӢвӮҠ).ncard) Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{N}$ be an infinite set such that $|A\cap \{1, ..., N\}| = o(N)$.
Then
$$
\limsup_{N\to\infty}\frac{|(A + A)\cap \{1, ..., N\}|}{|A \cap \{1, ..., N\}|} \geq 2.
$$

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (S : Set (Fin 3 вҶ’ в„Ө)),
    S.Finite вҶ’
      вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ Fin 3 вҶ’ в„Ө),
        Erdos193.IsSWalk S a вҶ’
          (Set.range a).Infinite вҶ’ Erdos193.HasCollinearTriple в„ҡ (Set.range fun n => Int.cast вҲҳ a n)
problems.docstring:
Let $S \subseteq \mathbb{Z}^3$ be a finite set and let $A = \lbrace a_1, a_2, \ldots \rbrace$ be
an infinite $S$-walk, so that $a_{i+1} - a_i \in S$ for all $i$. Must $A$ contain three collinear
points?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (S : Set (Fin 2 вҶ’ в„Ө)),
  S.Finite вҶ’
    вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ Fin 2 вҶ’ в„Ө),
      Erdos193.IsSWalk S a вҶ’ (Set.range a).Infinite вҶ’ Erdos193.HasCollinearTriple в„ҡ (Set.range fun n => Int.cast вҲҳ a n)
problems.docstring: [GeRa79] showed that the answer is yes for $\mathbb{Z}^2$

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), BddAbove (Erdos90.unitDistanceCounts n)
problems.docstring:
This lemma confirms that the set of possible unit distance counts is bounded above, which
ensures that taking the supremum (`sSup`) is a well-defined operation. The trivial upper bound is
the total number of pairs of points, $\binom{n}{2}$.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ O,
    вҲғ (_ : O =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => 1 / Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘n)),
      (fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos90.maxUnitDistances n)) =б¶ [Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘n ^ (1 + O n)
problems.docstring:
Does every set of $n$ distinct points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ contain at most
$n^{1+O(\frac{1}{\log\log n})}$ many pairs which are distance $1$ apart?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (a : в„• вҶ’ в„•), вҲҖ Оө > 0, 0 < a 0 вҶ’ StrictMono a вҶ’ вҲғ k, вҲҖ X > 0, вҶ‘вҶ‘(Erdos873.F a X k) < вҶ‘(X ^ Оө)
problems.docstring:
Let $A = \{a_1 < a_2 < \dots\} \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ and let $F(A,X,k)$ count the number of $i$
such that $[a_i,a_{i+1}, \dots ,a_{i+kвҲ’1}] < X$, where the left-hand side is the least common
multiple. Is it true that, for every $\epsilon > 0$, there exists some $k$ such that
$F(A,X,k) < X^\epsilon$?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (V : Type u_1) [inst : AddCommGroup V] [Module в„ҡ V],
  вҲғ X, (вҲҖ (Y : Set V), Y.IsAPOfLength вҠӨ вҶ’ (X вҲ© Y).Nonempty) вҲ§ вҲҖ (Y : Set V), Y.IsAPOfLength 3 вҶ’ (X вҲ© Y).ncard вүӨ 2
problems.docstring: The statement for which Baumgartner actually writes a proof.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (V : Type u_1) [inst : AddCommGroup V] [Module в„ҡ V] (k : в„•),
  вҲғ X, (вҲҖ (Y : Set V), Y.IsAPOfLength вҠӨ вҶ’ (X вҲ© Y).Nonempty) вҲ§ вҲҖ (Y : Set V), Y.IsAPOfLength вҶ‘k вҶ’ (X вҲ© Y).ncard вүӨ 2
problems.docstring:
Let $V$ be a vector space over the rationals and let $k$ be a fixed
positive integer. Then there is a set $X_k \subseteq V$ such that $X_k$ meets
every infinite arithmetic progression in $V$ but $X_k$ intersects every
$k$-element arithmetic progression in at most two points.

At the end of [Ba75] the author claims that by "slightly modifying the method of [his proof]", one
can prove this.

problems.statement: (вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), IsSidon A вҶ’ вҲғ Y, Y.IsAPOfLength вҠӨ вҲ§ Y вҠҶ Aб¶ң) вҶ” False
problems.docstring:
The answer is no; ErdЕ‘s and Graham report this was proved by Baumgartner, presumably referring to
the paper [Ba75], which does not state this exactly, but the following simple construction is
implicit in [Ba75].

Let $P_1,P_2,\ldots$ be an enumeration of all countably many infinite arithmetic progressions. We
choose $a_1$ to be the minimal element of $P_1\cap \mathbb{N}$, and in general choose $a_n$ to be an
element of $P_n\cap \mathbb{N}$ such that $a_n>2a_{n-1}$. By construction $A=\{a_1 < a_2 < \cdots\}$
contains at least one element from every infinite arithmetic progression, and is a lacunary set, so
is certainly Sidon.

AlphaProof has found the following explicit construction: $A = \{ (n+1)!+n : n\geq 0\}$. This is a
Sidon set, and intersects every arithmetic progression, since for any $a,d\in \mathbb{N}$,
$(a+d+1)!+(a+d)\in A$, and $d$ divides $(a+d+1)!+d$.

This was formalized in Lean by Alexeev using Aristotle.

problems.statement: вҲғ A, A = {x | вҲғ n, n.factorial + n = x} вҲ§ IsSidon A вҲ§ вҲҖ (Y : Set в„•), Y.IsAPOfLength вҠӨ вҶ’ (A вҲ© Y).Nonempty
problems.docstring: In fact one such sequence is $n! + n$. This was found by AlphaProof. It also found $(n + 1)! + n$.

problems.statement: sorry = sSup {k | вҲҖ (f : в„Ө вүғ в„Ө), HasMonotoneAP (вҮ‘f) k}
problems.docstring:
What is the largest $k$ such that in any permutation of $\mathbb{Z}$ there must exist a
monotone $k$-term arithmetic progression $x_1 < \cdots < x_k$?

problems.statement: 5 вүҘ sSup {k | вҲҖ (f : в„Ө вүғ в„Ө), HasMonotoneAP (вҮ‘f) k}
problems.docstring: Geneson [Ge19] proved that k вүӨ 5.

problems.statement: 4 вүҘ sSup {k | вҲҖ (f : в„Ө вүғ в„Ө), HasMonotoneAP (вҮ‘f) k}
problems.docstring: Adenwalla [Ad22] proved that k вүӨ 4.

problems.statement:
False вҶ”
  вҲғ k,
    вҲҖ (V : Set (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))),
      V.Finite вҶ’
        (SimpleGraph.UnitDistancePlaneGraph V).girth вүҘ k вҶ’ (SimpleGraph.UnitDistancePlaneGraph V).chromaticNumber вүӨ 3
problems.docstring:
Let $G$ be a finite unit distance graph in $\mamthbb{R}^2$.
Is there some $k$ such that if $G$ has girth $вүҘ k$, then $\chi(G) вүӨ 3$?

The general case was solved by O'Donnell [OD99], who constructed finite unit distance graphs with
chromatic number $4$ and arbitrarily large girth.

problems.statement: (вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), A.Finite вҶ’ IsSidon A вҶ’ вҲғ B, вҲғ n > 0, A вҠҶ B вҲ§ IsPerfectDifferenceSet B n) вҶ” False
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘s Problem 707**: It is false that any finite Sidon set can be embedded in a perfect
different set modulo some $n$.

As described in [arxiv/2510.19804], a counterexample is provided in [Ha47], see below.
The proof of this has been formalized.

This was formalized in Lean by Alexeev using ChatGPT.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), A = {1, 2, 4, 8, 13} вҶ’ Finite вҶ‘A вҲ§ IsSidon A вҲ§ вҲҖ (B : Set в„•) (n : в„•), A вҠҶ B вҶ’ В¬IsPerfectDifferenceSet B n
problems.docstring:
Alexeev and Mixon [arxiv/2510.19804] have disproved this conjecture, showing that $\{1, 2, 4, 8, 13\}$ cannot be
extended to any perfect difference set.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (p : в„•), IsPrimePow p вҶ’ вҲғ B, IsPerfectDifferenceSet B (p ^ 2 + p + 1) вҲ§ B.ncard = p + 1
problems.docstring:
The Singer construction gives perfect difference sets for `n = p^2 + p + 1` where `p` is a
prime power.

problems.statement: IsSidon {1, 2, 4}
problems.docstring: The set `{1, 2, 4}` is a Sidon set.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (A : Set в„•),
  A.Finite вҶ’ A.ncard вүӨ 3 вҶ’ IsSidon A вҶ’ вҲғ B p, IsPrimePow p вҲ§ A вҠҶ B вҲ§ IsPerfectDifferenceSet B (p ^ 2 + p + 1)
problems.docstring: For small Sidon sets, we can check the conjecture directly.

problems.statement: вҲғ B, {1, 2, 4} вҠҶ B вҲ§ IsPerfectDifferenceSet B 7
problems.docstring: The set `{1, 2, 4}` can be embedded in a perfect difference set modulo 7.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (A : Set в„•),
  A = {1, 2, 4, 8} вҶ’
    Finite вҶ‘A вҲ§ IsSidon A вҲ§ вҲҖ (B : Set в„•) (p : в„•), Prime p вҶ’ A вҠҶ B вҶ’ В¬IsPerfectDifferenceSet B (p ^ 2 + p + 1)
problems.docstring:
Alexeev and Mixon [arxiv/2510.19804] have disproved this conjecture, proving that $\{1,2,4,8\}$
cannot be extended to a perfect difference set modulo $p^2+p+1$
for any prime $p$.

problems.statement: (вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), A.Finite вҶ’ IsSidon A вҶ’ вҲғ B p, Nat.Prime p вҲ§ A вҠҶ B вҲ§ IsPerfectDifferenceSet B (p ^ 2 + p + 1)) вҶ” False
problems.docstring:
It is false that any finite Sidon set can be embedded in a perfect
difference set modulo `p^2 + p + 1` for some prime `p`.

As described in [arxiv/2510.19804], a counterexample is provided in [Ha47], see below.
The proof of this has been formalized.

problems.statement: (вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), A.Finite вҶ’ IsSidon A вҶ’ вҲғ B p, IsPrimePow p вҲ§ A вҠҶ B вҲ§ IsPerfectDifferenceSet B (p ^ 2 + p + 1)) вҶ” False
problems.docstring:
It is false that any finite Sidon set can be embedded in a perfect
difference set modulo `p^2 + p + 1` for some prime power `p`.

As described in [arxiv/2510.19804], a counterexample is provided in [Ha47], see below.
The proof of this has been formalized.
-

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (A : Set в„•),
  A = {1, 3, 9, 10, 13} вҶ’ Finite вҶ‘A вҲ§ IsSidon A вҲ§ вҲҖ (B : Set в„•) (n : в„•), A вҠҶ B вҶ’ В¬IsPerfectDifferenceSet B n
problems.docstring:
This conjecture was actually first disproved by Hall in 1947 [Ha47], long before ErdЕ‘s asked
this question.
A counterexample for any modulus from from [Ha47] in the paragraph following Theorem 4.3, where it
was given as $\{-8, -6, 0, 1, 4\}$, but this can be shifted to natural numbers
as pointed out in [arxiv/2510.19804].

problems.statement: вҲҖ (B : Set в„•) (n : в„•), IsPerfectDifferenceSet B n вҶ’ B.ncard вүӨ n.sqrt + 1
problems.docstring: A perfect difference set modulo `n` must have size `вүӨ вҲҡn + 1`.

problems.statement: Erdos961.Erdos961Prop 1 1
True вҶ” вҲғ C, вҲҖб¶  (k : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҶ‘(Erdos961.f k) < Real.log вҶ‘k ^ C
problems.docstring: It is conjectured that $f(k) \ll (\log k)^O(1)$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (k : в„•), 0 < k вҶ’ вҲғ n, Erdos961.Erdos961Prop k n

(fun k => вҶ‘(Erdos961.f k)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun k =>
  Real.log (Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘k)) / Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘k) * (вҶ‘k / Real.log вҶ‘k)
problems.docstring:
Jutila [Ju74], and Ramachandra--Shorey [RaSh73] proved a stronger upper bound
$f(k) \ll \frac{\log \log \log k}{\log \log k} \frac{k}{\log k}$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (k : в„•), 0 < k вҶ’ Erdos961.Erdos961Prop k k
problems.docstring:
Sylvester and Schur [Er34] proved that every set of $k$ consecutive integers greater than $k$
contains an integer divisible by a prime greater than $k$, i.e. not $(k+1)$-smooth.

problems.statement: вҲҖб¶  (k : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҶ‘(Erdos961.f k) < 3 * вҶ‘k / Real.log вҶ‘k
problems.docstring: Erdos [Er55d] proved $f(k) < 3 \frac{k}{\log k}$ for sufficiently large $k$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (m : Type u_1) [Infinite m], True вҶ” вҲғ c, вҲҖ (X : Set m), Cardinal.mk вҶ‘X = Erdos598.Оә вҶ’ c '' {s | вҶ‘s вҠҶ X} = Set.univ
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘s Problem 598:**
Let $m$ be an infinite cardinal and $\kappa$ be the successor cardinal of $2^{\aleph_0}$.
Can one colour the countable subsets of $m$ using $\kappa$ many colours so that every
$X \subseteq m$ with $|X| = \kappa$ contains subsets of all possible colours?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (f : в„• вҶ’ вҶҘ{-1, 1}) (C : в„қ), 0 < C вҶ’ вҲғ d вүҘ 1, вҲғ m вүҘ 1, C < |вҲ‘ k вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 m, вҶ‘(f (k * d))|
problems.docstring:
**The ErdЕ‘s discrepancy problem**

If $f\colon \mathbb N \rightarrow \{-1, +1\}$ then is it true that for every $C>0$ there
exist $d, m \ge 1$ such that $$\left\lvert \sum_{1\leq k\leq m}f(kd)\right\rvert > C?$$
This is true, and was proved by Tao [Ta16]

problems.statement: вҲҖ (f : в„• вҶ’ вҶ‘(Metric.sphere 0 1)) (C : в„қ), 0 < C вҶ’ вҲғ d вүҘ 1, вҲғ m вүҘ 1, C < вҖ–вҲ‘ k вҲҲ Finset.Icc 1 m, вҶ‘(f (k * d))вҖ–
problems.docstring:
**The ErdЕ‘s discrepancy problem (complex variant)**

If $f\colon \mathbb N \rightarrow S^1 вҠҶ в„Ӯ$ then is it true that for every $C>0$ there
exist $d, m \ge 1$ such that $$\left\lvert \sum_{1\leq k\leq m}f(kd)\right\rvert > C?$$
This is true, and was proved by Tao [Ta16]

problems.statement: True вҶ” Erdos424.generatedSet.HasPosDensity
problems.docstring:
Let $a_1 = 2$ and $a_2 = 3$ and continue the sequence by appending to $a_1, \ldots, a_n$ all possible
values of $a_i a_j - 1$ with $i \neq j$.
Is it true that the set of integers which eventually appear has positive density?

problems.statement:
вҲҖ (C : в„қ),
  0.5 < C вҶ’
    вҲҖ (f : вҶҘ(MeasureTheory.Lp в„Ӯ 2 AddCircle.haarAddCircle)) (n : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
      IsLacunary n вҶ’
        ((fun k => (MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (fourierPartial f k) 2 AddCircle.haarAddCircle).toReal) =O[Filter.atTop]
            fun k => 1 / Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘k) ^ C) вҶ’
          вҲҖбөҗ (x : AddCircle 1),
            Filter.Tendsto (fun N => (вҲ‘ k вҲҲ Finset.range N, вҶ‘вҶ‘f (n k вҖў x)) / вҶ‘N) Filter.atTop
              (nhds (вҲ« (t : AddCircle 1), вҶ‘вҶ‘f t вҲӮAddCircle.haarAddCircle))
problems.docstring: The following theorem is proved in [Ma66].

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ C,
    0 < C вҲ§
      вҲҖ (f : вҶҘ(MeasureTheory.Lp в„Ӯ 2 AddCircle.haarAddCircle)) (n : в„• вҶ’ в„•),
        IsLacunary n вҶ’
          ((fun k => (MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (fourierPartial f k) 2 AddCircle.haarAddCircle).toReal) =O[Filter.atTop]
              fun k => 1 / Real.log (Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘k)) ^ C) вҶ’
            вҲҖбөҗ (x : AddCircle 1),
              Filter.Tendsto (fun N => (вҲ‘ k вҲҲ Finset.range N, вҶ‘вҶ‘f (n k вҖў x)) / вҶ‘N) Filter.atTop
                (nhds (вҲ« (t : AddCircle 1), вҶ‘вҶ‘f t вҲӮAddCircle.haarAddCircle))
problems.docstring:
Does there exists a positive constant `C` such that for all `f вҲҲ LВІ[0,1]` and all lacunary
sequences `n`, if `вҖ–f - fвӮ–вҖ–вӮӮ = O(1 / log log log k ^ C)`, then for almost every `x`,
`lim вҲ‘ k вҲҲ Finset.range N, f (n k вҖў x)) / N = вҲ« t, f t вҲӮt`?

problems.statement: True вҶ” {p | вҲғ q k l, Nat.Prime p вҲ§ Nat.Prime q вҲ§ p = 2 ^ k * 3 ^ l * q + 1}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
Are there infinitely many primes $p$ such that $p = 2^k 3^l q + 1$
for some prime $q$ and $k вүҘ 0$, $l вүҘ 0$?

problems.statement: True вҶ” {p | вҲғ q k, Nat.Prime p вҲ§ Nat.Prime q вҲ§ p = 2 ^ k * q + 1}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
Are there infinitely many primes $p$ such that $p = 2^k * q + 1$
for some prime $q$ and $k вүҘ 0$?

This is mentioned as B46
in [Unsolved Problems in Number Theory](https://doi.org/10.1007/978-0-387-26677-0)
by *Richard K. Guy*

problems.statement: вҲҖ {О№ : Type u_1} [Finite О№] (A : О№ вҶ’ в„•), В¬Erdos26.IsThick A
вҲғ A, StrictMono A вҲ§ В¬Erdos26.IsThick A вҲ§ вҲҖ (k : в„•), В¬Erdos26.IsBehrend fun x => A x + k
problems.docstring: If we allow for $\sum_{a\in A} \frac{1}{a} < \infty$ then Rusza has found a counter-example.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {О№ : Type u_1} [Infinite О№], вҲҖ r > 0, Erdos26.IsThick fun x => r
вҲҖ {О№ : Type u_1} (A : О№ вҶ’ в„•) {Оө : в„қ}, Оө < 0 вҶ’ В¬Erdos26.IsWeaklyBehrend A Оө
False вҶ” вҲҖ (A : в„• вҶ’ в„•), StrictMono A вҶ’ Erdos26.IsThick A вҶ’ вҲғ k, Erdos26.IsBehrend fun x => A x + k
problems.docstring:
Let $A\subset\mathbb{N}$ be infinite such that $\sum_{a \in A} \frac{1}{a} = \infty$. Must
there exist some $k\geq 1$ such that almost all integers have a divisor of the form $a+k$
for some $a\in A$?

This was formalized in Lean by Alexeev using Aristotle.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {О№ : Type u_1} (A : О№ вҶ’ в„•) {Оө : в„қ}, 1 вүӨ Оө вҶ’ Erdos26.IsWeaklyBehrend A Оө
True вҶ” вҲҖ (A : в„• вҶ’ в„•), StrictMono A вҶ’ Erdos26.IsThick A вҶ’ вҲҖ Оө > 0, вҲғ k, Erdos26.IsWeaklyBehrend (fun x => A x + k) Оө
problems.docstring:
Tenenbaum asked the weaker variant where for every $\epsilon>0$ there is
some $k=k(\epsilon)$ such that at least $1-\epsilon$ density of all integers have a
divisor of the form $a+k$ for some $a\in A$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {О№ : Type u_1} (A : О№ вҶ’ в„•), 1 вҲҲ Set.range A вҶ’ Erdos26.MultiplesOf A = Set.univ
вҲҖ r > 1, В¬Erdos26.IsThick fun n => r ^ n
вҲҖ {О№ : Type u_1} (A : О№ вҶ’ в„•), 1 вҲҲ Set.range A вҶ’ Erdos26.IsBehrend A
True вҶ” вҲғ C, 0 < C вҲ§ вҲғб¶  (m : в„•) in Filter.atTop, m.IsPractical вҲ§ вҶ‘(Erdos18.practicalH m) < Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘m) ^ C
problems.docstring:
**Conjecture 1.**
Are there infinitely many practical numbers $m$ such that $h(m) < (\log \log m)^{O(1)}$?

More precisely: does there exist a constant $C > 0$ such that for infinitely many
practical numbers $m$, we have $h(m) < (\log \log m)^C$?

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ C, 0 < C вҲ§ вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҶ‘(Erdos18.practicalH n.factorial) < Real.log вҶ‘n ^ C
problems.docstring:
**Conjecture 3.**
Or perhaps even $h(n!) < (\log n)^{O(1)}$?

ErdЕ‘s offered \$250 for a proof or disproof.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲҖ (Оө : в„қ), 0 < Оө вҶ’ вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, вҶ‘(Erdos18.practicalH n.factorial) < вҶ‘n ^ Оө
problems.docstring:
**Conjecture 2.**
Is it true that $h(n!) < n^{o(1)}$? That is, for all $\varepsilon > 0$,
is $h(n!) < n^\varepsilon$ for sufficiently large $n$?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), n.factorial.IsPractical
problems.docstring: $h(n!)$ is well-defined since $n!$ is practical for $n вүҘ 1$.

problems.statement: Erdos18.practicalH 2 = 1
problems.docstring: $h(2) = 1$: divisors are {1, 2}, each of m=1,2 needs only 1 divisor.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (n : в„•), n.IsPractical вҶ’ Erdos18.practicalH n вүӨ n.divisors.card
problems.docstring: For any practical number $n$, $h(n) вүӨ number of divisors of $n$.

problems.statement: Erdos18.practicalH 1 = 1
problems.docstring: $h(1) = 1$: we need the single divisor {1} to represent 1.

problems.statement: вҲғ C, 0 < C вҲ§ вҲғб¶  (m : в„•) in Filter.atTop, m.IsPractical вҲ§ вҶ‘(Erdos18.practicalH m) < C * Real.log вҶ‘m ^ (1 / 2)
problems.docstring:
**Vose's Theorem.**
Vose proved the existence of infinitely many practical numbers $m$ such that
$h(m) \ll (\log m)^{1/2}$. This gives a positive answer to a weaker form of Conjecture 1.

problems.statement: Erdos18.practicalH 6 = 2
problems.docstring:
$h(6) = 2$: divisors are {1, 2, 3, 6}. The hardest m to represent is
m=4 or m=5, each requiring 2 divisors: 4=1+3, 5=2+3.

problems.statement: вҲҖб¶  (n : в„•) in Filter.atTop, Erdos18.practicalH n.factorial < n
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘s's Theorem.**
ErdЕ‘s proved that $h(n!) < n$ for all $n \ge 1$.

problems.statement: Erdos18.practicalH 12 = 3
problems.docstring:
$h(12) = 3$: divisors are {1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 12}. The hardest m is
m=11, requiring 3 divisors: 11=1+4+6.

problems.statement: вҲғ n m, (n, m) вҲҲ S вҲ§ m вү  n + 1
problems.docstring:
There are examples where $(n, m) вҲҲ S$ with $m вү  n + 1$.

(Found by AlphaProof, although it was implicit already in [A129515])
For example, $(87,88)$ and $(607,608)$ are such pairs.

problems.statement: True вҶ” S.Infinite
problems.docstring:
Are there infinitely many pairs of integers $n < m$ such that $\binom{2n}{n}$
and $\binom{2m}{m}$ have the same set of prime divisors?

problems.statement: True вҶ” {(n, m) | n.totient = (ArithmeticFunction.sigma 1) m}.Infinite
problems.docstring: Are there infinitely many integers $n, m$ such that $П•(n) = Пғ(m)$?

problems.statement:
False вҶ”
  вҲҖ (A B : Set в„•),
    ((fun n => вҶ‘n ^ (1 / 2)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Nat.count A n)) вҶ’
      ((fun n => вҶ‘n ^ (1 / 2)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Nat.count B n)) вҶ’
        {(aвӮҒ, aвӮӮ, bвӮҒ, bвӮӮ) | aвӮҒ вҲҲ A вҲ§ aвӮӮ вҲҲ A вҲ§ bвӮҒ вҲҲ B вҲ§ bвӮӮ вҲҲ B вҲ§ aвӮҒ вү  aвӮӮ вҲ§ aвӮҒ + bвӮӮ = aвӮӮ + bвӮҒ}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
Let $A,B\subseteq \mathbb{N}$ such that for all large $N$\[\lvert A\cap \{1,\ldots,N\}\rvert \gg
N^{1/2}\]and\[\lvert B\cap \{1,\ldots,N\}\rvert \gg N^{1/2}.\]
Is it true that there are infinitely many solutions to $a_1-a_2=b_1-b_2\neq 0$ with $a_1,a_2\in A$
and $b_1,b_2\in B$?

Ruzsa has observed that there is a simple counterexample: take $A$ to be the set of numbers whose
binary representation has only non-zero digits in even places, and $B$ similarly but with non-zero
digits only in odd places. It is easy to see $A$ and $B$ both grow like $\gg N^{1/2}$ and yet for
any $n\geq 1$ there is exactly one solution to $n=a+b$ with $a\in A$ and $b\in B$.

This was formalized in Lean by van Doorn using Aristotle.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (A B : Set в„•),
    (вҲғ c_A > 0, Asymptotics.IsEquivalent Filter.atTop (fun n => вҶ‘(Nat.count A n)) fun n => c_A * вҶ‘n ^ (1 / 2)) вҶ’
      (вҲғ c_B > 0, Asymptotics.IsEquivalent Filter.atTop (fun n => вҶ‘(Nat.count B n)) fun n => c_B * вҶ‘n ^ (1 / 2)) вҶ’
        {(aвӮҒ, aвӮӮ, bвӮҒ, bвӮӮ) | aвӮҒ вҲҲ A вҲ§ aвӮӮ вҲҲ A вҲ§ bвӮҒ вҲҲ B вҲ§ bвӮӮ вҲҲ B вҲ§ aвӮҒ вү  aвӮӮ вҲ§ aвӮҒ + bвӮӮ = aвӮӮ + bвӮҒ}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
Ruzsa suggests that a non-trivial variant of this problem arises if one imposes the stronger
condition that $|A \cap \{1,\dots,N\}| \sim c_A N^{1/2}$ for some constant $c_A>0$, and similarly
for $B$.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (r : в„•+), вҲғ p, Nat.Prime p вҲ§ Erdos1055.IsOfClass r p
problems.docstring:
A prime $p$ is in class $1$ if the only prime divisors of $p+1$ are
$2$ or $3$. In general, a prime $p$ is in class $r$ if every prime factor
of $p+1$ is in some class $\leq r-1$, with equality for at least one prime factor.
Show that for each $r$ there exists a prime $p$ of class $r$.

problems.statement: вҲғ M, вҲҖ (r : в„•+), вҶ‘(Erdos1055.p r) ^ (1 / вҶ‘вҶ‘r) вүӨ M
problems.docstring:
A prime $p$ is in class $1$ if the only prime divisors of $p+1$ are
$2$ or $3$. In general, a prime $p$ is in class $r$ if every prime factor
of $p+1$ is in some class $\leq r-1$, with equality for at least one prime factor.
If $p_r$ is the least prime in class $r$, then how does $p_r^{1/r}$ behave?
Selfridge conjectured that this is bounded.

problems.statement: вҲҖ (r : в„•+), {p | Nat.Prime p вҲ§ Erdos1055.IsOfClass r p}.Infinite
problems.docstring:
A prime $p$ is in class $1$ if the only prime divisors of $p+1$ are
$2$ or $3$. In general, a prime $p$ is in class $r$ if every prime factor
of $p+1$ is in some class $\leq r-1$, with equality for at least one prime factor.
Are there infinitely many primes in each class?

problems.statement: Filter.Tendsto (fun r => вҶ‘(Erdos1055.p r) ^ (1 / вҶ‘вҶ‘r)) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
problems.docstring:
A prime $p$ is in class $1$ if the only prime divisors of $p+1$ are
$2$ or $3$. In general, a prime $p$ is in class $r$ if every prime factor
of $p+1$ is in some class $\leq r-1$, with equality for at least one prime factor.
If $p_r$ is the least prime in class $r$, then how does $p_r^{1/r}$ behave?
Erdos conjectured that this tends to infinity.

problems.statement:
вҲҖ {S : в„• вҶ’ Set в„Ӯ},
  (вҲҖ (k : в„•), derivedSet (S k) = вҲ…) вҶ’
    вҲғ f n,
      Differentiable в„Ӯ f вҲ§
        Transcendental (Polynomial в„Ӯ) f вҲ§ вҲҖ (k : в„•), 0 < n k вҲ§ вҲҖ {z : в„Ӯ}, z вҲҲ S k вҶ’ iteratedDeriv (n k) f z = 0
problems.docstring:
Let $\{S_k\}$ be any sequence of sets in the complex plane, each of which has no finite
limit point. Then there exists a sequence $\{n_k\}$ of positive integers and a
transcendental entire function $f(z)$ such that $f^{(n_k)}(z) = 0$ if $z \in S_k$.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ (S : в„• вҶ’ Set в„Ӯ),
    (вҲҖ (n : в„•), derivedSet (S n) = вҲ…) вҶ’
      вҲғ f, Transcendental (Polynomial в„Ӯ) f вҲ§ Differentiable в„Ӯ f вҲ§ вҲҖ n вүҘ 1, вҲғ k, вҲҖ z вҲҲ S n, iteratedDeriv k f z = 0
problems.docstring:
Let $(S_n)_{n \ge 1}$ be a sequence of sets of complex numbers, none of which have a finite
limit point. Does there exist an entire transcendental function $f(z)$ such that, for all $n \ge 1$, there
exists some $k_n \ge 0$ such that $f^{(k_n)}(z) = 0$ for all $z \in S_n$.

This is Problem 2.30 in [Ha74], where it is attributed to ErdЕ‘s.

Solved in the affirmative by Barth and Schneider [BaSc72].

This was formalized in Lean by Alexeev using Aristotle.

problems.statement: True вҶ” {p | Nat.Prime p вҲ§ Erdos1072.f p = p - 1}.Infinite
problems.docstring: Is it true that there are infinitely many $p$ for which $f(p) = p вҲ’ 1$?

problems.statement: (fun x => вҶ‘({p | Nat.Prime p вҲ§ Erdos1072.f p = p - 1}.interIcc 0 x).ncard) =o[Filter.atTop] fun x => вҶ‘x / Real.log вҶ‘x
problems.docstring:
ErdЕ‘s, Hardy, and Subbarao [HaSu02], believed that the number of $p \le x$ for which $f(p)=pвҲ’1$
is $o(x/\log x)$.

[HaSu02] Hardy, G. E. and Subbarao, M. V., _A modified problem of Pillai and some related questions._
Amer. Math. Monthly (2002), 554--559.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ P вҠҶ {p | Nat.Prime p},
    P.HasDensity 1 {p | Nat.Prime p} вҲ§
      Filter.Tendsto (fun p => вҶ‘(Erdos1072.f p) / вҶ‘p) (Filter.atTop вҠ“ Filter.principal P) (nhds 0)
problems.docstring: Is it true that $f(p)/p \to 0$ for $p \to \infty$ in a density 1 subset of the primes?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (A : Set в„•) (N : в„•), 1 вүӨ N вҶ’ A.IsMaximalSidonSetIn N вҶ’ 0 вҲҲ A - A
problems.docstring: For any maximal Sidon set, the difference set contains 0.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲҖ M вүҘ 1,
    вҲҖб¶  (N : в„•) in Filter.atTop,
      вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), A.IsMaximalSidonSetIn N вҶ’ вҲғ B вҠҶ Set.Icc 1 N, IsSidon B вҲ§ B.ncard = M вҲ§ (A - A) вҲ© (B - B) = {0}
problems.docstring:
**ErdЕ‘s Problem 42**: Let M вүҘ 1 and N be sufficiently large in terms of M. Is it true that for every
maximal Sidon set `A вҠҶ {1,вҖҰ,N}` there is another Sidon set `B вҠҶ {1,вҖҰ,N}` of size M such that
`(A - A) вҲ© (B - B) = {0}`?

problems.statement: {1, 2, 4}.IsMaximalSidonSetIn 4
problems.docstring: The set `{1, 2, 4}` is a maximal Sidon set in `{1, ..., 4}`.
The difference set of `{1, 2, 4}` is `{0, 1, 2, 3}`.

problems.statement:
True вҶ”
  вҲғ f,
    вҲҖ (M N : в„•),
      1 вүӨ M вҶ’
        f M вүӨ N вҶ’
          вҲҖ (A : Set в„•), A.IsMaximalSidonSetIn N вҶ’ вҲғ B вҠҶ Set.Icc 1 N, IsSidon B вҲ§ B.ncard = M вҲ§ (A - A) вҲ© (B - B) = {0}
problems.docstring:
A variant asking for explicit bounds on how large N needs to be in terms of M.

This version provides a constructive function f such that for all M вүҘ 1 and N вүҘ f(M),
every maximal Sidon set A вҠҶ {1,вҖҰ,N} has another Sidon set B вҠҶ {1,вҖҰ,N} of size M with
disjoint difference sets (apart from 0).

problems.statement:
False вҶ”
  вҲҖ (C : в„қ),
    0 < C вҶ’
      have f := fun x =>
        match x with
        | (k, l) => 2 ^ k * 3 ^ l;
      {x | вҲғ B, вҲғ (h : B.Nonempty) (_ : вҶ‘(B.sup f) вүӨ C * вҶ‘(B.inf' h f)), вҲ‘ x вҲҲ B, f x = x}.HasDensity 0
problems.docstring:
Let $C > 0$. Is it true that the set of integers of the form $n = b_1 + \cdots + b_t$,
with $b_1 < \cdots < b_t$, where $b_i = 2^{k_i}3^{l_i}$ for $1 \leq i\leq t$ and
$b_t \leq Cb_1$ has density $0$?

van Doorn and Everts \cite{vDEv25} have disproved this with $C=6$ - in fact, they prove that all
integers can be written as such a sum in which $b_t<6b_1$.

This was formalized in Lean by Alexeev using Aristotle.

problems.statement: вҲғ c > 0, (fun n => Real.exp (c * Real.log вҶ‘n ^ (1 / 12))) =O[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos160.erdos_160.h n)
problems.docstring:
The observation of Zachary Hunter in [that question](https://mathoverflow.net/q/410808)
coupled with the bounds of Kelley-Meka [KeMe23](https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.05537) imply that
$$h(N) \gg \exp(c(\log N)^{\frac 1 {12}})$$
for some $c > 0$.

(fun n => вҶ‘(Erdos160.erdos_160.h n)) =O[Filter.atTop] upper_bound вҲ§ upper_bound =o[Filter.atTop] fun n => вҶ‘n ^ (2 / 3)

On [Mathoverflow](https://mathoverflow.net/a/410815) user
[leechlattice](https://mathoverflow.net/users/125498/leechlattice) shows that
$h(n) \ll n^{\frac 2 3}$.

вҲҖ {a b : в„•}, Erdos304.smallestCollection a b = 1 вҶ’ вҲғ m, 1 < m вҲ§ вҶ‘a / вҶ‘b = (вҶ‘m)вҒ»В№
(fun b => Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘b)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun b => вҶ‘(Erdos304.smallestCollectionTo b)
problems.docstring:
In 1950, ErdЕ‘s [Er50c] proved the lower bound $$\log \log b \ll N(b)$$.
[Er50c] ErdЕ‘s, P., Az ${1}/{x_1} + {1}/{x_2} + \ldots + {1}/{x_n} =A/B$ egyenlet eg\'{E}sz sz\'{A}m\'{u} megold\'{A}sairГіl. Mat. Lapok (1950), 192-210.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {a b : в„•}, a = 0 вҲЁ b = 0 вҶ’ Erdos304.unitFractionExpressible a b = {0}
(fun b => вҶ‘(Erdos304.smallestCollectionTo b)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun b => вҲҡ(Real.log вҶ‘b)
problems.docstring:
In 1985 Vose [Vo85] proved the upper bound $$N(b) \ll \sqrt{\log b}$$.
[Vo85] Vose, Michael D., Egyptian fractions. Bull. London Math. Soc. (1985), 21-24.

problems.statement: вҲҖ {a b : в„•}, 1 вҲҲ Erdos304.unitFractionExpressible a b вҶ’ a вҲЈ b

вҲҖ {a b : в„•}, 0 вҲҲ Erdos304.unitFractionExpressible a b вҶ” a = 0 вҲЁ b = 0
вҲҖ (b : в„•), 1 < b вҶ’ Erdos304.smallestCollection 1 b = 1
True вҶ” (fun b => вҶ‘(Erdos304.smallestCollectionTo b)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun b => Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘b)
problems.docstring: Is it true that $$N(b) \ll \log \log b$$?

problems.statement: вҲҖ {a b : в„•}, 1 вҲҲ Erdos304.unitFractionExpressible a b вҶ’ вҲғ m, 1 < m вҲ§ вҶ‘a / вҶ‘b = (вҶ‘m)вҒ»В№
вҲҖ {b : в„•}, Erdos304.unitFractionExpressible 0 b = {0}
(fun b => вҶ‘(Erdos304.smallestCollectionTo b)) =O[Filter.atTop] fun b => Real.log вҶ‘b / Real.log (Real.log вҶ‘b)
problems.docstring:
In 1950, ErdЕ‘s [Er50c] proved the upper bound $$N(b) \ll \log b / \log \log b$$.
[Er50c] ErdЕ‘s, P., Az ${1}/{x_1} + {1}/{x_2} + \ldots + {1}/{x_n} =A/B$ egyenlet eg\'{E}sz sz\'{A}m\'{u} megold\'{A}sairГіl. Mat. Lapok (1950), 192-210.

problems.statement: True вҶ” вҲғ atlas, IsManifold (modelWithCornersSelf в„Ӯ (EuclideanSpace в„Ӯ (Fin 3))) 1 вҶ‘(Mathoverflow1973.unitSphere 6)
problems.docstring:
Does the 6-sphere admit a complex structure, i.e. an atlas of holomorphically compatible charts
relating it to `EuclideanSpace в„Ӯ (Fin 3)`?

problems.docstring:
Is there any polynomial $f(x, y) \in \mathbb{Q}[x, y]$ such that
$f : \mathbb{Q} \times \mathbb{Q} \rightarrow \mathbb{Q}$ is a bijection?

problems.statement: вҲҖ (r : Mathoverflow34145.Rectangle), Mathoverflow34145.lbMeasure r.toSet = ENNReal.ofReal |r.width * r.height|
problems.docstring: The Lebesgue measure of the a rectangle `r` is `r.width * r.height`

вҲҖ (start : в„қ Г— в„қ) (Оё : Real.Angle) (s : Set (в„қ Г— в„қ)),
  Mathoverflow34145.lbMeasure (Mathoverflow34145.rigidMotion start Оё '' s) = Mathoverflow34145.lbMeasure s
problems.docstring: `lbMeasure` is invariant under `rigidMotion start Оё`.

problems.statement:
вҲғ c,
  (вҲҖ (n : в„•), (c.rect n).toSet вҠҶ { width := 133 / 132, height := 133 / 132, start := (0, 0), rotation := 0 }.toSet) вҲ§
    c.IsPacking
problems.docstring:
It is known that packing the rectangles into a square of side length `133/132` is possible.

Reference: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/0097316594901163

problems.statement: Mathoverflow34145.rigidMotion (вҲҡ2, вҲҡ11) вҶ‘(2 * Real.pi / 3) (вҲҡ5, вҲҡ7) = (вҲҡ2 - вҲҡ5 / 2 - вҲҡ21 / 2, -вҲҡ7 / 2 + вҲҡ11 + вҲҡ15 / 2)
True вҶ” вҲғ c, (вҲҖ (n : в„•), (c.rect n).toSet вҠҶ Mathoverflow34145.unitSquare) вҲ§ c.IsPacking
problems.docstring:
Equivalently, can a unit square be packed with rectangles of width `1 / (n + 1)` and height
`1 / (n + 2)`?

problems.statement:

  (вҲҖ (n : в„•), (c.rect n).toSet вҠҶ { width := 501 / 500, height := 501 / 500, start := (0, 0), rotation := 0 }.toSet) вҲ§
